- **`_responded` gate in pairwise-modular / integer-lattice / conditional-gate now requires the engineered MI to clear the permutation-null band by an ABSOLUTE margin (`_MIN_NULL_MARGIN=0.05`), not just `> null_hi`.** Fixes a pre-existing plug-in-MI OVER-FRAGMENTATION false positive surfaced by a few-class y (the ~10-bin quantile-binned regression target, and equally a 10-class classification target): a high-cardinality engineered column (a large modulus residue, an lcm / bitwise op on wide operands) has cardinality-inflated plug-in MI, so the z=3 null band sits just below it and a smooth / noise combiner can squeak over by ~0.007-0.01 nats of pure chance. A TRUE modular / gcd / regime hit clears the null by >1 nat, so the absolute gap floor kills the FP while leaving real hits untouched (verified: 0 FP on smooth regression controls over 3 seeds, all true regression targets still detect with 1.8-7.6x lift). Files: `_pairwise_modular_fe.py`, `_integer_lattice_fe.py`, `_conditional_gate_fe.py` (each `_responded` + a `_MIN_NULL_MARGIN` constant).

- **MRMR orthogonal TRIPLET (3-way) and QUADRUPLET (4-way) cross-basis FE flipped to default ON (`fe_hybrid_orth_triplet_enable` / `fe_hybrid_orth_quadruplet_enable`), AFTER fixing a replay-fidelity P0 in those recipes.** They capture genuine 3-/4-way interactions a LINEAR / additive downstream cannot otherwise form (`y=a*b*c`, `y=x1*x2*x3*x4`); measured on a 3-way `a*b*c` target the linear test MAE drops 0.094 -> 0.049 (the noise floor) once the `a*b*c` feature is available, with NO harm on an additive target (0.0488 unchanged) and negligible fit cost (the `seed_k=4` cap bounds candidates to a handful -- C(4,3)=4 triplets / C(4,4)=1 quadruplet -- regardless of `p`, so no wide-frame blow-up). PREREQUISITE FIX (P0, the blocker for enabling): both recipe families REFIT each leg's basis preprocess (z-score mean/std, min-max lo/hi, laguerre shift) from APPLY-time rows at replay, so a row-slice / drifted test frame shifted the basis axis and silently emitted WRONG feature values -- the same class as the pair "BUG2 FIX (2026-06-12)", never propagated to triplet/quad. Now each leg's fit-time preprocess params are FROZEN into the recipe (captured at the 4 fit-emit sites via `return_params=True`, plumbed through the builders, applied via the frozen path in the two `_apply_*` fns); `None` (legacy pickles) keeps the refit path -> byte-identical. End-to-end invariant fuzz layer (`test_mrmr_endtoend_invariants.py`) green with the flip; 117 existing triplet/quad tests green. Set either flag False to restore the pre-2026-06-13 default-OFF behaviour. Tests: `test_orth_triplet_quad_replay.py` (frozen recipe replays byte-exact full-vs-slice; unfrozen drifts on a slice). Files: `_orthogonal_triplet_fe_recipes.py`, `_orthogonal_quadruplet_fe_recipes.py`, `_orthogonal_triplet_fe.py`, `_orthogonal_quadruplet_fe.py`, `engineered_recipes/__init__.py`, `mrmr/_mrmr_class.py`.

- **MRMR conditional-gate FE cost optimized (`O(p^3)` -> `O(k^2)` relevance-pruned candidate set) and default flipped OFF -> ON (`fe_conditional_gate_enable=True`).** The select sweep was `C(p,2)` operand-pairs x `p` gate-columns x ~17-point tau-scan -- a wide-frame `O(p^3)` cost blow-up (measured +50%..+291% of a full MRMR fit at n=20000) that the column-count budget could not bound, which is why the operator shipped default OFF. The fix is a principled cheap-first candidate prune BEFORE the sweep: a regime switch `c>tau ? a : b` is only useful when the operands a,b carry relevance and the gate column c actually switches the regime, so two cheap one-shot batched-MI rankings restrict the candidate SET (not the gate logic). OPERANDS a,b come from the top-`fe_conditional_gate_k_operand=10` columns by raw binned-MI vs y (the value the switch routes carries marginal relevance; noise operands gate to noise). The GATE column c comes from the top-`fe_conditional_gate_k_gate=8` by a CONDITIONAL-DIVERGENCE rank -- how much a `c>median(c)` split changes the top operands' MI with y -- because on a PURE regime switch the gate column can be marginally INDEPENDENT of y (raw MI ~ 0; measured: the true gate column ranked 28/28 by raw MI on one synthetic seed), so a raw-MI top-k would drop it and miss the signal. The sweep is then `O(k_operand^2 * k_gate * tau-scan)`, FLAT in p: the isolated scan measured ~5.0s -> 7.3s across p in {15..100} at n=20000 (vs ~52s at p=20 unpruned), comparable to the modular/lattice/argmax operators. The tau-scan and the HARDENED best-existing-op gate are unchanged (still 0 FP at p=30 over 3 seeds across smooth/ordinary_mul/random per `_benchmarks/bench_conditional_gate_wideframe.py`), and the gate signal still survives the prune amid 25 noise columns on all 3 seeds. The conditional-gate is now SKIPPED on REGRESSION targets (its MI floor is class-MI; a continuous y cast to int64 becomes ~n classes and the tau-grid + divergence MI explode -- a 600-row regression fit would never complete; guarded via `infer_classification`). The column-count budget guard is kept as a defense-in-depth outer cap (`fe_conditional_gate_max_cols=200`, was 20) that still skips an absurdly wide frame. New ctor params: `fe_conditional_gate_k_gate=8`, `fe_conditional_gate_k_operand=10`. Opt out via `fe_conditional_gate_enable=False` for byte-identical legacy/replay (true no-op: the whole block is skipped, no `gate_` column in `transform()`, selection unchanged). Tests: `tests/feature_selection/test_biz_val_filters_conditional_gate.py` (`TestRelevancePruning` pins signal-survives-prune over 3 seeds + the conditional-divergence gate rank + the flat-in-p `O(k^2)` candidate bound; `test_gate_default_is_on`; `test_gate_skipped_on_regression_target_no_hang`).

- **MRMR row-argmax FE added, default ON (`fe_row_argmax_enable=True`); conditional-gate FE added (default-ON via the cost-optimization entry above).** Two frontier-pass-2 operators the rich catalog cannot express for the MI / linear-downstream selector. ROW-ARGMAX emits, for a column TRIPLE `(a,b,c)`, the integer index `0/1/2` of the row-maximum -- an ordinal / comparison pattern no single shipped column equals (a tree gets the pairwise comparisons for free, but the MI / linear path sees only marginals + pairwise diffs); measured +0.55 single-column MI lift over the best shipped op, ZERO free params. CONDITIONAL-GATE emits a regime switch `c>tau ? a : b` (select) and a masked interaction `1[c>tau]*a` (mask), routing / masking two raw features by a THIRD column's data-dependent threshold `tau` (found by a ~17-point quantile scan, FROZEN in the recipe for exact replay); no shipped op expresses a hard value-selecting switch (`conditional_residual` is a residual, the hinge basis is univariate, `a*c` is a smooth surface), measured +0.55 select / +0.31 mask MI lift. Both detectors are HARDENED: the engineered column's MI must beat the BEST-EXISTING-OP MI on the same operands (max over raw / product / ratio / diff / min / max, batched into one MI-kernel call), NOT just the raw single-operand floor -- the raw-floor prototype gate fired on smooth / ordinary_mul controls (a hard threshold can partly reconstruct an XOR-sign regime) AND a spurious row-argmax cleared the raw floor on an ordinary-multiplicative target at scale; the hardened best-existing-op floor removes both (wide-frame `_benchmarks/bench_conditional_gate_wideframe.py`: ZERO false-positive at p=30 over 3 seeds across smooth + ordinary_mul + random controls, for BOTH operators). Each responded detection becomes a frozen recipe (kind `row_argmax` -- `np.argmax` over the stacked source columns; kind `conditional_gate` -- `np.where(c>tau,a,b)` / `(c>tau)*a` with the frozen `tau`); replay is a pure function of X, no y reference, so transform() is leak-free, deterministic, and train/test bit-identical (including the frozen-`tau` gate replay). Both gated by a cheap-first scan + a DUAL test (best-existing-op margin AND a 12-permutation null upper band) and a column-count BUDGET GUARD that SKIPS the whole sweep (logged) above `fe_row_argmax_max_cols=30` (the `O(C(p,3))` triple sweep) / `fe_conditional_gate_max_cols=200` (the relevance-pruned select sweep). Wide-frame validation: row-argmax adds bounded within-budget cost (added wall-time ~-7%..+14% of a full MRMR fit across p in {15,30} x n in {2000,20000}, near-zero at the common shapes after the best-existing-op MI was batched into one kernel call -- a measured ~3.8x scan speedup, bit-identical) and the real argmax-of-triple signal is still detected+selected amid 25 noise columns over 3 seeds -> row-argmax is measured-safe ON-by-default. The raw-operand guard is preserved (operands restricted to raw input columns; combining onto an already-engineered `pmod_`/`il_`/`argmax_`/`gate_` column would build a nested recipe whose engineered source is unresolvable at replay -> NaN-drop). Opt out / in via the flags for byte-identical legacy / replay (verified true no-op: no `argmax_`/`gate_` column appears in `transform()` and selection is unchanged when OFF). Budget / top-k params: `fe_row_argmax_top_k=4`, `fe_conditional_gate_top_k=4`. Tests: `tests/feature_selection/test_biz_val_filters_conditional_gate.py` (prototype-direct fire/silent + hardened-gate specificity, leak-free bit-identical predict replay incl. frozen-tau, default-ON argmax + gate, opt-out no-op + byte-identical-selection, budget-skip logs, the all-operators-ON no-nested-recipe / no-NaN interaction regression, end-to-end MI / AUC lift) and the prototype `test_biz_val_conditional_gate_fe.py` (kept green).

- **MRMR pairwise integer-lattice FE added, default ON (`fe_integer_lattice_enable=True`).** A new operator (sibling of pairwise-modular) recovers a target that is a function of a hidden COMMON DIVISOR `gcd(a,b)` (shared factor / grid alignment), its dual `lcm(a,b)`, or a bit-level co-occurrence `a & b` of integer columns -- structure smooth bases (poly / Fourier / spline) and the existing arithmetic / modular ops cannot express (gcd is number-theoretic, non-smooth, and non-monotone in either argument: `gcd(6,9)=3` but `gcd(6,10)=2`). bitwise-XOR is EXCLUDED as redundant with the modular residue operator (bench measured its lift at ~0.09); bitwise-OR likewise dropped as a near-mirror of AND with no measured complementary edge -- only the three measured-distinct ops (gcd / lcm / bitwise_and) ship. It is self-limiting: a cheap-first pairs-only scan gated by a DUAL test (the engineered column's MI must beat BOTH operands' raw MI by a margin AND a 12-permutation null upper band), so a non-lattice frame injects ZERO columns. Each responded detection becomes a frozen recipe (kind `pairwise_integer_lattice`); replay is pure integer arithmetic on X (`np.gcd` / `np.lcm` / `np.bitwise_and` on int-cast operands) -- no y reference, so transform() is leak-free, deterministic, and train/test bit-identical. Wide-frame validation (`_benchmarks/bench_integer_lattice_wideframe.py`) measured: 6.97x MI lift on a gcd-shared-factor target with ZERO false-positive on smooth / noise controls; ZERO FP injection at the budget edge p=30 across 3 seeds; the real gcd signal still detected+selected amid 25 noise integer columns across 3 seeds; and bounded added wall-time (within-budget ~+0.3s / +6.7% of a full MRMR fit at p=30, n=2000; ~+1.5s / +7.4% at p=30, n=20000; above max_int_cols=30 the whole pairs-only sweep is SKIPPED, logged, so wide frames pay ~0 added cost by construction). The sweep is pairs-only (`O(C(p,2))`, no n-way analogue: gcd/lcm/AND are binary), strictly cheaper than the modular pairs+triples sweep, so there is no separate triple budget. Budget param `fe_integer_lattice_max_int_cols=30`, `fe_integer_lattice_top_k=4`. Opt out with `fe_integer_lattice_enable=False` for byte-identical legacy / replay (verified true no-op: no `il_` column appears in `transform()` and selection is unchanged). cProfile-optimized: a per-pair `_lattice_columns_for_pair` casts both operands to int once and reuses the gcd for lcm (bit-identical), cutting the per-pair build ~2x. Tests: `tests/feature_selection/test_biz_val_filters_integer_lattice.py` (`TestMRMRIntegration::test_default_on_detects_gcd_signal` pins the new default detects gcd with no flag; `test_opt_out_is_no_op` pins the opt-out no-op; `TestRecipeReplay` pins leak-free bit-identical predict replay; `test_budget_guard_skips_whole_sweep_and_logs` pins the budget skip; `TestBizValue` pins the end-to-end AUC lift).

- **MRMR pairwise/n-way modular FE default flipped OFF -> ON (`fe_pairwise_modular_enable=True`).** The detector recovers a target that is an integer MODULUS of a COMBINATION of columns -- `(a+b) mod m`, `(a*b) mod m`, n-way parity `(a+b+c) mod 2`, or a single column's hidden non-calendar period -- that smooth bases (poly / Fourier) cannot fit (a sawtooth residue needs unbounded harmonics). It is self-limiting: the permutation-null gate responds to nothing on non-modular data, so the only cost on an ordinary frame is the bounded cheap scan and ZERO columns are injected. Wide-frame validation (`_benchmarks/bench_pairwise_modular_wideframe.py`) measured: zero false-positive injection on pure-noise + smooth integer frames at the budget edge p=30 across 3 seeds, the real `(a+b) mod 7` signal still detected+selected amid 25 noise integer columns across 3 seeds, and the added wall-time bounded by the budget guard (within-budget scan ~+1.6s / ~+6% of a full MRMR fit at p=30, n=2000; above max_int_cols=30 the whole sweep is SKIPPED so wide frames pay ~0 added cost by construction). Budget params unchanged (`fe_pairwise_modular_max_int_cols=30`, `fe_pairwise_modular_max_triple_cols=20`). Opt out with `fe_pairwise_modular_enable=False` for byte-identical legacy / replay. Tests: `tests/feature_selection/test_biz_val_filters_pairwise_modular.py` (`TestMRMRIntegration::test_default_on_detects_modular_signal` pins the new default detects `(a+b) mod 7` with no flag; `test_opt_out_is_no_op` pins the opt-out stays a no-op).

### Fixed

- **MRMR conditional-gate FE no longer fires a spurious feature on a multi-driver ADDITIVE target (`best_existing_op_mi` baseline broadened with additive/linear terms).** The gate's hardened best-existing-op floor spanned only the cheap pairwise arithmetic ops (raw / product / ratio / diff / min / max) of the routed operands, so it did not know about the ADDITIVE combination of the involved columns. On a purely additive `y = x0+x1+x2` (no regime structure) a piecewise `c>tau ? a : b` partially reconstructs the additive sum and cleared that floor -- the gate emitted 1 spurious feature on a 10-bin-regression target (and on a 4-driver additive target even for a binary y), violating the gate's "0 FP" specificity contract on the COMMON additive-linear target shape (probe: 3-driver 10-bin `[0,0,1]`, 4-driver binary `[0,0,1]`, 4-driver 10-bin `[1,1,1]`). FIX: `best_existing_op_mi` now also scores the additive/linear baselines on the involved columns -- every pairwise sum (`a+b` / `a+c` / `b+c`) and, for a 3-column operand set, the full sum `a+b+c` -- batched into the SAME single `_mi_classif_batch` call (same binned y, bit-identical machinery). A linear selector captures those sums for free, so a purely-additive target now FAILS the floor while a TRUE regime switch (no additive combo reconstructs the data-dependent branch) still clears it by the existing `_MIN_MARGIN`. Applies to BOTH gate modes (select `c>tau?a:b` over `{a,b,c}` and mask `1[c>tau]*a` over `{a,c}`) and to row-argmax (shared helper; argmax was already clean on additive targets -- 0 emit pre and post -- since argmax is ordinal, not additive-reconstructing, so the extra baseline is harmless there). Measured after the fix: 0 emit on 3-/4-driver additive x binary/10-bin/noisy over 3 seeds each; the true-gate detection lift is UNCHANGED (binary margins 0.040/0.021/0.041, 10-bin margins 1.13/1.11/1.14 identical before/after -- the additive baseline is never the floor max for a true gate); smooth / ordinary_mul / noise controls still 0 FP; classification binary/multiclass selection bit-identical (the floor only ADDS candidates, never reorders the discrete-y path). Files: `_conditional_gate_fe.py` (`best_existing_op_mi`). Tests: `test_biz_val_filters_conditional_gate.py::TestPrototypeDirect::test_gate_silent_on_multidriver_additive_target` (RED on pre-fix: probe emitted 1; GREEN after); the prior "documents the additive limitation" note in `test_gate_specific_on_noise_regression_target_no_hang` updated to point at the now-passing additive sensor. Bench: `bench_conditional_gate_wideframe.py` gains an additive-FP gate row (`_additive_fp_gate`).

- **The four default-ON MRMR class-MI FE operators (pairwise-modular, integer-lattice, row-argmax, conditional-gate) no longer crash / hang / emit garbage on a continuous or 2D y.** All four gate candidates on plug-in class-MI (`_mi_classif_batch`), which treats y as raw discrete labels. On a CONTINUOUS target the int64 cast collapsed to ~n distinct classes -> inflated-garbage MI (a pure-noise column scored >2 nats and the modular operator EMITTED 3 spurious columns on a 600-row regression fit), ballooned cost, even a segfault; the conditional-gate additionally HANGED (its tau-grid x conditional-divergence sweep). On a 2D y (multilabel / multi-target) the kernel could not consume the label matrix and silently returned 0. The shared predicate `class_mi_fe_applicable(y)` guards all four fit-impl blocks. NOTE: the continuous-1D handling was subsequently UPGRADED from skip to a quantile-binned detect path (see the "Changed" entry above) -- continuous 1D y now WORKS; only 2D y stays skipped. BINARY / MULTICLASS paths are UNCHANGED. Files: `_fe_accuracy_gate.py` (`class_mi_fe_applicable`), `_mrmr_fit_impl/_fit_impl_core.py` (4 guarded blocks). Tests: the `TargetTypeRobustness` classes in the 3 operator test files.

- **RFECV / GroupAware(RFECV) hard-crashed on ordinary raw NaN in X -- their default sklearn linear core (`LogisticRegression` / `Ridge`) calls `validate_data`, which raises `ValueError: Input X contains NaN`, so any real-world frame with missing values could not be passed to RFECV at all (`wrappers/rfecv/_nan_policy.py`, `_fit_init.py`, `__init__.py`).** MRMR ingests NaN natively (separate-bin MI + the Layer-37 `is_missing__{col}` emitter); RFECV had no equivalent and crashed. New constructor param `nan_in_X_policy` mirrors MRMR's native-NaN contract with a FRIENDLY default: `"impute"` (NEW default) median-imputes NaN per column on the local working frame at fit entry so the linear core fits, while `"raise"` preserves the strict legacy crash for benchmarks / replay. When the core estimator NATIVELY tolerates NaN (HistGradientBoosting / CatBoost / LightGBM / XGBoost -- detected via the sklearn `allow_nan` input tag plus a known-name allowlist) imputation is SKIPPED and NaN passes through untouched. The impute path is a strict no-op on a NaN-free frame, so non-NaN selection is BYTE-IDENTICAL to before (verified: `impute` and `raise` select the same feature set on NaN-free data). Optional `nan_indicator_cols=` appends `is_missing__{col}` 0/1 indicators built from the PRE-impute mask so a missingness-carried (MNAR) signal stays capturable after the value is imputed, mirroring MRMR's Layer-37 emitter (which is itself opt-in, so indicators default OFF here for cross-selector parity). GroupAware(RFECV) inherits the fix automatically (it wraps RFECV as its inner estimator). RAM-safe: the impute mutates only the sanitised working frame RFECV already owns, never the caller's frame. Closes the former FS GAP in `tests/feature_selection/test_biz_val_mnar_missingness.py` (the strict `xfail` `test_biz_val_rfecv_family_raw_nan_is_fs_gap` is flipped to a passing gap-closed assertion). Tests: `::test_biz_val_rfecv_family_raw_nan_gap_closed` (no crash + MCAR signal recovered), `::test_biz_val_rfecv_raise_policy_still_crashes` (opt-out pin: `raise` reproduces the legacy crash, default does not), `::test_biz_val_rfecv_captures_mnar_via_indicator` (MNAR indicator captured, indicator-alone AUC >= 0.80), `::test_biz_val_rfecv_nan_free_selection_unchanged` (impute is a no-op on NaN-free data).

- **The binary-FE `div` and its `hermite_fe` sibling perturbed EVERY denominator with the divide-by-zero guard epsilon, distorting heavy-tailed ratio features on a LINEAR downstream (`filters/feature_engineering.py::_safe_div`, `filters/hermite_fe/__init__.py::_safe_div`).** The guard `x / (y + sign(y)*eps + eps)` shifted every positive denominator by `2*eps` (and, asymmetrically, left negative ones exact); the hermite form added `eps` to every denominator. The perturbation is negligible for ordinary `y` but reaches `~eps/|y|` relative error as `y -> 0`: on a heavy-tailed ratio target (`y = 0.2*a**2/b`, `b ~ U(0,1)` so `b` can be ~1e-6) the target magnitude is dominated by the smallest-`b` points, and a 0.2% feature error THERE inflates a linear model's test MAE by ~0.05 -- the entire irreducible noise floor (measured: the replayed `div(sqr(a),abs(b))` feature scored 0.10 test MAE vs 0.05 with the exact ratio; at n=80k the usability-aware linear list went 0.1005 -> 0.0517). FIX: divide EXACTLY wherever `y != 0`; substitute `eps` only for an exactly-zero denominator (which the downstream `nan_to_num` scrub maps through +-inf anyway). Recipe replay stays byte-consistent (fit and replay use the same `div`), the 73-cell recipe/replay suite is green, and a small in-process MRMR fit synthesizes `div(sqr(a),abs(b))` with `|Pearson|` 1.0 (exact) against the true `a**2/b`. Regression pin: `tests/feature_selection/test_safe_div_exact_heavy_tail.py` (exactness on nonzero denominators of both signs, finiteness at exact zero, heavy-tail linear floor -- both `_safe_div` implementations; VERIFIED RED on the prior form at ~1e-5 rel err).

- **RFECV admitted a near-exact MONOTONE replica (a scaled / shifted / tiny-noise copy of a real column) into `support_`, splitting the replica's importance across the copies and keeping the redundant decoy (`wrappers/rfecv/_validate.py`).** RFECV's existing fit-entry dedup only removed BIT-EXACT duplicate columns (via `hash_array`), so a `100*x` scaled copy or an `x + 1e-3*eps` near-copy (which differ bit-for-bit but have `|Spearman|` ~1.0) slipped through; on the synthesis/drop matrix the scaled-copy decoy was selected IN PLACE of the original signal column. New default-ON guard `drop_near_dup_corr` (threshold `near_dup_corr_threshold=0.999`, rank-based Spearman so any monotone rescale is caught regardless of slope/offset): at fit entry it drops every column that is `>= 0.999`-rank-correlated with an EARLIER-kept column, keeping the FIRST occurrence. The guard is NARROW BY CONSTRUCTION -- the 0.999 default fires ONLY on a near-perfect monotone replica, so a legitimately-distinct correlated pair (corr ~0.7, even ~0.95 high-VIF cluster mates) sits far below it and BOTH members survive: it CANNOT drop a weak recoverable signal (verified: the two independent drivers of a linear-combo target are both kept; the multicollinear-pollution fixture's 0.913 cluster + 0.897 singular triple are untouched -- reducing a genuine high-VIF cluster to a representative remains the redundancy-aware `GroupAwareMRMR` wrapper's job, `cluster_reduce=True`). Selection is BYTE-IDENTICAL on frames with no near-duplicate (the `corr` scan drops nothing), and the legacy behaviour is opt-out via `drop_near_dup_corr=False`. Closes the `scaled_copy` xfail GAP in `tests/feature_selection/test_biz_val_synthesis_and_drop_matrix.py::test_rfecv_dropping_matrix` (flipped from xfail to a passing decoy-drop assertion). Regression sensor: `::test_rfecv_near_dup_corr_guard` -- the scaled copy is dropped (kept original), the decoy is admitted with the guard OFF (sensor not blind), a corr~0.7 distinct pair is kept in full (the PB-5 safety gate), and both independent drivers of a linear-combo signal survive.

- **MRMR no longer crashes on a raw frame carrying a HIGH-CARDINALITY categorical (an ID / hash / free-text-coded column).** The cat-FE candidate gate (`filters/cat_interactions.py::_select_candidate_indices`) raised a hard `ValueError` for any categorical whose `nbins` exceeds the `sqrt(n)*2` safety ceiling, so a routine real-world frame with an id-like column could not be passed to `MRMR.fit` directly. New `CatFEConfig.on_high_cardinality` field, defaulting to `"skip"` (correct-by-default): such a column is dropped from the cat-FE interaction pool with a one-time `UserWarning` and flows through the ordinary MRMR relevance screen, which still drops it as noise. The low-card categorical SIGNAL is unaffected and still selected. Selection is BYTE-IDENTICAL on frames without a high-card categorical (verified vs HEAD across 5 seeds). The legacy hard error remains available opt-in via `CatFEConfig(on_high_cardinality="raise")`. Tests: `tests/feature_selection/test_biz_val_mixed_feature_types.py` (`test_biz_val_mrmr_drops_highcard_cat_noise_gracefully` flipped from xfail to a passing assertion that fit completes + `cat_noise` dropped + `cat_sig` kept; `test_biz_val_mrmr_highcard_cat_raises_when_opted_in` pins the opt-in raise) and unit-level skip/raise pins in `test_cat_interactions.py` + `test_cat_interactions_coverage.py`. Also fixed an unrelated pre-existing brittle fixture in `test_biz_val_filters_mrmr.py::test_biz_val_mrmr_sample_weight_flips_top_feature_under_recency_vs_uniform` (recent slice was too large at `n//3`, so uniform weighting already favoured A and the recency contrast collapsed; `n//5` restores uniform->B vs recency->A).
- **Grouped aggregation over quantile-binned NUMERIC cells** (`filters/_binned_numeric_agg_fe.py`, recipe kind `binned_numeric_agg`, `MRMR.fe_binned_numeric_agg_*`). The existing `_grouped_agg_fe` groups by low-cardinality CATEGORICAL columns; this sibling forms the group key by QUANTILE-BINNING a numeric column and emits the per-cell `mean`/`std`/`skew`/`kurt` of every other numeric as leak-safe `binagg_{stat}(num|qbin(group))` features. Captures the regime where the WITHIN-CELL SPREAD / SHAPE of a feature carries signal the cell mean cannot -- a heteroscedastic target whose variance, not mean, depends on the cell (measured **+0.9 OOS R2** on a `sigma(cell)` target where the cell mean is ~constant; +0.3 in-MRMR multistat-vs-mean biz_value). Design is measurement-backed (`_benchmarks/bench_cell_binning_for_moments`, `bench_richer_cell_panel`): (1) UNSUPERVISED quantile binning, NOT MRMR's supervised MI binning -- supervised cell edges built from y leak y into a feature aggregated to predict y, and MDLP gives uneven cells (tiny cells -> garbage higher moments); equal-frequency cells give uniform per-cell n. (2) bin count = `min(fe_binned_numeric_agg_nbins, moment_stability_cap)` where the cap = `floor((n / n_min(highest_moment))^(1/k))` ties resolution to the highest requested moment (mean ~5, std ~12, skew ~30, kurt ~100 rows/cell) -- **Freedman-Diaconis REJECTED** (over-bins at large n: 16->25 bins and DEGRADES 0.846->0.841, it optimises 1-D density not per-cell occupancy). (3) HIGH-MOMENT AUTO-DROP: when the cap forces fewer bins than the base (small n / high moment), the high-order moments whose per-cell floor cannot be met are dropped rather than coarsening every column. (4) per-cell stats vectorised with `np.bincount` raw moments (O(n)); the full 53-stat `compute_numaggs` panel was benched and **REJECTED** as default (+0.002..+0.012 R2 for 46-2088x the cost -- the lean 4-moment set captures ~all the value). Edges stored per group column for leak-safe `searchsorted` replay; NaN-bearing columns skipped in v1. Default OFF (`fe_binned_numeric_agg_enable=False`). Tests: `tests/feature_selection/test_binned_numeric_agg_fe.py` (6: moment-aware resolution + auto-drop, bincount==numpy, leak-safe replay incl. out-of-range, std recovers cell spread, biz_value spread recovery, MRMR fit/transform integration + off-gate).

- **`CatFEConfig.include_numeric` is now functional** (`filters/_cat_interactions_step.py`, `filters/cat_fe_state.py`, `filters/_mrmr_fit_impl/_fit_impl_core.py`). The flag was previously declared + documented but DEAD: `_select_candidate_indices` only iterated `categorical_vars`, and the call-site gate `len(categorical_vars) >= 2` blocked pure-numeric frames, so no numeric column ever entered the cat-FE candidate pool. Now, when `True`, eligible NaN-free RAW numeric columns are quantile-binned (`numeric_nbins`, capped at `floor(sqrt(max_combined_nbins))` so a numeric x numeric pair fits the data-aware cardinality budget) into a transient working pool and their pair/k-way crosses are searched by the existing machinery. Captures axis-aligned AND non-product (diagonal / rotated) interactions the numeric unary/binary FE cannot express: isolated honest-OOS bench (`_benchmarks/bench_numeric_cat_te_feedforward.py`, LogisticRegression downstream) measured **+0.42 AUC on a rotated quadrant target where `mul(a,b)` gives +0.00**, and +0.013 on an axis-aligned one. LEAK-SAFE transform replay: the per-column quantile edges are stamped into each recipe (`src_bin_edges`) and `_coerce_to_int_with_nan_handling` bins raw test values through them (`searchsorted`) instead of int-casting -- no train/serve skew. Two bugs fixed in the same change: (1) `include_numeric` no longer crosses pre-FE ENGINEERED numeric columns (e.g. `x__haar_j3k3`), which are absent from the raw frame at transform and produced a silent NaN-drop -- restricted to `feature_names_in_`; (2) `numeric_nbins` is capped to the cardinality budget so numeric pairs are not all rejected by the per-pair `nb_prod > max_combined` gate (the prior behaviour silently produced nothing). NaN-bearing numeric columns are skipped in v1 (the quantile-edge replay has no NaN bin). Default `False` (opt-in: noisy floats can alias). Tests: `tests/feature_selection/test_include_numeric_cat_fe.py` (5: cross built with stored edges, leak-safe replay vs independent edge recomputation, NaN-skip, off-gate, step-level biz_value rotated lift).

- **Multi-statistic K-fold target encoding** (`filters/_target_encoding_fe.py`, `filters/mrmr/_mrmr_class.py`). Beyond the per-category `mean(y)`, the encoder now emits the within-category `std` / `skew` / `kurt` of `y` as separate leak-safe `{col}__te_{stat}` columns (one OOF column + `kfold_target_encoded` recipe per stat; a stat column is structurally just a different per-category lookup, so the replay path is unchanged). These carry signal the mean cannot when the category MODULATES a raw feature: on a cell-varying-slope regression `y = a(cell) + b(cell)*x_raw + noise`, `mean(y|cell) ~ a(cell)` (the slope averages out) while `std(y|cell) ~ |b(cell)|`, so fed alongside `x_raw` to a GBM they recover the `b(cell)*x_raw` interaction -- honest-OOS bench (`_benchmarks/bench_multistat_cell_encoding.py`) measured **+0.04..+0.09 R2** in varying-slope regimes, and correctly **~0** (the moments are deterministic functions of the mean) for binary or pure-mean-shift targets, where the MI screen drops them as redundant. The per-category computation is now vectorised with `np.bincount` raw-moment accumulation (replaces the prior per-row Python loops -- O(n) per fold). **`MRMR.fe_kfold_te_stats` default flipped `("mean",)` -> `("mean","std","skew","kurt")`** per the accuracy-first variant-default rule (win where it helps, harmless+screened-out elsewhere); pass `("mean",)` to restore the lean single-stat encoder. Tests: `tests/feature_selection/test_multistat_target_encoding.py` (5: mean-only back-compat, one column+recipe per stat, std recovers within-cell spread, leak-safe deterministic replay incl. unseen category, varying-slope biz_value lift). REJECTED-not-built (documented with numbers): a Hermite/orth-poly basis ON TOP of a target-encoded column adds +0.0004 / -0.0008 (TE is already `E[y|cell]`, so a basis warp is a structural no-op).

### Changed

- **`DummyBaselinesConfig.bootstrap_ci_n_resamples` flipped 1000 -> 2000** (`training/configs.py`, drives the percentile 95% CI in `_compute_bootstrap_ci` / `_bootstrap_ci_for_strongest`, fired only at small n `< bootstrap_ci_threshold=2000`). A percentile-bootstrap CI bound is itself a Monte-Carlo estimate whose only n_resamples-dependent error is the seed-to-seed jitter of the 2.5/97.5 percentile of the resample distribution (the data-sampling error is fixed by n). That jitter scales ~1/sqrt(B). Isolated multi-scenario bench (`training/baselines/_benchmarks/bench_bootstrap_ci_n_resamples.py`, 5 scenarios x 3 data-seeds = 15 cells x 40 bootstrap-seeds; honest metric = scale-free RMS of CI-bound jitter+bias vs a 50k-resample reference bound, as a fraction of the CI half-width): B=2000 wins all 15/15 cells and is materially (>=10%) better than B=1000 in 15/15, cutting mean error from 0.0425 to 0.0302 of half-width (~29% lower). The numba kernels make the cost ~+0.9ms once per small-n target. Per the accuracy-first variant-default rule, the default flips. biz_value pin: `tests/training/baselines/test_biz_val_bootstrap_ci_n_resamples.py` (constant==2000 + B=2000 CI jitter < B=1000 by >=20%; fails on pre-flip 1000). REJECTED-not-DELETED: B<1000 (200/500) is monotonically worse and B stays a config knob; bench kept for re-test on other hardware.

- **`DEFAULT_ECE_NBINS` flipped 15 -> 10** (`calibration/policy.py`, drives the OOF ECE benchmark + bootstrap CI in `pick_best_calibrator` and the default in `_ece_score`). Equal-width ECE is biased upward when over-binned: each per-bin `|mean_y - mean_p|` absorbs sampling noise that does not cancel under the absolute value, so 15 bins systematically over-states miscalibration at small/medium n. Isolated multi-scenario bench (`calibration/_benchmarks/bench_ece_nbins.py`, 6 calibration-map scenarios x 3 sample sizes = 18 cells x 12 seeds; honest metric = RMSE of the binned ECE vs a 200k-grid ground-truth `E_p|g(p)-p|`): nbins=10 beats nbins=15 in 14/18 head-to-head cells and has the lowest mean RMSE (0.0097 vs 0.0109 = ~11% lower); nbins=15 is the second-worst over-binning floor. nbins>=20 monotonically worse (mean RMSE 0.0123/0.0151/0.0199). Per the accuracy-first variant-default rule, the default flips. biz_value pin: `tests/calibration/test_biz_val_policy_ece_nbins.py` (constant==10 + aggregate ECE-RMSE at default < nbins=15 by >=3%; fails on pre-flip 15). REJECTED-not-DELETED: nbins=5 wins the noise-floor-dominated cells (perfect-cal, piecewise) but loses the genuinely-miscalibrated shapes; bench kept for re-test.

- **`DEFAULT_CALIBRATION_CV_FOLDS` flipped 3 -> 7** (`training/models.py`, used by `_make_calibrated_classifier_cv` with isotonic calibration). Isotonic calibration is data-hungry: with k folds each calibrator fits the base model on (k-1)/k of train and learns the isotonic map on the held-out 1/k, so more folds cut base-model under-fit and average more calibrators. Multi-scenario honest-holdout bench (`_benchmarks/bench_calibration_cv_folds.py`, 5 scenarios x 4 seeds = 20 cells; Brier/log-loss/AUC on a TEST split never used for fit or calibration): k=5 wins 15/20 cells, mean Brier 0.11116 vs 0.11350, mean log-loss 0.35852 vs 0.36250; AUC unchanged (rank quality is calibration-invariant). The win concentrates on the data-hungry scenarios (small_n, high_dim_noisy, imbalanced); near-linear logreg is the only mixed case, and k=5 still wins the majority overall. Per the variant-defaults accuracy-first rule (majority of scenarios+seeds on the honest metric), the default flips. biz_value pin: `tests/training/test_biz_val_calibration_cv_folds.py` (constant==5 + k=5 mean honest Brier < k=3; VERIFIED delta b3-b5 ~0.0032).

### Fixed

- **Predict-time pre_pipeline recovery no longer silently serves RAW (un-transformed) features when the failed pre_pipeline VALUE-TRANSFORMS them (`training/core/_predict_pre_pipeline.py`, `_apply_pre_pipeline_with_passthrough`).** On a `pre_pipeline.transform` failure the recovery subsets the raw predict frame to the inner model's `feature_names_in_` and predicts. That is value-correct ONLY for a pure name-keyed column selector (MRMR / RFECV / SelectKBest), whose transform is "select these columns by name". For a value-altering pre_pipeline (StandardScaler / imputer / encoder / PCA / engineered features) the raw subset is NOT the fit-time input -- the model was trained on the transformed space -- so the prior WARN-and-serve path shipped silently-wrong predictions as if correct. FIX: gate the subset-recovery on a new `_pre_pipeline_is_pure_selector` predicate (selector exposes `feature_names_in_` + `support_`/`get_support`, and any wrapping Pipeline has no value-altering step); for a value-transformer, WARN + re-raise so the per-model handler drops the model (clean, announced degrade) instead of producing nonsense. The legitimate selector recovery (iter-59 clone-not-refit MRMR) is untouched. Regression: `tests/training/test_predict_pre_pipeline_value_transform_recovery.py` (broken StandardScaler model dropped while a sibling broken-SelectKBest model survives with predictions == `inner.predict(raw)`; fails on pre-fix code where the scaler model is kept and served unscaled input).

- **MRMR `transform()` delivered every engineered `cluster_aggregate` feature as its internal MI QUANTILE CODE instead of its continuous value -- the same magnitude-stripping bug class as the `unary_binary` fix below (`engineered_recipes/_recipe_poly_cluster.py`).** `_apply_cluster_aggregate` discretised its replay output (`Z @ weights` over the cluster's standardised members) to a ~10-level quantile code, so a downstream LINEAR model saw only the RANK of the cluster aggregate, not its magnitude. Measured on a target linear in a mean-z cluster aggregate: a linear model scored test-R2 0.936 on the 10-bin code (Pearson 0.967 with the true aggregate) vs **0.99972** on the continuous value (Pearson 1.000). FIX: return the continuous aggregate and never discretise at replay -- mirroring the `unary_binary` / `prewarp` / `hermite_pair` siblings. `recipe.quantization` is kept for provenance; the downstream MRMR fit still discretises the fit-time column for its own MI matrix via `_mrmr_fe_step`. Continuous replay also subsumes the Wave-9.1 iter-29 quantile-edge leak fix (the output is a closed-form function of the row given the frozen `member_mean`/`member_std`/`signs`/`weights`, so an identical physical row maps to an identical value regardless of test-distribution drift). Files: `engineered_recipes/_recipe_poly_cluster.py` (continuous replay; `discretize_array` block removed from `_apply_cluster_aggregate`). Tests re-framed to the continuous/drift-invariant contract (no mask / no xfail): `tests/feature_selection/test_cluster_aggregate_quantile_edges.py` (continuous-not-quantized, identical-row-same-value-under-drift, legacy-no-edges-also-continuous-and-warning-free) and `tests/feature_selection/test_cluster_aggregate.py::test_fit_binned_column_matches_binned_apply_recipe_replay` (the fit-time fast-path code must equal the continuous replay re-binned with the recipe's FROZEN edges -- preserving the no-FP-drift invariant). Full cluster_aggregate suite (101 cells) green.

- **MRMR `transform()` delivered every engineered `unary_binary` feature as its internal MI QUANTILE CODE (a ~10-level integer) instead of its continuous value, so a downstream LINEAR model could not use the synthesized feature's MAGNITUDE -- collapsing R^2 on any target whose signal rides in that magnitude (`engineered_recipes/_recipe_unary_binary.py`).** On the heavy-tailed regression target `y = 0.2*a**2/b + f/5 + log(c*2)*sin(d/3)` (`f` hidden), `Var(0.2*a**2/b)` is ~1367 vs `Var(log*sin)` ~0.04 and `Var(f/5)` ~0.003 -- the `a**2/b` term is 99.9997% of `Var(y)`, so the entire achievable R^2 lives in capturing `a**2/b`. MRMR DID synthesize it correctly (`div(sqr(a),abs(b))` = `a**2/b`), but `_apply_unary_binary` then discretised the replay output to a 10-bin quantile code: the code's Pearson correlation with the true `a**2/b` was 0.03 (rank-corr 0.99 -- ORDER preserved, MAGNITUDE destroyed), and a closed-form-Ridge linear model on the code scored test-R2 ~0.002 (its predictions capped near 2.7 while the heavy-tailed target reaches ~3133). The quantisation was an internal-MI artifact leaking into the model-facing output; raw columns were always passed continuous, only engineered ones were binned. FIX: `_apply_unary_binary` now returns the CONTINUOUS engineered value and never discretises at replay -- generalising the behaviour the `prewarp` / `hermite_pair` recipe siblings already had (their builders set `quantization=None` for the same measured reason: binning a heavy-tailed product keeps only rank and throws away correlation with the target). `recipe.quantization` is left populated for provenance/audit only; the downstream MRMR fit still discretises the fit-time column for its OWN MI matrix via `_mrmr_fe_step` (a separate path, unaffected -- this is a replay-only change). Continuous replay also SUBSUMES the Wave-9.1 iter-28 quantile-edge leak fix: the output is now a pure element-wise (closed-form) function of the operand row, so an identical physical row maps to an identical value regardless of test-distribution drift -- there is no quantile recomputation left to drift. RESULT on the user's EXACT df (`np.random.seed(0)`, n=100000): `train_mlframe_models_suite(mlframe_models=["linear"])` with MRMR feature selection lifts test-R2 from ~0.002 to **0.99998**; the engineered `div(sqr(a),abs(b))` column comes out of `transform()` as continuous float (100k distinct values, |Pearson| 1.00 with `a**2/b`) instead of a 10-bin int code. (`cluster_aggregate` has the same replay-time-quantisation shape and is a candidate for the identical treatment, but it has a distinct downstream contract + dedicated quantile-edge tests, so it is deferred to a separately-measured change rather than folded in here.) Files: `engineered_recipes/_recipe_unary_binary.py` (continuous replay; the `discretize_array` import + quantisation block removed from `_apply_unary_binary`). Tests: NEW `tests/feature_selection/test_biz_value_mrmr_continuous_engineered_linear_r2.py` -- a deterministic regression pin (the synthesized pair-FE column must be continuous: high cardinality, floating dtype, |Pearson| ~1 against the true `a**2/b`, never a low-cardinality int code) + the biz-value proof (`train_mlframe_models_suite` linear test-R2 >= 0.99 on CASE2 via MRMR; VERIFIED ~0.002 pre-fix, 0.99998 post-fix). Re-framed (replay no longer quantises, so the superseded mechanism's pins assert the stronger continuous + drift-invariant contract, NO mask / NO xfail): `tests/feature_selection/test_unary_binary_quantile_edges.py` (4 cells: continuous-not-quantized output, identical-row-same-value-under-drift, legacy-no-edges-also-continuous-and-warning-free, edges-still-built-as-provenance) and `tests/feature_selection/test_engineered_recipes.py::TestApplyRecipeUnaryBinary::test_replay_is_continuous_even_when_quantization_recorded`. The byte-exact slice-replay adversarial battery (`test_f2_adversarial_battery.py`, 35 cells) stays GREEN -- continuous replay is slice-consistent.

- **MRMR raw-redundancy drop KEPT a fully-subsumed dominant raw operand whenever it was selected ALONGSIDE a FUSED full-target composite, because the verdict conditioned the raw on the WHOLE fused composite instead of the clean raw-containing sub-expression nested inside it (REAL BUG1, robust fix; the 65d18475 fix only repaired the never-empty collapse path).** On the user's EXACT df `y = a**2/b + f/5 + log(c)*sin(d)` the `fe_max_steps=2` search builds, on most seeds, ONE fused composite e.g. `add(div(log(c),reciproc(d)),abs(div(sqr(a),abs(b))))` that combines BOTH independent signal terms (the `a**2/b` ratio AND the `log(c)*sin(d)` product) into a single feature. When the whole selection collapses into that recipe-only composite the never-empty-raw path (fixed in 65d18475) correctly drops `a`; but on the seeds where the dominant operand `a` is selected ALONGSIDE the composite, the MAIN `drop_redundant_raw_operands` path ran and conditioned `a` on the WHOLE fused composite's continuous values. Because the composite fuses `a**2/b` with `log(c)*sin(d)`, the second term acts as nuisance variation across the conditioning strata, so `CMI(a; y | fused_composite)` retained a spurious residual and `a` was wrongly KEPT -- the seed-dependent failure the user reported (`a` kept on the non-collapse seeds, e.g. seed 4 at n=40000: `['a', 'add(div(log(c),reciproc(d)),abs(div(sqr(a),abs(b))))']`). The fragility was masked when running several fits in one interpreter because `MRMR.fit` consumes global `np.random` state during fitting, so which composite a seed selects depends on prior fits in the same process -- the prior single-seed pin landed on a lucky ordering. FIX (NESTED-OPERAND CONSUMER DETECTION): `drop_redundant_raw_operands` now walks each consuming survivor's recipe OPERAND TREE (the `EngineeredRecipe` `nested_parent_a/b` dataclass structure, NOT `str(name)`) for the CLEANEST raw-containing sub-expression -- the tightest sub-recipe that still contains the raw plus a SECOND signal-bearing raw (a true combination, passing the DPI-trap filter) and that is a PROPER sub-expression of the fused survivor (fewer signal-bearing parents). That sub-expression (`div(sqr(a),abs(b))` = a**2/b on the user fixture) is replayed to its own CONTINUOUS values via `apply_recipe` (quantization stripped) and the raw is conditioned on THAT isolated sub-expression instead of the fused whole. A fully-subsumed operand's conditional excess then collapses (`CMI(a; y | div(sqr(a),abs(b)))` ~0.7% retention, below the `RAW_SELF_RETAIN_FRAC`=0.05 bar) -> DROP; a GENUINE private term (e.g. `y += 10*a`) keeps a large residual given the same clean sub-expression -> KEEP (over-drop control). Any failure to parse/replay falls back to the fused-composite bin (the conservative KEEP direction). New optional kwargs `recipes` (name -> EngineeredRecipe) and `raw_X` are threaded from both the main and never-empty call sites in `_fit_impl_core.py`; defaulting to `None` keeps every existing caller and the 6 helper-level unit tests unchanged. RESULT on the user's EXACT df at n=40000, each seed in a FRESH process (the user's real invocation): raw `a` DROPPED on ALL of seeds 0/1/2/3/4 (was kept on the non-collapse seeds pre-fix), and the private-raw control (`y += 10*a`) KEEPS `a` on ALL 5. Files: `filters/_fe_raw_redundancy_drop.py` (recipe-tree walk `_recipe_subexprs` + sub-expression replay `_subexpr_continuous` + per-consumer clean-sub-expression conditioning), `filters/_mrmr_fit_impl/_fit_impl_core.py` (pass `recipes`/`raw_X` at the main + never-empty call sites). Tests: `tests/feature_selection/test_biz_value_mrmr_fe_canonical.py::test_user_case_drops_redundant_raw_a_multi_seed` (5 seeds, fresh-subprocess-per-seed so the in-process RNG contamination cannot mask the bug; VERIFIED RED pre-fix at seed 4 -- `a` kept -- and GREEN post-fix on all 5) + `::test_private_raw_a_kept_alongside_engineered_multi_seed` (5-seed over-drop control, `a` KEPT on all). The fresh-subprocess isolation is the fix for the recurring single-seed-validation failure mode.

- **MRMR: a cross-fold-stability-vote-rejected engineered feature could re-enter `support_` / `get_feature_names_out` without a replayable recipe and be silently dropped from `transform()` output (`filters/_mrmr_fe_step/_step_core.py`, `filters/_mrmr_fit_impl/_fit_impl_core.py`).** At `fe_max_steps=2` the FE step's cross-fold stability vote pops a fold-unstable engineered recipe AND de-selects its column for that step, but the materialised bin-code column stays in `cols`/`data`, so the downstream greedy MRMR screen (the step>1 re-screen / final selection) re-admitted it on its marginal MI. It then arrived at the fit-end selection finaliser with NO recipe and was silently DROPPED from transform output -- a select-then-drop contract violation (a feature advertised in `support_` / discovered must survive `transform`). On the user's uniform `y = 0.2*a**2/b + f/5 + log(c*2)*sin(d/3)` repro this fired `"MRMR.fit: 1 engineered feature(s) selected without replayable recipe ..."` and lost `add(qubed(a),rint(d))` from the output roster. The vote is now authoritative: vote-rejected engineered names are recorded on `self._fe_stability_vote_dropped_` and stripped from the selection BEFORE `support_`/discovered is finalised, so a fold-unstable feature can never re-enter without a recipe. (Distinct from the earlier b6627f85, which froze axis params for EXISTING recipes; this closes the MISSING-recipe re-admission gap.)

### Tests

- **End-to-end `train_mlframe_models_suite` FE-recovery tests (`tests/feature_selection/test_suite_fe_linear_recovery.py` + shared scaffold `_suite_fe_helpers.py` + subprocess worker `_suite_fe_worker.py`).** The library's MAIN entry point had no parametrized test asserting that FE delivers a downstream-USABLE feature space to a LINEAR model on a magnitude-carrying target -- exactly the gap the quantized-transform-output bug fell through. The scaffold provides 5 magnitude-carrying target generators (heavy-tail ratio `a**2/b`, bilinear `a*b`, polynomial, trig product, cluster aggregate) x distribution, a `run_suite` helper, `best_test_metric`, and `prediction_span_fraction` (the sharp guard against the capped-prediction signature the bug produced). Asserts test-R2 floors AND prediction-span floors for linear/ridge + MRMR, plus an FE-uplift leg (linear+FE >> raw-only-linear on a polynomial). Each suite fit runs in a fresh subprocess (two heavy-tail n=100k fits in one process intermittently corrupt downstream global state -- same isolation the invariant layer already uses). 7 passed.

- **Hardcoded-threshold audit of the MRMR subsystem (`tests/feature_selection/MRMR_HARDCODED_THRESHOLDS_AUDIT.md`).** A read-only audit cataloguing 31 magic numeric constants that gate STATISTICAL decisions (MI/prevalence ratios, retention fractions, pairness/uplift margins, self-ratios) and could instead be DATA-DERIVED via permutation nulls / cross-val -- grouped HIGH(5)/MED(9)/LOW(12), each with file:line, what it gates, the n/data-dependency rationale, and a suggested permutation-null replacement referencing an existing primitive (`compute_pair_maxt_floor` / `_conditional_perm_null`). Top HIGH items: `fe_min_pair_mi_prevalence=1.05`, `fe_synergy_min_prevalence=1.5`, `_FE_MARGINAL_UPLIFT_MIN_RATIO=1.30`, `fe_escalation_pairness_margin=1.15`, `fe_escalation_underdelivery_self_ratio=3.0`.

### Added (experimental, default OFF)

- **MRMR usability-aware MULTI-LIST feature selection (`usability_aware_lists`, default OFF; exposes `support_linear_` / `support_universal_` / `support_nonlinear_` + `transform_usability(X, which=...)`).** MRMR's pure-MI `support_` is the right objective for trees (they build interactions internally) but NOT for a LINEAR / additive downstream: MI is rank-based and blind to linear usability, so on F2 it selects raw `c`, `d` and `a**2/b` but NO `c*d` interaction, leaving a linear model at ~0.096 test MAE (it cannot form `c*d` from raw `c,d`). With the flag ON, after the pure-MI fit the estimator runs a SECOND pass (`_usability_lists` -> `select_usability_aware_features`) over a fresh replayable candidate pool whose relevance blends MI with the held-out partial correlation of each candidate with the RESIDUAL after the already-selected features (the residual escapes the `a**2/b` heavy tail that swamps a raw-y/R^2 usability signal). It stores TWO additional selections -- `support_linear_` (`usability_w_linear`, default 0.85, `w->1`) and `support_universal_` (`usability_w_universal`, default 0.5, blend) -- each a list of replayable `UsableCandidate`s (raw passthrough or `EngineeredRecipe`), plus `support_nonlinear_` as an alias of `support_`. `transform_usability(X, which='linear'|'universal'|'nonlinear')` materialises the exact feature space the greedy scored. MEASURED on F2: a linear model on `transform_usability(X, 'linear')` reaches ~0.05-0.06 test MAE (the `f/5` floor) selecting genuine `mul(log(c),...)`-shaped `(c,d)` forms, vs 0.096 for the pure-MI list. DEFAULT OFF: the CV-MAE forward selection it runs costs seconds-to-minutes (a `PERF TODO` marks the inner loop for an incremental-Gram solver) and must not be charged to every fit; the suite turns it on and routes linear/additive models to the linear list. `support_` is NEVER touched -- byte-identical whether the pass is on or off (verified). Continuous engineered values are stored at `usability_feature_dtype` (default float32); `usability_max_base_features` (default 16) caps the pair enumeration on wide inputs by marginal MI. Files: `_usability_lists.py` (wiring + `materialize_usability_features`), `_usability_aware_selection.py` (the standalone selector, committed earlier), `mrmr/_mrmr_class.py` (7 knobs + `transform_usability`), `_mrmr_fit_impl/_fit_impl_core.py` (gated post-pass after `support_`). Tests: `tests/feature_selection/test_mrmr_usability_lists.py` (end-to-end: linear floor + byte-identical `support_` + replayable lists) and `tests/feature_selection/test_usability_aware_selection.py` (the standalone selector's floor + (c,d) + slice-consistent replay).

- **`train_mlframe_models_suite` usability routing via the materialised UNION (`feature_selection_config.mrmr_usability_aware_lists`, default OFF).** Per-model feature-space routing in the suite is intrusive (it materialises ONE transformed matrix shared across all models). Instead, when the usability pass is on, `MRMR.transform` now materialises the UNION of all three lists -- pure-MI (`support_`) + `support_linear_` + `support_universal_`, deduped by name -- and records a `usability_feature_groups_` map of which emitted column belongs to which list. The union puts the engineered interaction in EVERY model's input, so a LINEAR model on the suite's shared matrix simply assigns it a coefficient and reaches the floor, while a tree ignores the columns it does not split on (and can optionally subset to `support_` via the groups map) -- no per-model re-transform. `get_feature_names_out` is kept consistent with the wider transform (single source of truth `_usability_union_extra`) so the sklearn-Pipeline width check passes. The suite flag `mrmr_usability_aware_lists` injects `usability_aware_lists=True` into the MRMR (an explicit `mrmr_kwargs` entry wins; flag off leaves the path byte-identical). MEASURED end-to-end on F2 (n=14k, single-process fit): `train_mlframe_models_suite(mlframe_models=["linear"])` test MAE 0.099 (pure-MI) -> 0.060 (union), approaching the `f/5` floor. Note: the suite caches fitted pipelines by `id(df)` + content, so an OFF-then-ON pair in ONE process can reuse the OFF imputer (fit on the narrower columns) against the wider union -- the goal test isolates a single fit per process, as the existing suite-FE tests do. Files: `mrmr/_mrmr_class.py` (`transform` union + `_append_usability_union` + `_usability_union_extra` + `get_feature_names_out` union + `usability_feature_groups_`), `_feature_selection_config.py` (`mrmr_usability_aware_lists`), `core/_phase_config_setup.py` (inject into `mrmr_kwargs`). Test: `tests/feature_selection/test_suite_mrmr_usability_goal.py` (suite linear test MAE <= 0.075 on F2 with the flag on).

- **Per-pair MULTI-CANDIDATE diverse emission for the unary/binary FE search (`fe_multi_emit_max_per_pair` / `fe_multi_emit_mi_floor` / `fe_multi_emit_diversity_corr`, default 1 = legacy one-per-pair).** The pair search emits only the SINGLE max-target-MI engineered form per raw pair. MI is a RANK statistic blind to LINEAR usability: on F2 `log(2c)*sin(d/3)` the MI-winner `sub(exp(c),cbrt(d))` (MI 0.288) helps a LINEAR downstream by ~0 (closed-form MAE 0.092->0.093) while the lower-MI `mul(log(c),sin(d))` (MI 0.264, INSIDE the existing 0.85 leaders band) is the linearly-aligned additive term and cuts MAE 0.092->0.063. With `fe_multi_emit_max_per_pair > 1` the search ALSO emits the next DISTINCT forms (greedy by target MI, skipping any whose continuous values correlate above `fe_multi_emit_diversity_corr` with an already-emitted column, down to `fe_multi_emit_mi_floor` x best_mi); the downstream MRMR redundancy gate prunes residual overlap. Purely additive; ==1 is byte-identical to the legacy path (verified: default CASE2 unchanged, synergy-prevalence-rescue tests green). DEFAULT OFF pending a multi-task downstream bench: MEASURED on F2 it emits the diverse forms per pair but the cross-pair MRMR GREEDY still selects high-MI cross-mix `esc_poly_*(a,c)/(b,c)` over the linearly-usable `mul(log(c),sin(d))` (the MI-vs-linear-usability blindness is also in the greedy, not only per-pair emission), so it does not on its own reach the `linear MAE ~0.05` goal and it adds candidate-buffer cost. Files: `_feature_engineering_pairs/_pairs_core.py` (diverse-emit block + 3 kwargs), `_mrmr_fe_step/_step_core.py` (thread the knobs at both call sites), `mrmr/_mrmr_class.py` (3 knobs).

- **Two opt-in FE-pair admission knobs (`fe_pair_perm_null_admission_enable`, `fe_prevalence_rescue_all_pairs`) -- MEASURED to NOT improve downstream, kept as research knobs.** Investigation of the F2 `log(2c)*sin(d/3)` (c,d) term: the joint feature genuinely exists (oracle `mul(log2c,sin(d/3))` nearly HALVES closed-form-linear MAE, 0.092->0.050, capturing all but the irreducible `f/5` noise floor ~0.05), but the current FE cannot synthesize it -- `_propose_poly(c,d)` returns None at every degree AND pairness margin (the orth-poly ALS cannot represent `log(c)`'s c->0 singularity; representational gap, not a threshold). `fe_pair_perm_null_admission_enable` (conditional-permutation-null admission, cancels the MM-debias bias the hardcoded ratio over-penalises) DOES admit the genuine (c,d) pair but cannot separate it from an additive cross-mix `(a,c)` (CMI sees both), so the FE builds cross-mix composites that HURT: closed-form-linear MAE 0.092 (master) -> 0.097 (fe1) -> 0.868 (fe2 step-2 fusion). `fe_prevalence_rescue_all_pairs` (route to the escalation pairness test) is a no-op on F2 (the escalation produces no (c,d) candidate). Both default OFF; the measured verdicts are documented at each knob. A clean (c,d) win needs a NEW log/power-warp separable-product proposer (continuous held-out scoring, terminal) -- not a threshold change. Default MRMR behaviour is byte-unchanged (synergy-prevalence-rescue regression tests green).

- **End-to-end MRMR/FE invariant fuzz layer (`tests/feature_selection/test_mrmr_endtoend_invariants.py` + generator `_mrmr_realistic_data.py` + gap analysis `MRMR_FE_TEST_GAP_ANALYSIS.md`).** The hundreds of existing FE tests probed each stage in ISOLATION -- which feature is SELECTED (`support_`/`get_feature_names_out`/recipe names/MI), slice-replay CONSISTENCY, and TREE/MI downstream score -- but none asserted the end-to-end contract "the VALUE a downstream model receives can reconstruct the target", which is exactly where the quantized-transform-output bug lived (a tree splits a rank code fine; only a LINEAR model on a magnitude-carrying target exposes it). This layer fuzzes a realistic grid (family x distribution x seed x FE config) and asserts six invariants on the ACTUAL fitted+transformed estimator: I1 every advertised feature survives transform; I2 names_out == transform columns; I3 byte-exact slice replay; I4 a genuine PRIVATE additive raw signal is not over-dropped (measured by the TREE downstream, since MRMR may keep the raw OR re-express it via a single-operand transform like `a__relu(a)`); I4b no kept redundant raw costs downstream + the strict cosmetic redundant-drop on the canonical UNIFORM terrain only; I5 FE space not materially worse than raw-only; I6 no input mutation + pickle round-trip preserves names_out/transform columns. The worker seeds the GLOBAL `np.random` stream (MRMR.fit consumes it independently of `random_seed`, which made the literal-column survivor set flaky across fresh workers). 68 passed / 9 skipped (the 9 = non-uniform strict-drop calibration boundary + no-engineered cases). The gap-analysis doc records why the prior suite missed the bug and the two documented limitations the layer surfaced (small-effective-n FE selection at ~21k train; continuous engineered ratios being less linear-friendly than the old codes on targets where they are not the dominant signal -- a scaling property, not lost information).

- **MRMR vote-dropped feature no recipeless re-selection regression (`tests/feature_selection/test_mrmr_fe_vote_dropped_no_recipeless_select.py`).** Pins the user's EXACT end-to-end repro (uniform `X={a,b,c,d,e}`, hidden noise `f`, CASE2 target, `fe_max_steps=2`, n=40000): asserts the `"selected without replayable recipe"` warning does NOT fire and every advertised / discovered feature survives `transform(df)`. Verified RED on the pre-fix commit (the warning fires and `add(qubed(a),rint(d))` is dropped) and GREEN post-fix.

- **Adversarial hardening battery for the three MRMR F2 feature-engineering fixes (`tests/feature_selection/test_f2_adversarial_battery.py`, 26 cells).** A deliberately adversarial probe of the BUG1 conditional raw-redundancy drop, BUG2 replayable-nested-recipe, and BUG3 poly/Fourier escalation fixes -- designed to FIND weak points, not confirm them. Grouped by bug class at n=20000 (n=12000 for the multi-seed majority cell): BUG1 (7) probes the over-drop vs under-drop boundary (raw entering the target BOTH privately and via a composite must be KEPT; a fully-subsumed raw's signal must remain recoverable; the f0fd18ad empty-selection class -- a raw subsumed only by a non-surviving feature must NOT be dropped; two independent privates both kept); BUG2 (5) pins BYTE-EXACT slice-vs-full replay of nested/mixed-operator/chained engineered recipes at fe_max_steps=2; BUG3 (12, incl. a multi-seed majority cell) pins Hermite He2/He3/He4 + Legendre/Chebyshev/Laguerre + adaptive-Fourier (k in {0.3,1,3,3.7}) operand-support recovery and HARD-rejects spurious poly/synergy fabrication on pure-noise and pure-linear targets. The BUG3 fix (c4c4dfbf) HOLDS across all 12 cells. FINDINGS surfaced by the battery: (1) the He2 PRODUCT pair `(a**2-1)*b` is a documented weak-signal wobble -- `a` recovers on a MAJORITY of seeds (6/6 at n=12000) but can be lost on a single unlucky n=20000 draw (same class as the F2 cross-mix detectability limit), so the He2 cell pins the per-seed FLOOR + a cross-seed majority rather than a brittle all-seed recovery; (2) the BUG2 replay contract is RED pre-fix -- any selected engineered feature nesting a warp-tagged `X__He2` sub-operand replays with a quantisation-bin |delta|=1 drift on a slice-vs-full transform (bin edges recomputed from the slice instead of frozen into the recipe). The 5 replay-dependent cells are `xfail(strict=True)` with a precise mechanism reason so master stays green AND they XPASS -> strict-fail the instant the BUG2 fix lands, forcing the marker's removal (the bug can never be silently masked).

### Fixed

- **MRMR never-empty raw-representative rescue RE-ADDED a fully-subsumed dominant raw operand because its conditional-redundancy guard resolved the engineered survivor by `str(recipe)` (dataclass REPR) instead of `recipe.name` (REAL BUG1, missed by the aad5ac39 leg-B removal).** On the user's EXACT fixture `y = a**2/b + f/5 + log(c)*sin(d)` at n=100000, `fe_max_steps=2` collapses the whole target into ONE recipe-only composite `add(mul(log(c),sin(d)),abs(div(sqr(a),abs(b))))`, so the raw `support_` is empty and the never-empty-raw-representative block runs. That block's conditional-redundancy guard (added to stop it resurrecting a subsumed operand) built its engineered-survivor anchor and replayable-name set from `str(r)` over `self._engineered_recipes_` -- but those are `EngineeredRecipe` OBJECTS whose `str()`/`repr()` is the full dataclass repr, NOT the column name. So `str(r) in cols` was always False, the anchor set was empty, the guard's inner `drop_redundant_raw_operands` call was SKIPPED (gated `if _eng_survivor_cols and _operand_idxs`), `_subsumed_operand_names` stayed empty, every operand was "eligible", and the empty-raw rescue re-added the highest-marginal operand `a` -- exactly the spurious bare raw the campaign set out to remove. MEASURED on this fixture (n=40000, the pipeline's own binned-MI primitive `_cmi_from_binned`/`_quantile_bin` + the S5 within-stratum conditional-permutation null): `CMI(a; y | composite)` debiased excess `= 0.0024` vs marginal excess `0.326` (0.7% retention) and `CMI(a; y | div(sqr(a),abs(b)))` excess `= 0.0024` (0.7%) -- `a` is FULLY subsumed by its `a**2/b` child; the composite fusion does NOT mask it (both conditionings collapse the residual identically, far below the `RAW_SELF_RETAIN_FRAC`=0.05 bar), so this is a genuine redundancy, not a residual-keep. FIX: resolve the recipe column name via `recipe.name` (with a `str(r)` fallback for a legacy bare-string entry) when building both the operand-token set and the engineered-survivor anchor / replayable set; AND, when the guard confirms every operand subsumed, record the verdict (`_raw_redundancy_dropped_ |= subsumed`, `_redundancy_emptied_raw_ = True`) so the DOWNSTREAM empty-raw rescue / RFECV-rescue / raw-signal augmentation (all of which key on those attributes) leave the support engineered-only instead of re-adding `a` -- the earlier raw-retention pass had dropped a/b/c/d WITHOUT setting those attributes, which is why the rescue fired. RESULT on the user's EXACT df (seed 0, n=100000): `get_feature_names_out()` goes from `['a', 'add(mul(log(c),sin(d)),abs(div(sqr(a),abs(b))))']` (raw `a` spuriously kept) to `['add(mul(log(c),sin(d)),abs(div(sqr(a),abs(b))))']` (engineered-only, `a` dropped); the genuine-private-raw control (`y += 3*a`) still KEEPS `a`. Files: `_mrmr_fit_impl/_fit_impl_core.py` (never-empty block: `recipe.name` resolution + record subsumption verdict). Test: `tests/feature_selection/test_biz_value_mrmr_fe_canonical.py::test_user_case_drops_redundant_raw_operands_at_large_n` TIGHTENED from "at most ONE raw stand-in (documented residual)" to "ZERO redundant raw operands AND `a` not in output" -- VERIFIED failing pre-fix at commit 23d71f0b (`support raw={'a'}`) and passing post-fix.

- **MRMR engineered-recipe replay was NOT byte-exact on a row-slice -- a nested engineered operand drifted by up to several quantisation bins on `transform()` of a sub-slice vs the full frame (BUG2 replay determinism).** A selected nested engineered feature (a step-2 composite, e.g. `sub(log(sub(invcbrt(b),cbrt(c))),qubed(a))` or a feature nesting a warp-tagged `a__He2` sub-operand) reproduced DIFFERENT values on a row-slice transform than on the full-frame transform, because two operand-axis parameters were REFIT from the transform input each replay instead of being FROZEN at fit time: (1) the orthogonal-basis preprocess (Hermite z-score mean/std, Legendre/Chebyshev min-max lo/hi, Laguerre shift) -- `_eval_orth_basis_column` called the basis `fit` on the replay data, so a slice's `nanmean`/`nanstd` drifted the z-score by ~1e-3 and the downstream quantiser turned it into a |delta|=1 bin drift; (2) the `smart_log` additive shift `(1e-5 - nanmin(operand))` (the registry `log` unary) -- a row-slice recomputed a different `nanmin`, shifting every `log` output and producing a multi-bin drift (max|delta|=8 measured on the nested `log(...)` operand). FIX: freeze both into the recipe at fit time and replay from the frozen values. The orth-basis builders (`build_orth_univariate_recipe` / `build_orth_pair_cross_recipe`) now store the fit-time `preprocess_params` (computed from the full fit column, reproducing the train-time axis exactly) and `_eval_orth_basis_column` replays via the basis `apply(x, frozen_params)` when present; the unary_binary builder stores a per-`log`-side `log_shift_<side>` anchor (computed from the reconstructed continuous fit-time operand, raw or nested-parent) and `_apply_unary_binary` replays `log` from that frozen shift. Both are omitted (legacy refit path) when absent, so pre-fix pickles stay byte-equal. RESULT: every selected engineered column -- including deeply nested ones -- replays BYTE-EXACTLY on any row-slice; on the user case 2 fixture `y = 0.2*a**2/b + f/5 + log(c*2)*sin(d/3)` the selected nested feature is no longer dropped for replay non-determinism and `transform()` is deterministic-from-raw. Files: `engineered_recipes/_orth_basis_recipes.py` (frozen preprocess params on univariate/pair-cross eval + builders), `_orthogonal_univariate_fe/__init__.py` + `_orth_pair_cross_fe.py` (capture + pass fit-time params), `engineered_recipes/_recipe_unary_binary.py` (frozen `smart_log` shift on replay + builder), `_mrmr_fe_step/_step_core.py` (compute the fit-time log-shift anchor from the continuous operand). Tests: the 5 BUG2 replay cells in `tests/feature_selection/test_f2_adversarial_battery.py` (former `xfail(strict=True)` markers removed; they now PASS -- VERIFIED failing pre-fix with max|delta|=8 replay drift and passing post-fix).

- **MRMR raw-redundancy drop FALSELY KEPT a DOMINANT raw operand that was fully subsumed by its surviving engineered child (BUG1).** On the user fixture `y = a**2/b + f/5 + log(c)*sin(d)` the numerator `a` carries the bulk of the target variance, so its marginal debiased excess is large (~0.5). The conditional raw-redundancy sweep (`drop_redundant_raw_operands`) had a second keep leg -- leg B, `max_child_anchor <= RAW_SUPERSET_MULT(=3) x raw_marg_excess` -> KEEP "the child is only a re-expression, not a superset" -- whose premise is FALSE by construction once step 0's DPI-trap consumer filter runs (a noise-paired re-expression child is already excluded from `consumers`, so every consumer reaching the keep rule is a genuine multi-source combination). Leg B's only live effect was the FALSE KEEP: for a dominant operand `3 x marg_excess` exceeds any realistic child anchor, so `a` was rescued even though the `a**2/b` ratio child (`div(neg(a),sqrt(b))`) FULLY subsumes it (leg A -- the significant-residual leg -- correctly failed at ~1-4% retention). FIX: removed leg B; the verdict is now leg A alone (CMI(raw; y | combination children) clears the within-stratum conditional-permutation floor AND the debiased conditional excess retains >= `RAW_SELF_RETAIN_FRAC` of the raw's own marginal excess). A genuine PRIVATE-term raw (linear/additive, ~6-11% retention) still clears it; a fully-subsumed dominant operand drops. Two companion guards keep the verdict consistent across the rest of the fit: (a) `_raw_redundancy_dropped_` operands are excluded from the RFECV rescue pool (CatBoost scores raw MARGINAL usefulness, blind to the engineered child's coverage, and would re-admit the dropped operand); (b) the never-empty-raw re-attach (which back-fills the highest-marginal raw when only engineered features were selected) now runs the SAME conditional-redundancy verdict and skips operands judged subsumed -- when every operand is subsumed (`_redundancy_emptied_raw_`) the support stays engineered-only (the recipe IS the complete feature set), no spurious raw stand-in. RESULT on the user fixture (n=20000): `get_feature_names_out()` raw-selected goes from rescuing the spurious bare `a` to `['c','f']` -- the dominant `a` no longer appears as a bare raw (its signal rides inside `mul(sin(c),exp(a__He2))` / `add(log(f),a__He2)`), while the genuine PRIVATE raws `c`,`f` are kept. Files: `_fe_raw_redundancy_drop.py` (leg-B removal), `_mrmr_fit_impl/_fit_impl_core.py` (RFECV-rescue exclusion + re-attach redundancy guard + `_redundancy_emptied_raw_` skip). Test: `tests/feature_selection/test_biz_value_mrmr_fe_canonical.py::test_redundancy_drop_drops_dominant_subsumed_operand_unit` (unit pin: dominant subsumed `a` must DROP; VERIFIED fails pre-fix -- leg B kept `a` -- and passes post-fix), plus the adversarial BUG1 battery cells (6 boundary cells: private+composite keeps raw, fully-subsumed signal retained, two-independent-privates both kept, empty-selection class).

- **MRMR FE produced NO engineered feature for a genuine weak-marginal SMOOTH interaction (e.g. `a**2/b`) -- the orth-poly / escalation machinery never even saw the pair (default-ON `fe_synergy_prevalence_rescue_enable`).** On the user's F2 `y = 0.2*a**2/b + f/5 + log(c*2)*sin(d/3)` the fitted output was `['f','d','c','mul(prewarp(c),prewarp(d))','add(prewarp(d),prewarp(f))']` -- carrying NO `(a,b)` feature at all, despite the shipped orthogonal-poly bases + auto-escalation working on synthetic tests. ROOT CAUSE (confirmed by symbol + numbers at n=12000): `a` and `b` have weak univariate MI (0.022 / 0.016), so neither is selected; the `(a,b)` pair enters the FE pool ONLY via the synergy bootstrap, where it must clear the STRICTER `fe_synergy_min_prevalence=1.5` RAW-MI ratio bar. The genuine `a**2/b` term scores joint MI 0.028 vs marginal-sum 0.025 -- ratio ~1.11, far below 1.5 -- so it was DROPPED at the `marginal_pair_mi_prescreen` gate, which sits UPSTREAM of all FE/escalation. The raw-MI prevalence ratio structurally UNDER-estimates a smooth, non-bilinear ratio interaction; LOWERING the bar is bench-rejected (injects optimisation-inflated noise products). The escalation never fired on `(a,b)` because the pair never reached `prospective_pairs`; it only escalated irrelevant cross-mix pairs. The `(c,d)` `log(c)*sin(d)` term was already optimally captured by the default prewarp (`mul(prewarp(c),prewarp(d))`=0.837 ≈ ceiling 0.838), so no escalation headroom there. FIX (two parts, behind the full admission gates): (1) a synergy pair that cleared the order-2 maxT permutation null but only missed the raw-MI synergy ratio is recorded and handed to the auto-escalation as a SECOND-CHANCE `failed_pair`; `_propose_poly`'s LEAK-SAFE held-out rank-1 ALS pair-vs-single |corr| margin (`fe_escalation_pairness_margin`=1.15) re-decides -- the genuine `(a,b)` scores pair/single ratio 1.24 vs cross-mix/noise ~1.0 -- and the proposed candidate then faces the order-2 maxT + marginal-permutation + S5 CMI-redundancy gates. (2) the escalation now ranks RESCUE pairs FIRST (a smooth interaction has LOW raw joint MI by construction, so a plain joint-MI sort buried `(a,b)` below 6 higher-MI cross pairs and the `fe_escalation_max_pairs=8` cap dropped it). (3) escalation features are made TERMINAL in the composite feed-forward (`fe_escalation_feedforward_enable=False`, new default): once `esc_poly_legendre_mul(a,b)` was admitted, the existing engineered-operand feed-forward re-paired it into `div(log(esc_poly_legendre_mul(a,b)),exp(mul(prewarp(c),prewarp(d))))` -- FUSING two INDEPENDENT additive target terms (`a**2/b` and `log(c)*sin(d)`) into a single ratio whose joint MI tops the greedy ranking, so MRMR then DROPPED the clean raw predictors + standalone captures and downstream R^2 REGRESSED (measured 0.943 -> 0.864). An escalation feature already captures a richer-basis interaction the library could not express; it stays SELECTED but no longer seeds further composites. RESULT on F2: the output is now `['f','d','c','mul(prewarp(c),prewarp(d))','add(prewarp(d),prewarp(f))','esc_poly_legendre_mul(a,b)']` -- the clean (a,b) feature appears STANDALONE; downstream 5-fold HistGBR R^2 lifts 0.943 -> 0.991 (the rescued `esc_poly_legendre_mul(a,b)` tracks `a**2/b` and on continuous values reaches R^2 0.991, vs 0.998 for the simple `div(sqr(a),neg(b))` -- the poly does NOT BEAT the simple form, their MIs tie at ~0.032; the WIN is letting the `(a,b)` signal through the gate that previously blocked it entirely). The rescue is INERT on pure-noise / cross-mix pairs (held-out ALS pair-vs-single margin ~1.0 fails the 1.15 pairness guard; the maxT null is never cleared at collection time). All three default ON/terminal; `fe_synergy_prevalence_rescue_enable=False` restores the pre-fix hard drop, `fe_escalation_feedforward_enable=True` restores unrestricted feed-forward. Files: `_mrmr_fe_step/_step_core.py` (rescue collection + escalation wiring + terminal feed-forward filter), `_fe_auto_escalation.py` (rescue-first ranking + `rescue_pairs` param), `mrmr/_mrmr_class.py` (two flags + `__setstate__` defaults). Tests: `tests/feature_selection/test_fe_synergy_prevalence_rescue.py` (unit rescue-fires/inert-on-noise + OFF true-negative control + biz_value F2 downstream-R^2 win).

- **`test_biz_val_mrmr_quantization_nbins_range_parametrize[5]` re-framed from a RAW-`support_` count to the full-mode total-selection invariant (wrong test expectation, no code change; no mask / no xfail / no opt-out).** The test asserted `1 <= len(sel.support_) <= df.shape[1]`, counting the RAW `support_` only. At `quantization_nbins=5` on the `make_signal_plus_noise(p_signal=3, p_noise=5)` fixture the FE step engineers a single additive child `add(add(x0,x2),x1)` (the sum of all three signal operands) that conditionally SUBSUMES x0, x1 AND x2; the raw-redundancy sweep then legitimately drops every raw operand (`_redundancy_emptied_raw_` set True, `_raw_redundancy_dropped_={x0,x1,x2}`) leaving an ENGINEERED-ONLY selection — a complete, intended full-mode outcome, NOT an empty/broken one. The child IS replayable (a `nested_parent_a` recipe is recorded, so it survives `transform()`): `get_feature_names_out()` returns `['x0', 'add(add(x0,x2),x1)']` (x0 incidentally re-attached by raw-signal retention) and the full selection is valid; only the RAW bin count is fragile and can legitimately be empty. This is the SAME class the sibling property test (`test_biz_val_mrmr_property_no_crash_on_random_configs`) and the f0fd18ad replayable-anchor fix already recognise (full mode may de-duplicate all signal into engineered features, leaving an empty raw `support_`). Root-caused as a WRONG ASSERTION, not an empty-support code bug: the replayable-anchor guard (`_fe_raw_redundancy_drop.py`) and support finalisation are correct here — the engineered child reaches transform output and carries all three signals. FIX: assert on the TOTAL selected set via `get_feature_names_out()` (raw + engineered), keeping the raw-column-count upper bound (FE only compresses signal here, never explodes it). All four parametrized bin counts green (`[5]`/`[10]`/`[20]`/`[50]`); 11 neighboring quantization / redundancy / property / dtype tests green; selection at the other bin counts unchanged (they return the full 3 raw signals — no engineered subsumer wins there).

- **biz_value pre-distortion: 5 stale TRUE-NEGATIVE pins re-framed into positive capability pins for the default-on FE auto-escalation (no revert / no xfail / no opt-out).** tests/feature_selection/test_biz_value_mrmr_pre_distortion.py asserted that the elementary unary/binary search and the fixed-cell hybrid-orth pair "cannot recover" the non-monotone F-POLY (`P(a)*Q(b)`) and oscillatory F-OSC (`sin(a**2)*b`) pre-distorted inners (corr `< 0.30`). The default-on FE auto-escalation (2026-06-10, `fe_auto_escalation_enable`, `_fe_auto_escalation.py`) now escalates the unrecovered high-MI pair to an orthogonal-poly product cell and DOES recover them: `esc_poly_hermite_mul(a,b)` at |corr| 0.736 for F-POLY (both UNB and hybrid paths), `esc_poly_laguerre_mul(a,b)` at |corr| 0.964 for F-OSC; downstream 5-fold Ridge R^2 lifts ~0.22 to ~0.67. These are stale negatives obsoleted by a real capability WIN, not regressions. RE-FRAMED `test_fpoly_unary_binary_fails_to_recover`, `test_fpoly_orth_hybrid_pair_default_does_not_recover`, `test_fosc_unary_binary_fails_to_recover`, and the two-path downstream `test_fpoly_downstream_score_stuck_at_raw_baseline_for_true_negative_paths` from negatives into positive recovery pins (corr/R^2 thresholds set with margin below the measured values).
