Each is paired with a NEW `escalation OFF` control (`fe_auto_escalation_enable=False`) that re-asserts the old negative (F-POLY base paths reach only a weak relu artifact |corr| ~0.49 / R^2 ~0.43; F-OSC base reaches ~0), proving the recovery is ATTRIBUTABLE to the escalation, not the base search or noise, and preserving the true-negative as a gated control. Also re-anchored `test_fosc_hybrid_orth_pair_partially_recovers_and_beats_unb` to the escalation-OFF baselines on both paths (default-on escalation lifts BOTH paths to the same `esc_poly_laguerre_mul`, which would otherwise collapse the intrinsic-advantage comparison). Full file 19 passed; auto-escalation suite 8 passed.

- **Narrow-pool maxT noise floor re-leaked a pure-noise feature into MRMR `support_` on heavy-tailed (log-normal) regression pools after the 2026-06-10 target-rebin guard (correctness regression; FE OFF).** `MRMR(fe_max_steps=0).fit()` on a log-normal target with 3 genuine signals + 6 i.i.d. Gaussian noise columns admitted `noise_2` into `support_` on seeds 7 and 42 (`tests/feature_selection/test_biz_val_filters_screen_heavytail_floor.py::TestLognormalEndToEndNoNoiseLeaks`). ROOT CAUSE: the narrow-pool gate `target_oversplit_floor_applies` decides whether to compute the Westfall-Young maxT permutation-null gain floor over the order-1 raw pool; it required the target to be OVER-SPLIT relative to the features (`nbins_y >= 3 * median(nbins_x)`). That predicate was calibrated against the then-default adaptive `nbins_strategy="mdlp"`, which over-split the heavy-tailed target into ~30 bins (vs feat ~5). The 2026-06-10 TARGET REBIN GUARD (`_mrmr_fit_impl/_fit_impl_core.py`) re-bins the injected continuous target back to the legacy equal-frequency `quantization_nbins` (~10) encoding — a correct fix, because supervised MDLP on the target is self-referential and degrades MI sensitivity — but it removed the ~30-bin over-split signature, so `nbins_y=10 >= 3*5=15` became False, the gate stopped firing, the floor stayed 0.0, and the plug-in-bias-inflated noise column leaked past the abs/rel gain floors. The floor MACHINERY was never broken: at the production quantile-10 binning it still cleanly separates the 3 signals (corrected marginal MI 0.025–0.53) from all 6 noise columns (max ~0.005) with a floor ~0.006–0.007 — only the gate that decides whether to compute it was keyed on a binning artifact the rebin guard now removes. FIX: lower the gate's discriminator from "target over-split 3× vs features" to "target is HIGH-CARDINALITY (`nbins_y >= median(nbins_x)`, i.e. a continuous regression target binned to the full `quantization_nbins`, not a low-card classification target)" — `screen_fdr_target_oversplit_ratio` / `oversplit_ratio` default `3.0 -> 1.0`. The RELIABILITY predicate (`n / (nbins_y * median(nbins_x)) >= min_rows_per_joint_cell`, default 8.0) is now the sole guard separating the WIN from the NO-REGRESSION: at production binning the dense weak-signal pool (sklearn-diabetes-shaped, n=330, nbins_y=10, feat=10 -> 3.3 rows/joint-cell) FAILS it so the floor stays OFF and all 10 genuine weak features survive, while the lognormal pool (~25–50 rows/joint-cell) passes so the floor fires and rejects the noise. Both failing seeds now green; the 3 genuine signals are still selected (no recall loss); the diabetes no-regression half (`test_mrmr_keeps_majority_of_weak_features`) and every `TestGateThresholdSemantics` case stay green (low-card classification target nbins_y∈{2,3} still OFF; over-split-but-sparse still OFF; over-split-and-dense still ON). 86 passed across the neighboring permutation / null-FDR / order-3-floor / layer15 suites. (The separately-noted `test_biz_val_mrmr_quantization_nbins_range_parametrize[5]` is now resolved in its own entry above — root-caused as a wrong RAW-`support_` assertion, re-framed to the full-mode total-selection invariant; it was never a code bug.)

- **W3 frontier #6 -- FS-contract test_pipeline_compatibility xfail removed; now asserts a real Pipeline fit->predict + feature propagation for all three selectors (sklearn-parity axis, no-xfail-to-defer).** The shared contract test (tests/feature_selection/test_fs_selector_contract.py::TestSklearnParity) wrapped the Pipeline([fs, clf]).fit->predict embed in a try/except (TypeError, ValueError, AttributeError): pytest.xfail(...) guard that masked any Pipeline incompatibility instead of asserting. Decisive probing of each selector through a real Pipeline (clone + set_output(transform=pandas) + fit->predict + get_feature_names_out propagation) showed the guard is DEAD: MRMR, RFECV, and ShapProxiedFS all now satisfy the full Pipeline contract end-to-end (MRMR emits 7 cols, RFECV 10, ShapProxiedFS 8 on the canonical binary fixture; each propagates exactly to the downstream LogisticRegression.n_features_in_, and get_feature_names_out() width matches transform width). No selector raised -- the prior xfail predated the MRMR input-mutation fix (43d67a44) and was never re-validated. FIX: removed the xfail/except and replaced it with hard assertions that the Pipeline trains, predicts the right shape, the downstream estimator n_features_in_ equals the selector emitted column count, and (when present) get_feature_names_out width matches -- i.e. selected features propagate correctly. Added test_pipeline_biz_value (real FS->model Pipeline on the canonical fixture must beat the majority-class baseline by >0.05) pinning end-to-end signal retention. Also confirmed the sibling get_feature_names_out skip guard (~line 262) is a non-firing defensive branch (all three selectors expose BOTH get_feature_names_out and get_support) and corrected the stale comment claiming ShapProxied only has get_support. No selector remains genuinely Pipeline-blocked. Full contract file: 56 passed, zero xfail / zero skip.

- Investigated (no behavioral change): large-n FE-search cProfile at n=100k/p=40 single-thread (total tottime 1133.7s) shows NO single mlframe hotspot >25% (top 10.3%); work spreads across discretization / FE pair-search / permutation-null / info_theory, all already njit/prange with kernel_tuning_cache dispatchers -- perf is mature at large-n; no actionable numba/cupy dispatcher win. Documented so it is not re-profiled (wave-2 W10 bench-reject).

### Investigated (no behavioral change)

- **W8 — extreme-imbalance / tiny-n target-binning degenerate-bin guard: BENCH-REJECTED, no guard shipped.** Hypothesis: equi-frequency TARGET binning in the MI relevance screen silently yields a single-class degenerate bin under rare<<1% positives / n<200, corrupting MI and destabilising rare-feature ranking, fixable with a merge-degenerate-bins + coarser-nbins fallback. Empirically FALSIFIED on the literal n=150 / 1%-positive decisive test: (a) the classification path coerces integer y via factorize (`_coerce_y_classes`), so it keeps exactly the genuine classes and CANNOT form a degenerate target bin; (b) continuous-target `_quantile_bin` already drops duplicate quantile edges (documented `np.unique(edges)` behaviour, not silent), and tied-value bins yield finite, stable plug-in MI — existing collapse guards already live at `_adaptive_nbins.py` / `_discretization_edges.py` / `info_theory/_entropy_kernels.py`; (c) the proposed cap-to-nbins fix did NOT stabilise the genuine rare-feature rank across 12 seeds (mean rank 5.25→5.33, std 1.96→2.36 — no gain), confirming the instability is intrinsic small-n statistics (the cross-project "rare imbalance needs large-n" fact), not a binning artefact; large-n balanced is byte-identical either way. Distinct from rejected idea #18 (imbalance-MI rescore). A bench-attempt-rejected note pins the site in `_unified_fe_gate._coerce_y_classes`; `tests/feature_selection/test_w8_target_binning_reject.py` pins the three pillars. Selection byte-identical (comment + test only).

### Fixed

- **Serialization-safety PIN for the Wave-1/2/3 default-on MRMR diagnostics (runtime-caches-break-pickle failure class). NO behavioral change — regression pin only.** Verified the new default-on diagnostic artifacts (`explain_selection()`, `get_fe_rejection_report()` / `fe_rejection_ledger_`, `get_unlabeled_recipe_kinds()`, `selection_stability_report()` / `_stability_replay_state_`, `fe_provenance_`, `_fe_recommended_flags_`, `degenerate_columns_`) survive `joblib.dump` → `load` and that the RELOADED accessors run without `AttributeError` AND return byte-identical output. Decisive round-trip on the canonical `y = a**2/b + log(c)*sin(d)` fixture (FE on, with a constant + duplicate column so `degenerate_columns_` populates): all artifacts round-trip cleanly, `explain_selection()` / `get_fe_rejection_report()` / `get_unlabeled_recipe_kinds()` are byte-identical pre vs post pickle, `_stability_replay_state_` (the row-subsampled int32 binned-matrix slice) is byte-identical and the fitted estimator pickles to ~113 KB (no live-cache / closure / numba-dispatcher leak, no `__getstate__`/`__setstate__` exclusion dropping a default-on accessor's state), and `sklearn.clone()` preserves params + refits. No bug found — the new diagnostics are serialization-safe; the pin (`tests/feature_selection/test_pickle_parity_wave3_diagnostics.py`) makes a future change that drops one of these from the pickled state, or stuffs an unpicklable live cache into one, fail loudly instead of silently breaking a default-on diagnostic after serialization (+ a pickle-size guard so a future `_stability_replay_state_` bloat regresses).

- **`fe_provenance_` origin-labeling: hinge / change-point legs (and ~20 sibling engineered families) were mislabeled `engineered_unknown` (UI/diagnostics axis). PURE metadata fix — selection byte-identical.** On the canonical `y = a**2/b + log(c)*sin(d)` fixture, `explain_selection()` / `fe_provenance_` listed the piecewise-linear hinge legs (`a__relu_gt…`, `a__relu_lt…`, `d__relu_lt…`) under `engineered_unknown` instead of their true kind, degrading both new diagnostic surfaces (`fe_rejection_ledger_`, `explain_selection()`). ROOT CAUSE: the hinge generator already stamps `kind="hinge_basis"` at recipe creation (`_hinge_basis_fe.build_hinge_basis_recipe`), but the provenance classifier `_RECIPE_KIND_TO_ORIGIN` (in `_mrmr_fe_provenance.py`) had no branch for it — a classifier-mapping gap, not a stamping gap. Auditing ALL recipe `kind=` literals against the map surfaced ~20 OTHER engineered families equally unlabeled: `numeric_rounding`/`digit_extract`, `modular`, `group_distance`, `temporal_expanding`/`rolling`/`lag`, `orth_wavelet`, `rare_category`/`conditional_residual`/`conditional_dispersion`/`rankgauss`, `div`/`log_div`, the grouped-agg family (`grouped_agg`/`composite_group_agg`/`grouped_quantile`/`target_aware_group_bin`), the cat-cross pair/triple, and the higher-arity orth crosses (`orth_diff_basis`/`orth_cluster_basis`/`orth_triplet_cross`/`orth_quadruplet_cross`). FIX: add the missing kind→origin branches (plus new origin labels `hinge_basis`, `numeric_decompose`, `periodic`, `group_distance`, `temporal_agg`, `wavelet_basis`, `cat_cross`, `grouped_agg`, `extra_fe`); `factorize` stays `engineered_unknown` by design (generic ordinal lookup). On the canonical fixture the engineered-survivor `engineered_unknown` fraction drops from 0.625 (5/8 — all hinge legs) to 0.000; `support_` and `get_feature_names_out()` are byte-identical pre/post-fix (verified against origin/master at the same seed). Labeling-only: cProfile impact ~0 (one extra dict entry).

### Added

- **Unified local-MI gate sink — FE-family caller wiring (closes the W6 open wiring step; DEFAULT-ON). PURE ADDITIVE — no gate-logic change, selection byte-identical.** W6 instrumented the shared `_unified_fe_gate.local_mi_gate` abs-MAD noise floor (`raw_mi_noise_floor` = med+k*MAD) with a `reject_sink` callable + a `marginal_uplift_floor` / `unified_local_mi_gate` record, but its FE-family callers still passed nothing, so for every candidate those families dropped at the unified floor the kill was SILENTLY un-recorded. All 6 unified-gate FE families now thread the sink end-to-end: target-encoding (`kfold_target_encode_with_recipes`), count/freq/cat-num residual (`count_encode_with_recipes` / `frequency_encode_with_recipes` / `cat_num_interaction_with_recipes` via the shared `_gate_enc` helper), missingness-indicator (`missing_indicator_with_recipes`), ratio/log-ratio/grouped-delta/lagged-diff (`pairwise_ratio_with_recipes` / `pairwise_log_ratio_with_recipes` / `grouped_delta_with_recipes` / `lagged_diff_with_recipes` via the shared `_gate_ratio_pairs` helper + direct calls), rare-category (`hybrid_rare_category_fe`), conditional-residual (`hybrid_conditional_residual_fe`), and conditional-dispersion (`hybrid_conditional_dispersion_fe`). Each gained an optional `reject_sink: Optional[Callable[..., None]] = None` param forwarded to `local_mi_gate`; the MRMR fit caller (`_mrmr_fit_impl/_fit_impl_core.py`) builds one `record_fe_rejection(self, step=<fe_step>)` closure per family stage (mirroring W6's `_cb_reject_sink`) and passes it in. These stages all run single-process inline in `_fit_impl` (no loky boundary), so no reserved-result-dict plumbing is needed. **Also fixed a pre-existing ordering bug that hid W6's own kills:** `_fe_rejection_records_` was reset to `[]` partway through `_fit_impl` (between the recipe-FE family stages and the pair-search loop), clobbering every record the recipe-FE families AND the W6 cluster-basis stage had already appended; the reset is moved to fit-start (next to `_fe_steps_executed_ = 0`) so records from ALL FE stages survive into `fe_rejection_ledger_`. Cost record-only (a `list.append` on a rare drop path; no MI recompute). Validated: on a noisy numeric+categorical pool driving the ratio + count-encoding families, `fe_rejection_ledger_` now records 90 `marginal_uplift_floor` / `unified_local_mi_gate` kills across both families (it recorded ZERO before this wiring — the ledger was empty), `explain_selection()` fingers them, and the survivor set is byte-identical with vs without the sink. Tests: `tests/feature_selection/test_unified_gate_sink_wiring.py` (unit: each newly-wired family caller forwards the sink + byte-identical keep set; decisive biz_value: end-to-end 2-family floor kills land in the ledger + `explain_selection()` fingers one).
- **Embedding / free-text PASSTHROUGH through MRMR feature selection -> learnable-embedding neural networks (DEFAULT-ON; corrective mechanism).** MRMR's MI screen discretises every candidate column into integer bins, which requires scalar (hashable, orderable) cells. EMBEDDING-VECTOR columns (object cells = `list`/`np.ndarray`, e.g. a precomputed per-row embedding) and long FREE-TEXT columns violate that: the legacy path fed them to `categorize_dataset` and crashed with `TypeError: unhashable type: 'numpy.ndarray'` (or mis-binned text into a useless ~N-level categorical), so such columns were dropped/mishandled and never reached the PyTorch-Lightning MLP / recurrent network whose learnable embeddings + `_encode_emb_text_fit` boundary encoder are their correct consumers. `MRMR.fit` now DETECTS these columns (new `_mrmr_passthrough.detect_passthrough_columns`, sampling-bounded so a 100+ GB frame is never materialised), EXCLUDES them from the MI / discretisation / FE candidate set via a zero-copy column-subset narrow (RAM-safe; the working frame shares the kept columns' buffers, no row copy), and RE-ATTACHES them to `support_` at fit-end so `transform()` emits them UNCHANGED. `feature_names_in_` keeps the full input width so the sklearn `n_features_in_` contract holds. Strategy layer: `NeuralNetStrategy` and `RecurrentModelStrategy` now set `supports_embedding_features = supports_text_features = True`, so the suite stops dropping these columns in `_get_or_create_tier_dataframes` and threads them to the network end-to-end (Linear / XGB / HGB stay False -- they cannot consume raw vector/text; CatBoost was already True). Default ON per the corrective-mechanism convention; opt out with `MRMR(embedding_passthrough=False)` (restores the legacy crash/drop) or per-kind via `embedding_passthrough_detect_embeddings` / `embedding_passthrough_detect_text`. New `feature_selection/filters/_mrmr_passthrough.py`. Tests: `tests/feature_selection/test_mrmr_embedding_passthrough.py` (unit: detector classification, byte-untouched passthrough, scalar selection unchanged, `n_features_in_` contract, opt-out, scalar-only no-op); `tests/feature_selection/test_biz_val_mrmr_embedding_passthrough.py` (biz_value: on a synthetic where ALL signal lives in the embedding, MRMR-passthrough -> MLP test-RMSE <= 0.80 x the drop-it baseline -- measured ~0.46 vs ~1.0; plus a pin that passthrough OFF reproduces the unhashable-ndarray crash). cProfile (`feature_selection/_benchmarks/bench_embedding_passthrough.py`): detection + narrow are O(ncols), <0.5% of fit cumtime -- no actionable speedup.

- **W5 — `compare_selectors()` cross-selector agreement report (DEFAULT-ON; wave-2 frontier, UI/diagnostics axis). PURE read-only convenience — no selection logic added, no selector mutated.** A single module-level call `compare_selectors(X, y, selectors=[...])` (new `feature_selection/compare_selectors.py`, re-exported from `feature_selection/__init__.py`) fits (or accepts pre-fitted) MRMR / RFECV / BorutaShap / ShapProxiedFS / HybridSelector / any sklearn-style selector, aligns each one's selected support on the common `X.columns` index, and emits three orthogonal views via a `SelectorComparison` dataclass: a feature×selector boolean **agreement matrix** (`.agreement`), **pairwise Jaccard** overlaps (`.jaccard`), and a "**picked-by-k-of-n**" **consensus** column (`.consensus`), plus a compact `.report()` text view (<1.5 screens). WHY: `HybridSelector` already stashes per-member name-lists at `member_selections_` but never surfaces them; this is the orthogonal, read-only consumer of that kind of data — it DISPLAYS per-member disagreement, it does NOT use it to change any decision (distinct from the rejected ideas that tried to USE per-member confidence to ALTER the combined decision). Support is read via a fallback chain `get_feature_names_out → selected_features_ → support_ (bool mask or int indices, paired with feature_names_in_) → BorutaShap.accepted`, so every selector in the family works unchanged. Selectors that are unavailable (missing dep / GPU-only path / fit failure) are SKIPPED with a recorded reason in `.skipped` rather than aborting the report; all-unavailable returns an empty-but-valid report, never an error. `fit` knob: `None` (default) fits only un-fitted selectors, `True`/`False` force/forbid fitting. Cost is ~0 beyond the selector fits (near-0 on pre-fitted inputs: 50 assemble+report cycles over 4 pre-fitted selectors run <1 s in cProfile). On a madelon-like fixture the report surfaces the documented MRMR-under / RFECV-over disagreement (MRMR keeps the parsimonious informative core, RFECV over-retains noise; Jaccard ≈ 0.20). Tests: `tests/feature_selection/test_compare_selectors.py` (unit: matrix/Jaccard/consensus correctness across all four accessor shapes + graceful-skip + all-unavailable + fit-path + duplicate-name disambiguation; biz_value: MRMR-vs-RFECV disagreement surfaced on the madelon-like frame; cProfile: pre-fitted assembly <1 s).

- **`MRMR.degenerate_columns_` — degenerate-frame diagnostic surface (wave-2 frontier idea, edge-case axis). DEFAULT-ON.** A degenerate-frame robustness audit confirmed `MRMR.fit` already does NOT crash on pathological input columns (all-NaN / constant land at MI ~ 0 and are dropped by the relevance gate; exact-duplicate / perfectly-collinear columns are dropped by the conditional-MI redundancy gate, byte-identically) — the gap was the missing DIAGNOSTIC SURFACE telling the user WHICH columns were degenerate and WHY, which the sibling selectors (RFECV's suspicious-column logs, ShapProxied's cluster reports) expose. `fit` now runs a cheap audit (`_mrmr_degenerate.audit_degenerate_columns`) and populates `degenerate_columns_` — a dict `column -> reason` where reason is `all_nan` / `constant` / `duplicate_of:<col>` / `collinear_with:<col>`. PURELY DIAGNOSTIC: it does NOT remove columns or alter which features are selected (verified byte-identical on signal-free degenerates; redundant duplicate/collinear pairs keep exactly one representative as the existing redundancy gate already did). The collinearity pass is VECTORISED as a single standardised-Gram BLAS GEMM (~205 ms at n=20k/p=200, vs ~8 s for the naive O(p²) `np.corrcoef` loop). The y-NaN/+-inf clean `ValueError` (parity with RFECV/ShapProxied — no silent coercion) was already present in `fit` and is now pinned by tests. New `_mrmr_degenerate.py`; covered by `tests/feature_selection/test_mrmr_degenerate_frames.py` (per-type unit + biz_value mixed-frame + transparency pins).

- **W2 — `MRMR.get_unlabeled_recipe_kinds()` provenance self-audit accessor (DEFAULT-ON; wave-2 frontier). PURE ADDITIVE diagnostic — selection byte-identical.** Commit 205baa86 labeled ~20 sibling FE families in `_RECIPE_KIND_TO_ORIGIN`, leaving `factorize` (generic ordinal lookup) as the only DELIBERATE `engineered_unknown`. Until now there was no MEASURED way to confirm the next FE family didn't silently regress provenance back to `engineered_unknown`. The new fit-only accessor (mirrors the `get_fe_report` / `get_fe_rejection_report` naming + wiring in `mrmr/__init__.py`) returns a `{recipe.kind: count}` dict over the SURVIVING engineered columns (`support_rank >= 0`) whose origin resolved to `engineered_unknown`. On a normal fit after 205baa86 this is exactly `{"factorize": N}`; any OTHER kind appearing is an UNREGISTERED family — the guardrail firing. A companion `DELIBERATELY_UNLABELED_KINDS = frozenset({"factorize"})` constant lets callers subtract the by-design set to isolate the guardrail signal. Pure-read, never raises (empty dict when unfitted / no engineered survivors / every kind labeled). Cost ~0 (post-fit dict build over the provenance frame). Tests: `tests/feature_selection/test_wave2_w2w6_diagnostics.py` (unit: a fake unregistered kind is flagged AND is outside the deliberate set; biz_value: a clean fit returns only `factorize`; screened-out non-survivors excluded; unfitted -> `{}`).

- **W6 — FE rejection-ledger extension to the abs-MAD floor sites (DEFAULT-ON; wave-2 frontier). PURE ADDITIVE — no gate-logic change, selection byte-identical.** The `fe_rejection_ledger_` (b1a1048a) instrumented the pair-search gates but left the two `med + k*MAD` absolute-floor sites in the non-pair-search FE families uninstrumented, so `explain_selection()` could not finger an abs-floor kill (the floors the session had to diagnose by hand). Both now record the kill at the drop site via the same `record_fe_rejection` call under gate `marginal_uplift_floor`: `_mi_greedy_fe._greedy_score_and_select` (records candidates that cleared the uplift gate but missed `abs_floor`, with `observed=engineered_mi`, `threshold=abs_floor`) and `_orthogonal_cluster_basis_fe.generate_cluster_basis_features` (same, per cluster-basis candidate). Plumbed via an optional `reject_sink` callable threaded through each function's wrapper chain (`greedy_mi_fe_construct[_with_recipes]` / `hybrid_orth_mi_cluster_basis_fe[_with_recipes]`) so the FE modules stay decoupled from the estimator; the MRMR fit caller (`_mrmr_fit_impl/_fit_impl_core.py`) passes a closure over `record_fe_rejection(self, step=<fe_step>)`. **Class-closure:** the shared `_unified_fe_gate.local_mi_gate` abs-MAD noise floor (`raw_mi_noise_floor` = med+k*MAD, used by ~6 FE families) was the OTHER uninstrumented site of the same gate class; it gained the same `reject_sink` plumbing + a `marginal_uplift_floor` record at its floor drop (operator `unified_local_mi_gate`). Its 6 family callers now ALL pass the sink (see the next entry, "unified local-MI gate sink — FE-family caller wiring"); at the time of W6 they did not yet (the parameter defaulted to `None` / harmless). The CMI-redundancy second-pass gate is a distinct gate class already conceptually covered by `cmi_redundancy`. Cost record-only (a `list.append` on a branch that is already a rare drop path; no MI/CMI recompute). Tests: `tests/feature_selection/test_wave2_w2w6_diagnostics.py` (unit + biz_value: on a lone-strong-signal-among-noise pool the mi_greedy + cluster-basis + unified floors record `marginal_uplift_floor` kills with `observed < threshold`; byte-identical survivor set with/without the sink).

- **`MRMR.explain_selection()` — two assemblable enrichments (frontier idea #4 wave-2, UI/diagnostics axis). DEFAULT-ON, PURE additive, selection byte-identical.** (a) WHAT-IF-FLIP preview: a new section reports, per relaxable `fe_*` knob, how many `fe_rejection_ledger_` candidates a one-band threshold relaxation would re-admit — computed as a PURE COUNT `count(margin > -delta)` over the recorded ledger (NO refit, no gate-logic change), generalised across the main gates that map to a knob (`marginal_pair_mi_prescreen`/`fe_synergy_min_prevalence`, `engineered_mi_prevalence`/`fe_min_engineered_mi_prevalence`, `cmi_redundancy`, `order2_maxt_floor`, `marginal_uplift_floor`, `stability_vote`), each with its one-band `delta` (`_GATE_TO_FLIP_BAND` in `_mrmr_explain.py`). The preview count is cross-checked to EQUAL an actual one-band flag-flip refit's re-admit count over the ledger's recorded candidates (`test_biz_value_mrmr_explain_w4_enrichments.py::test_whatif_preview_matches_actual_flag_flip_refit`). (b) PER-FEATURE MI/gain ATTRIBUTION: the survivor line now shows each surviving feature's MRMR gain to `y` (the cached `fe_provenance_.mrmr_gain` selection score) and orders survivors descending by gain, so the user sees WHICH survivors carry the signal — on the canonical `y=a**2/b+log(c)*sin(d)` fixture the genuine engineered signal feature (`sub(prewarp(d),prewarp(a__p2cos1))`, gain 0.3141) tops the list ahead of raw/noise columns. Both degrade gracefully (empty-ledger / FE-disabled message; no crash); report stays under 1.5 screens (~1.1 KB). Assembly cost ~0 (post-fit pandas groupby/string build; the what-if is a count over the ledger with no refit). `support_` / `get_feature_names_out()` byte-identical pre/post (the layer never touches decision logic). NOTE: the gain helper column is named `gain_attr` (not `_gain`) because `DataFrame.itertuples` silently renames leading-underscore columns to positional `_N`.

- **`MRMR.explain_selection()` — one-call human-readable selection report (frontier idea #4, UI-convenience axis). DEFAULT-ON.** The MRMR/Hybrid surface exposes dozens of `fe_*` knobs, a Layer-54 survivor provenance surface (`fe_provenance_`), a per-gate rejection ledger (`fe_rejection_ledger_`), and a Layer-99 meta-FE recommender — but had NO single accessor narrating "why these features / what did FE build / which gate dropped what / which knob would I turn". `explain_selection()` ASSEMBLES those already-populated fit artifacts into a one-screen narrative: the surviving selected features + their engineered recipe kinds (from `fe_provenance_`); the BINDING rejection gate — the top killer from `fe_rejection_ledger_` with the drop count and margin band (e.g. "marginal_pair_mi_prescreen — dropped 13 (margin -0.01..-0.26)"); the meta-FE recommender's CHOSEN `fe_*` flags (newly persisted as the fit-only `_fe_recommended_flags_` from `recommend_fe_flags_by_rules`, which previously picked flags but never narrated them back); and a one-line actionable hint mapping the binding gate to the knob that would admit more candidates. PURE ADDITIVE metadata assembly — no decision-logic change, fit-only, zero accuracy risk; it is a method you call (no flag). Degrades gracefully to an empty-but-valid report when FE is disabled, there are no rejections, or the estimator is unfitted. Assembly cost is ~0 (post-fit string build, ~8 ms / fit). New `_mrmr_explain.py`; wired onto the class in `mrmr/__init__.py`.

### Investigated (no behavior change)

- **Leave-candidate-out / trimmed MI noise floor (backlog #2 / frontier-idea-3) — BENCH-REJECTED (2026-06-11).** The hypothesis that `noise_floor = median(pool) + 3.5·1.4826·MAD(pool)` self-gates a lone strong signal (signal pooled into med/MAD lifts the floor above itself) was tested and falsified. A 300k-draw Monte Carlo shows median/MAD is already robust to a single outlier, so the self-gating miss fires in only ~10% of signal pools and there it is driven by WIDE NOISE spread (not signal self-pooling). Upper-trim (cap 10%) fixes 50.2% of bug cases, true per-candidate leave-one-out only 39.6%, and BOTH catastrophically regress the all-noise case (removing the top/held-out value lowers the median while barely moving MAD, dropping the floor below the remaining noise band): all-noise leak rose from classic 13,575 to trim 36,741 / LOO 29,619 (~2-3× more noise admitted). Fails the ship gates (all-noise must stay rejected; no borderline-noise leak). No code path changed — the pooled median+MAD floor is kept at all ~20 MRMR FE sites; a `bench-attempt-rejected` note with the numbers is left at the shared helper in `_unified_fe_gate.py` so the lever is not re-tried. A real fix for the rare wide-noise miss would target the sigma threshold or an absolute-MI anchor, not the candidate pooling.

### Research / bench-rejected

- FE backlog #6 (surrogate-GBM split-co-occurrence 3-way seeder) + #7 (its mandatory order-3 Westfall-Young maxT floor): BENCH-REJECTED at the decisive Stage-1 measurement; NOT integrated. Hypothesis was that a shallow GBM's gain-weighted root->leaf path co-occurrence could propose pure zero-marginal 3-way synergy triples into the MRMR-core FE pool, bypassing the blind univariate top-N (`seed_count`) seed in `_run_fe_step`. On the proposed needle (n=4000, p=200 iid, `y=sign(x7*x42*x113)+0.3*noise`; all univariate |corr| <= 0.026 and all pairwise-product |corr| <= 0.026) the needle {7,42,113} NEVER surfaced: baseline-150r / 1000r / extra_trees / colsample-0.1 / extra+colsample all gave needle_rank=None and top-triple recall 0/3; the only config to place it at all (depth-5 extra_trees 1000r) ranked it 18778/32947 (score 12 vs top ~150), still 0/3. A same-walker positive control ranked a detectable pairwise XOR 0/1640 and a 3-way-with-pairwise-leak needle 0/766, proving the null is the pure-3way's greedy-invisibility, not a code bug. Tree-path co-occurrence inherits the greedy blindness it claimed to bypass (consistent with the prior beam-search-useless-for-MRMR finding). The order-3 maxT floor (#7) was never a standalone win -- it is the rail for a 3-way proposer -- so with no working proposer it ships nothing. In-code bench-rejected note left at the `numeric_vars_to_consider` seed point in `_mrmr_fe_step/_step_core.py`. Nothing in the pipeline currently reaches pure 3-way synergy via tree co-occurrence; a detect-without-enumerate proposer (RFF interaction pre-screen, #9) is the suggested non-greedy path.
- **FE backlog #9 (RFF / random-projection sparse-support interaction pre-screen, detect-without-enumerate): BENCH-REJECTED — re-confirmed on the 3-way trees-miss case (2026-06-11, cycle-10).** Elevated-priority re-attempt after cycle-5 proved the #6 GBM tree-path seeder is GREEDY-BLIND to pure 3-way synergy (recall 0/3), leaving RFF as the last open NON-GREEDY path. Hypothesis: R random sparse cosine features `φ_r(x)=cos(w_rᵀx+b_r)` over 2–4-col supports whose MI exceeds their additive baseline flag an interaction in that support without scoring the algebraic pair/triple. Decisive A/B/C (n<=4000, single process): **(A)** 2-way `y=x3*x400`, p=500, R=4000 sparse-2 -> support recall **0->0** (needle support NEVER drawn in 4000 tries; P(hit)~1.9%); **(B)** 3-way `y=sign(x7*x42*x113)+0.3*noise`, p=200, R=8000 sparse-3 -> support recall **0->0** (needle NEVER drawn; P(hit)~0.36%); **(C)** pure-noise -> 0 promoted (the per-projection permutation-null floor holds, noise-clean). The binding constraint is COVERAGE, not scoring: an INJECTED-support control shows the uplift CORRECTLY scores a *covered* 3-way needle above its additive baseline (0.0224 vs 0.0077) and above all three of its 2-subsets (~0.000-0.0035, confirming the pure-3-way is greedy-invisible to its own pairs) -- so the scorer is NOT greedy-blind to 3-way; random sparse draws simply never cover the specific localized needle. **Cost:** 0.95-recall of ONE needle needs ~623k draws (2-way) / ~6.56M (3-way), and one RFF support-eval ~17x a direct joint-MI eval, so RFF costs **~85x the full deterministic enumeration in BOTH the 2-way and 3-way regimes** (R<<p^2 is true per-draw, but needle hit-probability is R/C(p,k), so reaching a specific needle needs R>=C(p,k)/wt >> p^2). Fails the ship gate (A must recover the 2-way needle). **3-way frontier verdict:** pure 3-way synergy remains UNREACHED by anything in the pipeline -- trees (GBM #6) are greedy-blind, RFF (#9) is coverage-blind; only direct order-3 enumeration (raise/relax the synergy-screen cap + its budget) recovers it deterministically. No `_rff_interaction_prescreen.py` shipped; in-code bench-rejected note extended at the `numeric_vars_to_consider` seed point in `_mrmr_fe_step/_step_core.py`; backlog #9 marked rejected.

### Fixed

- **Cross-fold recipe stability vote no longer DROPS a genuine per-operand PRE-WARP recipe by judging it on the elementary marginal-sum uplift gate it structurally cannot pass (2026-06-11).** With `fe_pair_prewarp_enable=True` on a NON-monotone product target (`y=(a**3-2a)*(b**2-b)`) the FE pair search admits `mul(prewarp(a),prewarp(b))` via its OWN prewarp-uplift acceptance path — a 1-D summary of a 2-D pair that intentionally does NOT beat the raw operand marginal sum (the elementary library is representationally blind to the non-monotone inner; that is the whole reason the prewarp gate exists). The cross-fold stability vote (`confirm_recipes_cross_fold`, backlog #15) re-confirms every `unary_binary` recipe against the elementary held-out gate `eng_mi > MI(a)+MI(b)`, which a prewarp recipe STRUCTURALLY fails, so the vote popped it from `engineered_recipes` — at support finalisation the column then has no replayable recipe and is DROPPED from `transform()` output, leaving the prewarp recovery empty (`get_feature_names_out()` engineered set collapsed to a weak univariate `a__qcos2.5` only). The bug stayed LATENT because the voter is a no-op below 2 `unary_binary` recipes; it surfaced once the 2026-06-10 auto-escalation UNDERDELIVERY trigger added a SECOND `unary_binary` recipe (the residual complement `esc_poly_chebyshev_mul(a,b)`) to the same pool, tripping the vote. **Root cause** (git-bisected): `81117aa6` (underdelivery trigger) is the surfacing commit; the latent defect is the vote applying the elementary uplift bar to alternative-acceptance recipes. **Fix:** the vote now detects an ALTERNATIVE-ACCEPTANCE recipe (one whose `extra` carries the `prewarp_<side>_coef` / `gate_med_<side>_median` fit-time spec — i.e. admitted in-fit via the prewarp-uplift / gate path, not the joint-prevalence gate) and confirms it on genuine held-out signal (positive MI) instead of the marginal-sum bar; the elementary leg is unchanged for ordinary library-unary recipes, so the noise/quirk-drop contract is intact (the in-fit prewarp-uplift gate + out-of-sample min-val-corr validation already screen noise, and the non-constant materialisation guard drops dead columns — the vote is a confirmation layer). **Measured:** the prewarp `mul(prewarp(a),prewarp(b))` recovery is restored to the final selection (was dropped); `tests/feature_selection/test_fe_auto_escalation.py` (9 incl. the He3 underdelivery-completion + exact-capture-silence contracts) and `tests/feature_selection/test_fe_stability_vote.py` (now 9, incl. the new `test_alt_acceptance_prewarp_recipe_not_voted_against_marginal_sum`) all pass; the `test_regression_mrmr_prewarp_chunk_clobber.py` transform-replay regression passes. The previously KNOWN-SEPARATE prewarp-RECONSTRUCTION-quality regression on this same fixture is now FIXED (see the next entry).

- **Prewarp ALS now reconstructs against the CONTINUOUS target, restoring per-operand pre-warp fidelity (|corr| 0.88 -> ~1.0; 2026-06-11).** On the non-monotone product `y=(a**3-2a)*(b**2-b)` the per-operand pre-warp `mul(prewarp(a),prewarp(b))` reconstruction quality had regressed from |corr| 0.9696 (`6d18060c`) to 0.877 (`81117aa6`/master), so the engineered feature no longer delivered its predictive lift (downstream 5-fold Ridge R² stuck at ~0.80 vs the ~0.997 true-signal fit; the prewarp-OFF control partially recovered too, shrinking the lever margin). **Root cause:** the rank-1 ALS warp fit / held-out validation / winning-spec reconstruction (`fit_pair_prewarp_als` in `hermite_fe/_hermite_prewarp.py`) is a least-squares solve of `y ~ f(a)*g(b)`; its fidelity depends on the RESOLUTION of the reconstruction target. The 2026-06-10 TARGET-REBIN GUARD (`_mrmr_fit_impl/_fit_impl_core.py`) correctly coarsens the screening target `classes_y` to the 10-bin equal-frequency `quantization_nbins` codes — the MI screen/gates NEED those faithful coarse codes (the guard fixed a degenerate self-referential MDLP encoding that collapsed 83.7% of rows into one bin and dropped genuine CMI ~6x) — but the pair search was feeding those SAME binned codes (`prewarp_y=classes_y`) to the ALS, and a least-squares product reconstruction loses the degree-3 tail when fit to 10-bin codes. Re-binning the already-binned codes finer does NOT help (verified); the ALS needs the continuous target. **Fix (surgical):** thread the RAW CONTINUOUS y into the prewarp ALS fit/validate/score ONLY — a new `prewarp_y_continuous` parameter on `check_prospective_fe_pairs` (`_feature_engineering_pairs/_pairs_core.py`) drives `_prewarp_y_eff` (the ALS reconstruction + held-out-corr validation target) when supplied, while the MI screen and EVERY gate keep using the binned `classes_y` codes unchanged. `_fit_impl` stashes the raw continuous y as the transient `self._fe_prewarp_y_continuous_` (full-n, fit-time only, deleted at fit end, mirroring `_fe_escalation_y_rank_`); `_mrmr_fe_step/_step_core.py` forwards it on both the serial and joblib pair-search call sites. Strictly gated behind `fe_pair_prewarp_enable` (byte-identical no-op when prewarp is off; the target-rebin guard's own purpose for the screen is untouched). **Measured:** prewarp-ON best engineered |corr| recovered 0.877 -> **1.000**; downstream 5-fold Ridge R² recovered to within 0.10 of the true-signal R²; all 8 `test_biz_value_mrmr_pair_prewarp.py` cases pass. The He3 `y=(x0**3-3*x0)*x1` capture is now COMPLETE (held-out R² ~1.0, was throttled to ~0.80), so the auto-escalation underdelivery trigger correctly LEAVES IT ALONE — `test_fe_auto_escalation.py::test_biz_val_escalation_completes_underdelivering_he3_capture` re-framed to `..._skips_complete_he3_capture` (a completely-captured pair must not be escalated, same contract as the He2 leg-3 control); `test_fe_auto_escalation.py` (8) green. The lever-margin pin in `test_fpoly_prewarp_is_the_lever_vs_no_prewarp_control` relaxed 0.30 -> 0.20 to reflect the OFF control's now-higher partial-recovery ceiling (~0.74 via escalation) while ON is a near-exact ~1.0 reconstruction.

### Added

- **PER-GATE FE REJECTION LEDGER — the rejection side of the survivor-only `fe_provenance_` (`fe_rejection_ledger_`, DEFAULT-ON; FS backlog idea #3, 2026-06-11).** The FE pair/candidate search runs every candidate through ~6 gates and SILENTLY drops the rejected ones, so diagnosing "why was this candidate killed?" required hand-instrumenting the search. Every fitted MRMR now carries `fe_rejection_ledger_` — a pandas DataFrame, one row per rejected FE candidate-per-gate, with columns `candidate / operands / operator / gate / observed / threshold / margin / reason / step` — populated at fit-end alongside `fe_provenance_`, plus a `get_fe_rejection_report()` accessor that renders a per-gate count header + the table. The ledger fingers WHICH gate killed each candidate and the MARGIN (`observed - threshold`; negative = missed, e.g. a 0.94-vs-0.97 prevalence ratio records −0.03). **Gates instrumented (all additive — NO decision logic changed; every record reuses values the gate ALREADY computed, the ledger only RECORDS):** the marginal pair-MI prevalence pre-screen + the order-2 maxT permutation-null floor (`_mrmr_fe_step/_step_core.py` pair pre-screen `else` branches), the per-pair engineered-MI prevalence floor (the 0.97 floor) + the marginal-uplift / joint-recovery (abs-MAD) fallback floor (`_feature_engineering_pairs/_pairs_core.py`, exported via a reserved result-dict key `_FE_REJECTION_RESULT_KEY` so it survives the loky-parallel path like the prewarp/gate-med specs), the S5 conditional-MI redundancy gate (harvested from the gate's existing per-name `diagnostics` dict), and the cross-fold stability vote (via a new additive `diagnostics_out` out-param on `confirm_recipes_cross_fold` carrying per-recipe `passes` vs the quorum bar). Recorder + accessor live in the new `_fe_rejection_ledger.py`; the per-fit raw record list `_fe_rejection_records_` is reset at fit start and capped at `FE_REJECTION_LEDGER_CAP=50000` records (a `ledger_capped` marker row is recorded when hit — never silent). **Cost:** record-only (one `list.append` of a small dict per rejected candidate; cProfile confirms no MI / permutation / discretise recompute, sub-µs per call) — ~0 added wall. **Validated** on the canonical poly×raw fixture `y=a²/b + log(c)·sin(d)`: the ledger fingers the marginal pair-MI prevalence floor as the binding killer of the dropped pair candidates (the floor the session diagnosed by hand) with negative margins, alongside a second floor (CMI-redundancy / stability-vote). Pickle-survives; default-ON (transparency, not opt-in). UPDATE (W6, see Added above): the two abs-MAD floor sites in the non-pair-search FE families (`_orthogonal_cluster_basis_fe.py` / `_mi_greedy_fe.py`) — plus the shared `_unified_fe_gate.local_mi_gate` floor — are now instrumented with the same `record_fe_rejection` call (gate `marginal_uplift_floor`). Tests: `tests/feature_selection/test_biz_value_fe_rejection_ledger.py` (unit per-gate margin + real CMI/stability diagnostics round-trip + decisive canonical-fixture biz_value + cProfile ~0-wall + memory-cap + pickle/default-on).
- **Native learnable categorical embeddings for the PyTorch-Lightning MLP / recurrent models (`use_learnable_cat_embeddings`, DEFAULT-ON; 2026-06-11).** Categoricals reach the network as raw integer codes through a per-categorical `nn.Embedding` (factorize -> codes at the fit boundary `_factorize_cats_fit` / `_apply_cat_codes`, the `CategoricalEmbedding` hook in `generate_mlp`, `NeuralNetStrategy.requires_encoding=False`) instead of being one-hot / CatBoostEncoder-encoded upstream; an unseen level at predict routes to a reserved unknown code, and a non-frame / no-cat input is a no-op. Tests: `tests/training/neural/test_estimator_categorical_embeddings_boundary.py`.
- **AUTO-ESCALATION of zero-admission FE pairs to the richer SHIPPED bases (`fe_auto_escalation_enable`, DEFAULT-ON; backlog idea B / #23, 2026-06-10).** A prospective pair that PASSED the pair-MI prescreen (joint-MI ratio gate + order-2 maxT floor) but for which the unary/binary operator search admitted NOTHING used to end in the "FE produced 0 engineered features despite N pair(s) passing the pair-MI gate" WARNING — detected signal, silently abandoned. The FE step now ESCALATES those pairs to two richer shipped basis families and lets the EXISTING gates decide ("escalation proposes, gates decide"): **(1) signal-adaptive orth-poly** — the rank-1 ALS per-operand warp (`fit_pair_prewarp_als`) re-run at `fe_escalation_poly_degree=6` across all four shipped bases (chebyshev/hermite/legendre/laguerre), best basis by held-out rank-1-reconstruction |corr|, with a **PAIR-NESS guard** (the pair reconstruction must beat the best SINGLE-operand 1-D warp by `fe_escalation_pairness_margin=1.15`, else it is a wrapped univariate trend / cross-mix and is not proposed — measured on the weak F2: 6 cross-mix wrappers admitted without the guard, 0 with it, genuine product terms keep ratios ≥ 1.5); **(2) demodulated adaptive-frequency Fourier/chirp** — for a multiplicative pair `y ~ g(a)·b` the shipped held-out multitone detector (`_detect_fourier_freqs_for_col`) is run on `(z01(a), t = y_c·zscore(b))` (`E[t|a] ∝ g(a)`), locking an INNER frequency no library unary can express; the chirp axis covers growing-frequency inners. Candidates must clear the order-2 maxT floor on the Miller-Madow-debiased MI scale, a marginal-permutation floor, and the S5 conditional-MI redundancy gate vs the admitted engineered support. Recipes are standard `unary_binary` + `prewarp` specs — the Fourier mix is the new closed-form `fourier_adaptive` spec replayed by `apply_operand_prewarp` (pure function of x; leak-safe) — so transform()/pickle/stability-vote treat escalated features exactly like default-prewarp pair features. **Measured:** `y=sin(3.7·x0)·x1` (n=5000) — default admitted 0 genuine features (the warning fired); with escalation the genuine `esc_fourier_mul(x0,x1)` is recovered (detected inner frequency 3.72 vs true 3.7; MI 0.434) and replays on fresh rows at corr ≥ 0.99 vs the true signal. NOISE control: 10 seeds × 8 forced pure-noise pairs → 0 proposed, 0 admitted (hard gate). Structurally a no-op (one set-difference) when every surviving pair already produced an admitted column. The escalation's supervised warp fits use a RANK transform of raw y (`_fe_escalation_y_rank_`, transient, cleared at fit end) — the FE step's `classes_y` label codes are not guaranteed ordinal and silently destroy Pearson-validated fits. New module `_fe_auto_escalation.py`; knobs `fe_escalation_max_pairs=8` / `fe_escalation_min_rows=500` / `fe_escalation_min_val_corr=0.15` / `fe_escalation_poly_degree=6` / `fe_escalation_fourier_max_freqs=3` / `fe_escalation_max_candidates_per_pair=3` / `fe_escalation_pairness_margin=1.15`; provenance on `fe_escalation_info_` / `fe_escalation_history_`. Tests: `tests/feature_selection/test_fe_auto_escalation.py`.
- **UNDERDELIVERY trigger for the FE auto-escalation (`fe_escalation_underdelivery_enable`, DEFAULT-ON; 2026-06-10).** Zero-admission alone is not enough: once the target-rebin guard (below) restored honest target codes, the unary/binary search started ADMITTING a weak ENVELOPE capture for the `y=sin(3.7·a)·b` pair (`mul(sin(a),qubed(b))` — the heteroscedastic |b|³ envelope) via the marginal-uplift fallback, which made the pair "delivered" and silently blocked the escalation that recovers the genuine inner frequency. An MI-RATIO bar vs the prescreen `pair_mi` CANNOT detect this (the 2-D prescreen joint MI under-estimates pair information, so the junk capture measures ratio 1.20 — above genuine captures' own scale); the trigger instead tests the LEFTOVER CONDITIONAL MI `CMI(joint(a,b) codes; y | best admitted column's codes)` with three legs: (1) above the conditional-permutation null's quantile floor (same-bias null), (2) debiased excess ≥ `fe_escalation_underdelivery_excess_frac=0.05` of the captured MI, and (3) a **discretisation-residual control** — even a functionally COMPLETE capture leaves leftover CMI in the 2-D joint because its own `nbins` quantile code is coarse, so the joint's leftover must also exceed `fe_escalation_underdelivery_self_ratio=3.0` × the capture's OWN finer-binning refinement `CMI(capture@2·nbins; y | capture@nbins)` (measured: complete captures 0.70–2.44, the sin envelope junk 14.6). Escalated proposers for such pairs fit the **binned-mean RESIDUAL** of the rank-y target given the existing capture (nonparametric — removes ANY function of the capture, crucially the rank-vs-raw monotone remap a linear residual leaves behind, which otherwise yields a 0.72-val-corr remap candidate worth +0.0008 held-out R²), so only genuinely-MISSING signal can be proposed; all admission gates unchanged. Per-fit dedup ledger (`_fe_escalation_done_pairs_`): a pair escalated once is never re-escalated in a later FE step of the same fit. **Measured:** sin fixture end-to-end — the genuine `esc_fourier_mul(x0,x1)` is recovered (replay corr 0.995 vs truth) and the final selection DROPS the junk envelope; He3(x0)·x1 — the default prewarp capture is genuinely incomplete (held-out R² 0.796 of ~0.99; 0.40 nats leftover CMI) and the residual-fit escalation adds a complementary `esc_chirp` feature worth **+0.009 held-out R²** on fresh rows; EXACT capture (`y=x0·x1`) — leg 3 keeps the pair out of escalation entirely (0 esc features); noise — unchanged 0 admissions. Trigger cost in the every-pair-delivers common case: stride-subsampled (≤20k rows) binning + an 8-permutation conditional null per delivered pair (~ms scale; the heavier machinery runs only when the trigger fires).

### Fixed

- **optbinning IV feature-selection pipelines crashed under scikit-learn 1.6+ (`get_binningprocess_featureselectors`; 2026-06-11).** Every `BinningProcess.fit` reaching optbinning's `metrics._check_x_y` raised `TypeError: check_array() got an unexpected keyword argument 'force_all_finite'`: optbinning <= 0.20.x calls the sklearn API `check_array(force_all_finite=...)`, which sklearn deprecated in 1.6 and removed (renamed to `ensure_all_finite`). With sklearn 1.8 installed, all 17 fit-level tests in `tests/feature_selection/test_optbinning_fs_fit.py` (the IV noise-drop gate, the IV-dominates-noise biz_value floors, the categorical CatBoostEncoder path, the memory-cache smoke, and the perfect-separator skip-smell documentation) errored out before any selection logic ran. **Fix:** raise the dependency floor to `optbinning>=0.21` (pyproject `feature_engineering` extra + `requirements.txt`), the first optbinning release that uses `ensure_all_finite`; refreshed the stale compatibility note in `supervised_binning.optimal_bin_edges` (it previously advised pinning `sklearn<1.5`, which is no longer the right remediation). Root cause was third-party version drift between optbinning and sklearn, not an mlframe selection regression. **Regression:** the 17 tests in `tests/feature_selection/test_optbinning_fs_fit.py` (all green after the upgrade; all error pre-upgrade on optbinning 0.20.1).
- **GPU permutation-MI kernel diverged from the CPU selection for NON-BINARY targets (`compute_mi_from_classes_cuda`, P0; 2026-06-11).** The CUDA single-permutation MI reducer indexed the joint histogram with a HARDCODED row stride of `2` (`i*2+j`) and narrowed its marginals + joint frequency to `float32`. The companion histogram kernel `compute_joint_hist_cuda` writes the buffer row-major with stride `nbins_y` (`cx*nbins_y+cy`), so the reducer only read the correct cells for a BINARY target (`nbins_y==2`); for any other target cardinality — multi-class classification, or quantile-binned regression where `nbins_y` runs up to 10 — it read the WRONG joint cells and returned a garbage permutation-null MI (measured abs error up to **2.8e-2**, including mathematically impossible NEGATIVE MI). That garbage systematically over-rejected genuine candidates in the GPU permutation gate, silently diverging `MRMR(use_gpu=True)` selection from the CPU path. **Fix:** index with stride `nbins_y` (`i*nbins_y+j`) and use `double` throughout, matching the bit-exact CPU njit `compute_mi_from_classes` (now agrees to ~1e-17). Also reallocate the pooled `joint_counts` buffer whenever the target-bin width CHANGES (not only grows): the module-singleton pool persists across fits, and a wider leftover buffer from a prior fit would make `joint_counts[:nbins_x,:nbins_y]` a non-contiguous slice whose true stride is the allocated width, corrupting both the histogram write and the MI read. **Regression:** `tests/feature_selection/test_gpu_cpu_mi_selection_equivalence.py::test_compute_mi_from_classes_cuda_matches_cpu` (kernel-unit, the CUDA reducer must equal the CPU njit MI to ~1e-9 for `nbins_y in {2,3,5,7,10}` — FAILS pre-fix on all five with abs diff up to 1.6e-2, PASSES after) + `::test_mrmr_gpu_cpu_selection_identical` (end-to-end: forced-GPU MRMR selects byte-identically to forced-CPU on regression + classification fixtures) + `::test_mrmr_gpu_cpu_selection_identical_adversarial` (4 near-tie/gate-epsilon/p=60-accumulation-order/dispatcher-edge fixtures × 2 seeds at the tighter `full_npermutations=128` — the tightest tie under which a cc 6.1 GPU still selects byte-identically; ALL identical, no new divergence). Numbers: `D:/Temp/cov_item1_results.md`.
- **`MRMR(use_gpu=True)` crashed reaching the FE pair-search via a cupy `classes_y_safe` (P0; 2026-06-11).** `screen_predictors` overloaded a single `classes_y_safe` local across two consumers with incompatible array-backend needs: the FE step threads the returned `classes_y_safe` into the batched MI noise-gate, whose njit kernels (CPU fallback and the bit-identical GPU twin that does `np.asarray` + a numba Fisher-Yates shuffle) REQUIRE a host numpy array; the confirm / baseline-eval GPU branches want a pre-warmed CuPy device buffer to skip the H2D copy. On the GPU path the local was reassigned to a CuPy array and returned to the FE step, so any `use_gpu=True` fit that reached the FE pair-search crashed with `TypingError: Cannot determine Numba type of <class 'cupy.ndarray'>`. **Fix:** keep an unconditional host buffer `classes_y_safe_host` (== `classes_y_safe` on the CPU path) for the return, and let only the CuPy device buffer flow into `ctx` for `mi_direct_gpu`. **Regression:** the same end-to-end selection-identity tests above exercise the FE pair-search on the GPU path (engineered candidates `add(exp(a),sin(b))` / `b__relu_*` / `div(neg(a),sqrt(b))` are recovered identically by forced-GPU and forced-CPU), which would not even run pre-fix.
- **Four fuzz-bug-hunt fixes at 1k rows, each with a regression test (2026-06-10/11).** (1) MLP/recurrent `_apply_cat_codes` crashed on a pandas Categorical-dtype column — `Series.map` on a Categorical returns a Categorical, so the reserved-unknown-code `fillna` raised "Cannot setitem on a Categorical with a new category"; it now maps the object values. (2) The MRMR hinge change-point protection gate crashed (`could not convert string to float`) when a raw categorical was in the selected set under `skip_categorical_encoding` — non-numeric selected columns are now excluded from the numeric R² baseline design. (3) The test-split transform gate handed a raw (un-reduced) frame to an FS-trained model (`LightGBMError: number of features in data (7) != training (4)`) because it compared the raw width to the selector's `n_features_in_` (which a zero-variance pre-filter shrinks below the raw width); it now keys off the selector's OUTPUT width (`_selector_output_columns`). (4) MRMR `stability_selection_method='cluster'`/`'complementary_pairs'` blanket-coerced the feature matrix to float64 and crashed on a raw categorical (silently falling back to classic); the helpers now cluster only the numeric columns (a categorical becomes a singleton cluster) and feed the bootstrap selector dtype-preserved frame rows. Also: a KTC-dispatcher source-inspection test repointed to `_feature_engineering_pairs/_pairs_dispatch` after that subpackage split.
- **F2 e2e selection test reframed to the HONEST rational-cross-mix behaviour — the stale assertion was the test, NOT a bug (`test_user_f2_e2e_recovers_genuine_drops_noise_and_cross_signal`, 2026-06-11).** `tests/feature_selection/test_fe_cmi_redundancy_gate.py::test_user_f2_e2e_recovers_genuine_drops_noise_and_cross_signal[20000]` and `[30000]` failed on `origin/master`. A DISCRIMINATING investigation (test ALONE 3x vs LAST-in-file) settled two contradicting prior diagnoses: the recovered `support_` is **BYTE-IDENTICAL isolated and in-suite**, so this is a genuine deterministic selection result (Diagnosis B), NOT test-order shared-state pollution (Diagnosis A refuted). On the user's exact F2 `y=0.2*a**2/b + f/5 + log(c*2)*sin(d/3)` the default pipeline recovers the pure `(a,b)` form `div(sqr(a),neg(b))` AND folds the `(c,d)` signal into a single second-step cross-mix `sub(invcbrt(add(reciproc(c),invsquared(d))), log(div(sqr(a),neg(b))))`. On a matched 10-bin grid that cross-mix carries marginal MI **1.05 to y (~46% of H(y)=2.30 nats)** — DOMINATING the pure `(c,d)` parent's 0.535 (~23%) — because F2's `y` is the ADDITIVE sum of an `(a,b)` term and a `(c,d)` term, so one feature built from a `(c,d)`-operand AND an `(a,b)`-operand captures BOTH additive components. GIVEN the cross-mix is selected, the pure `(c,d)` form is genuinely conditionally redundant, so the post-FE greedy re-selection CORRECTLY drops it (confirmed: with `fe_max_steps=1`, no second composite step, the pure `(c,d)` parent survives — the drop is rational subsumption by the higher-MI cross-mix, not screening, not a vocabulary gap). The cross-mix is the RATIONAL MAX-MI pick. An "information-destroying composite" FE-bug hypothesis was implemented and DISPROVEN by measurement (the composite's real MI is 1.05, not a low value a flawed standalone reconstruction had suggested) and fully reverted — prod code is unchanged. **Reframe (no skip/xfail/masking):** assertion (2) "a GENUINE (c,d) form is recovered as PURE c,d operands" was the stale half; it now accepts the `(c,d)` signal in ANY form (pure `(c,d)` OR a cross-mix carrying the `(a,b)` operands too) and adds a PRINCIPLED-SUBSUMPTION check that the selected cross-mix is STRICTLY MORE informative about y (matched-bin MI) than the pure `(c,d)` reference `log(c)*sin(d)` it absorbed — so dropping the pure form is provably the correct max-MI choice. The old `len(eng_cmi) <= len(eng_ratio)` count assertion was ALSO stale (n=30000: honest `eng_cmi=6 > eng_ratio=3`, the hinge-leg stage adds four `d__relu_*` legs the legacy ratio path lacks); removed in favour of the subsumption MI check. Result: both params PASS isolated AND in-suite; the file goes 21+1F -> **22 passed**. Numbers: `D:/Temp/f2_resolve_results.md`.
- **Raw-redundancy drop no longer EMPTIES the selection by crediting raw operands to an UN-REPLAYABLE nested-engineered subsumer (P0 value bug, 2026-06-11).** On the canonical golden composite `y=a**2/b + f/5 + log(c)*sin(d)` at moderate n (n=8000, `random_seed=42`) `MRMR.fit` returned an EMPTY `support_` — `get_feature_names_out()==[]` and `transform()` shape `(n, 0)` — so the downstream model got ZERO features and could not even train (sklearn `ValueError: Found array with 0 feature(s)`). ROOT CAUSE: `drop_redundant_raw_operands` (the n-invariant conditional-redundancy sweep) credited ALL four raw operands `a,b,c,d` as "fully subsumed by a strictly-more-informative combination child" whose anchor was the NESTED-engineered survivor `add(prewarp(div(sqr(a),abs(b))),neg(mul(log(c),sin(d))))` (MI 1.61, parents themselves engineered). That nested child has NO 1-deep replayable recipe, so it is DROPPED from transform output at support finalisation — the raws were dropped against a child that then ceased to exist, leaving raws-gone AND child-gone = empty selection. A raw cannot be redundant given a feature that will not exist at predict time. **Fix:** the sweep now takes a `replayable_eng_names` set (the fit threads `set(engineered_recipes.keys())` from the call site) and restricts the subsumer/anchor set to engineered survivors that WILL survive into `transform` output; when no replayable anchor remains it keeps all raw operands. `replayable_eng_names=None` preserves the legacy trust-every-survivor behaviour for callers that guarantee replayability. **Measured:** post-fix the same fit returns `['a','d','b','c']` (4 raw features) instead of empty; the LEGITIMATE subsumed-operand drop is untouched (the `a**2/b` denominator `b` still drops when its `div(neg(a),sqrt(b))` ratio child IS replayable). **END-TO-END VALUE PROOF** (the FE pipeline's downstream-accuracy contract, `tests/feature_selection/test_biz_value_mrmr_fe_downstream_delta.py`): with a leak-safe train/test split (FE fit on train, replayed on test) and a LINEAR downstream model, the recovered composites lift held-out accuracy on genuinely NON-ADDITIVE signal and are HARMLESS on raw-linear signal — golden `y=a**2/b+log(c)*sin(d)` Ridge **R2 +0.125** (FE-on 0.822 vs raw-only 0.697), polynomial-interaction LogReg **AUC +0.127**, heavy-tail log-multiplicative LogReg **AUC +0.038**, raw-linear LogReg **AUC ~0.000** (FE does not crowd out raws). The composites' value is specifically LINEAR usability (a gradient-boosted tree models the interactions natively, so its delta is ~0 — the composites are not redundant bloat). Regression: `tests/feature_selection/test_biz_value_mrmr_fe_canonical.py::test_canonical_fit_never_returns_empty_selection_at_moderate_n` (e2e, FAILS pre-fix with `support_=[]`, PASSES after) + `::test_redundancy_drop_keeps_raws_when_subsumer_is_unreplayable_nested` + `::test_redundancy_drop_replayable_anchor_still_drops_subsumed` (unit, both legs). Also reframed `::test_subsumed_ratio_operands_drop_at_small_n` to separate the load-bearing subsumed-operand-drop contract (asserted at every n) from the pure-noise-`e` exclusion (a small-n screening-significance question, reliable only at n>=2000; the negligible `0.01*e` term's column crosses the screen floor on some data-seeds at n=1000 — verified across seeds). Numbers: `D:/Temp/cov_item6_results.md`.
- **`MRMR.fit` no longer MUTATES the caller's input DataFrame — sklearn fit-must-not-mutate-input violation + silent cross-fit corruption (P1, 2026-06-11).** `_fit_impl` injects temporary `targ_*` columns into the working pandas frame (`X.loc[:, target_names] = vals`) AND the default-on FE pipeline appends engineered columns IN PLACE (`X[name] = ...`, the hinge / cat-FE generators). The `targ_*` injection was reversed in the `fit` `finally` block, but the engineered FE columns were NEVER removed — so a caller-supplied frame came back with `['a','b'] -> ['a','b','a__relu_gt-0.738167','a__relu_lt-0.738167', ...]` permanently appended. Beyond the contract violation this SILENTLY CORRUPTS a frame REUSED across fits: the 2nd fit's FE builds on the 1st fit's leaked columns → a different post-FE X content → the `_FIT_CACHE` / replay signature no longer matches, so the cache-replay path is skipped and the engineered selection diverges (measured: a 2nd fit on the polluted frame engineered `a__relu_gt-0.738167__haar_j1k0` ON TOP of the leaked column — a nested name a fresh-copy fit never produces). This is exactly why `tests/feature_selection/test_replay_fitted_state_isolation.py` failed on pristine master (`test_support_ndarray_frozen_to_prevent_silent_corruption`, `test_ndarray_sharing_preserved_for_density`): fitting A then B on the SAME frame left A's leaked FE columns in it, so B's content hash differed → B never hit the `_FIT_CACHE` replay path → its `support_` was neither shared with nor frozen against A's. **Fix at the BOUNDARY** (the public `MRMR.fit` wrapper, NOT the FE-step machinery which is being actively re-split): the wrapper copies a pandas input frame ONCE so every downstream append (targets + engineered) lands on an internal copy and the caller's frame is never touched. Polars input is immutable (`with_columns` returns a new frame) and numpy arrays carry no column index — neither is mutated by our code, so only pandas needs isolating. Under pandas ≥ 3.0 Copy-on-Write (always on) the copy is a SHALLOW `copy(deep=False)` that fully isolates column appends AND in-place cell writes because any write triggers a per-block CoW — cost is data-size-INDEPENDENT at **~0.06 ms** even on the wide p=299 path (cProfile: 0.059 ms median at n=2000, 0.074 ms at n=20000); on older pandas without CoW it falls back to a deep copy (~11 ms at n=20k/p=299, negligible vs the multi-minute FE pipeline). **Selection is BYTE-IDENTICAL** before vs after (only the side-effect was removed): `support_` + `get_feature_names_out()` match exactly across the canonical create/keep/drop + composite-F1 fixtures (`np.array_equal`). Regression: `tests/feature_selection/test_mrmr_input_not_mutated.py` (caller columns/shape/values/dtypes unchanged after fit; 2nd fit on a reused frame == fresh-copy fit; polars-input sentry) — FAILS on the pre-fix per-frame mutation and PASSES after; the two `test_replay_fitted_state_isolation.py` tests now pass.
- **S5 CMI redundancy gate no longer FALSELY REJECTS a genuinely complementary but individually WEAKER engineered feature (`fe_engineered_cmi_significance_escape_margin`, DEFAULT-ON; 2026-06-11).** Adversarial probe of the default `fe_acceptance="conditional_mi"` gate (`apply_cmi_redundancy_gate`). The relative-gap leg-2 bar is `retain_frac * min(admitted_excess)` — a RELATIVE separator anchored to the WEAKEST admitted feature's debiased excess. When the admitted set is dominated by ONE strong seed, that bar collapses to a large ABSOLUTE CMI threshold `TAU * seed_excess`, and a genuinely COMPLEMENTARY feature that is merely weaker than the seed is dropped `redundant_below_rel_bar`. Repro: one strong driver + several INDEPENDENT weak drivers (n=20000, 6/6 seeds) — each weak driver provably non-redundant (`MI(weakWk; strongS) ~= 0.004`, statistically independent of the support), each clearing its OWN conditional-permutation floor by ~20x (so leg-1 significance passes decisively — it is real signal, not estimation noise), yet ALL six dropped as redundant, costing **~3% held-out R² (0.9988 -> 0.9680)** on a real `HistGradientBoostingRegressor`. "Much weaker than the strongest selected feature" is NOT the same as "redundant"; the leg-2 bar by itself conflated the two. Fix: a STRONG-SIGNIFICANCE ESCAPE — a candidate whose observed CMI clears its own conditional-permutation floor by at least `significance_escape_margin` (default 3.0) is admitted even when its debiased excess is below the relative bar, because robust conditional significance proves the information is genuinely NEW (a truly redundant feature's CMI collapses to ~its floor: measured `cmi/floor` 1.0–1.4 for the spurious cross-signal `sub` vs 20–340 for genuine complementary features, so 3.0 sits with ~2x margin on BOTH sides). The escape requires `passes_floor` first (leg 1), so it only ever loosens leg 2, never leg 1, and opens NO false-ADMIT path: a monotone remap of an admitted driver has identical quantile bins -> `CMI(remap; y | driver) == 0` -> caught at the floor itself (`redundant_below_floor`), never reaching the escape. The redundant `sub` (cmi/floor 1.03–1.42 < 3.0) is STILL rejected at every n on both canonical formulas — all 13 pre-existing gate-function unit tests stay green. New knob `MRMR(fe_engineered_cmi_significance_escape_margin=3.0)` (set <= 1 to disable -> pure two-leg behaviour, which reproduces the false reject); threaded into the gate call in `_mrmr_fe_step/_step_core.py`. Regression: `tests/feature_selection/test_fe_cmi_redundancy_gate.py` — `test_complementary_weak_feature_not_falsely_rejected[0|1|2]` (the false-reject, FAILS pre-fix), `test_strong_significance_escape_is_decisive_for_weak_complementary`, `test_escape_disabled_reproduces_false_reject`, `test_escape_does_not_admit_monotone_redundant_remaps` (no new false-admit). Numbers: `D:/Temp/cov_item5_results.md`.
- **S5 CMI redundancy ACCEPTANCE gate: BOUNDED cost + exact-partition dedup under a MANY-ENGINEERED load (`fe_engineered_cmi_max_candidates`, DEFAULT 64; 2026-06-11).** Stress-testing the default `fe_acceptance="conditional_mi"` gate (`apply_cmi_redundancy_gate`, `filters/_fe_cmi_redundancy_gate.py`) with DOZENS-to-HUNDREDS of engineered candidates (the regime the synergy bootstrap / GBM seeder produce) surfaced two real defects. **BUG 1 — UNBOUNDED O(K²) COST.** The greedy gate re-scores every still-remaining candidate against the admitted support in EACH greedy round, and each scoring runs a 25-permutation within-stratum conditional-permutation null with a per-stratum Python shuffle — with NO candidate-count guard. Measured 1.9 s @ 9 cands → 27 s @ 99 cands, a clean 2.0x per doubling of K (pure quadratic). **BUG 2 — EQUIV-CLASS STARVATION + WASTED COST.** A monotone/linear remap of a feature bins to the IDENTICAL equi-frequency partition (rank-invariant binning; a decreasing remap yields the reversed-but-identical partition), so the greedy correctly admits exactly one and rejects the rest — but at full per-round permutation-null cost, AND a plain top-M-by-marginal-MI cap could STARVE a genuine driver: when many tied-marginal-MI remaps of the strongest driver fill the cap, every form of a weaker (but genuine) driver is dropped before the greedy runs (observed: at K=200 with tied-MI remaps the weakest two of three genuine drivers lost ALL their forms). **Fix:** (a) collapse each exact-partition equivalence class to its highest-marginal-MI representative (deterministic name tie-break) BEFORE the cap — labelled `reason="redundant_partition_duplicate"` — so the cap operates on DISTINCT partitions and every genuine driver keeps a representative while redundant siblings never pay the greedy cost; (b) PRE-RANK the surviving distinct-partition pool by marginal MI and keep the top-`fe_engineered_cmi_max_candidates` (default 64, `<=0` disables) before the greedy, bounding cost to O(M²). The dropped tail is `reason="dropped_cost_cap"`. **Measured (n=20000):** cost flattened from O(K²) to ~flat (1.0x–1.6x per doubling; ~0.5 s @ 200 cands vs 32 s unguarded); driver starvation eliminated (0/3 drivers lost at K=200, was 2/3); the gate now admits the CANONICAL form over arbitrary noisy remaps; the TRUE false-admit rate (two+ forms of the same driver admitted) is 0 and false-reject of genuinely-complementary drivers is 0 as K scales; the CMI estimate stays sane (all debiased-excess finite & non-negative) as the conditioning support grows (12/12 independent product drivers admitted). The fix preserves every existing gate decision: all 13 gate-function unit tests in `test_fe_cmi_redundancy_gate.py` are byte-stable (the dedup+cap fire only on pools WIDER than 64 distinct partitions, which the 3-candidate validation suite never hits). Old pickles default to the same 64-cap (re-fits of any pool ≤ 64 distinct partitions are byte-identical). Knob wired through `MRMR.fe_engineered_cmi_max_candidates` → `_step_core` call site → gate. Regression: `tests/feature_selection/test_fe_cmi_gate_many_engineered.py` (11 tests: partition-dedup one-rep-per-driver, no-starvation under tied-MI cap, canonical-preferred, cost-bounded-as-K-grows, cap-disable escape hatch, cap-keeps-genuine-drops-noise-tail, MRMR knob default + get_params round-trip).
- **Cat-FE `factorize` / `target_encoding` replay no longer all-zeros STRING / Categorical source columns at transform — silent train/serve skew (2026-06-10).** The FS-side analog of the neural `_apply_cat_codes` bug (4b299e25). A cat-interaction recipe (`kind="factorize"` / `"target_encoding"`) stores `src_names` pointing at the ORIGINAL categorical columns and a lookup table keyed on the fit-time INTEGER codes that `categorize_dataset` assigns (`.cat.codes` category-order for Categorical, `pd.factorize` first-appearance for object/string). At `transform`, `_extract_column` returns the RAW string/category values and `_coerce_to_int_with_nan_handling` ran `vals.astype(np.int64)`, which raises on strings and fell through to an all-zero clip fallback — so EVERY test row got code `0`, the `(a,b)` lookup collapsed to the single `(0,0)` cell, and the engineered cat-synergy column came out CONSTANT at serving time. End-to-end repro (3×3 cat cross carrying all XOR signal): the `kway(cat_a__cat_b)` feature returned 1 unique value at `transform` (all-zero) instead of the 9 cell codes the fit-time lookup was built on; the strongest selected feature was silently destroyed for any consumer of `MRMR.transform` on a raw frame. Fix: the fit-time `raw_value -> code` mapping is now captured once per categorical source column (`build_category_code_map`, reproducing the discretiser BIT-EXACTLY: `.cat.codes` for Categorical, `pd.factorize(use_na_sentinel=True)` for object/string, AND the NaN `+1` shift — when the training column had any NaN, `categorize_dataset` shifts `-1`(NaN)`-> 0` and real categories `-> 1..K`, so the map mirrors that and routes NaN cells to the dedicated `0` bin) and stamped onto each cat-FE recipe's `extra["cat_code_maps"]` right after `run_cat_interaction_step` (where the raw `_x_for_cat` frame is in scope); `_coerce_to_int_with_nan_handling` takes an optional `cat_code_map` and maps raw values through it for categorical/string sources (NaN -> NaN-bin when present, else unseen -> `unknown_strategy`). Verified the map-driven replay produces codes BIT-IDENTICAL to `categorize_dataset` across Categorical/object x NaN/no-NaN, and train/serve consistent end-to-end on disjoint holdout (same value-pair -> same code, 0 mismatches; NaN and unseen categories handled without crash). Numeric sources and legacy pickles (no `cat_code_maps`) keep the byte-identical int-cast path. `EngineeredRecipe` gains a `with_extra(**updates)` helper to attach late-bound metadata onto the frozen recipe (its `extra` is a read-only `MappingProxyType`); pickle round-trips the nested map. Regression: `tests/feature_selection/test_mrmr_cov_sweep_four_paths.py` (categorical replay-not-constant + holdout-consistency for both dtypes; `build_category_code_map` unit; plus coverage pins for binary/multiclass classification recovery, wide p=299 signal recovery, and NaN-feature recovery + raw-NaN-preservation + NaN-target-raises).
- **Cat-FE `factorize` / `target_encoding` replay: the NaN `+1` code shift is now a BLOCK property, fixing off-by-one codes on a NaN-FREE partner column (combined-path follow-up, 2026-06-11).** The 2026-06-10 fix above built each source column's `cat_code_maps` replay table with a PER-COLUMN NaN check. But `categorize_dataset` factorises ALL categorical columns as ONE 2-D block and applies the `+1` shift to the WHOLE block when ANY column in it has a NaN (`_discretization_dataset.py`: `_has_nan = (new_vals < 0).any()` over the full block then `new_vals = new_vals + 1`). So a NaN-FREE categorical paired in the same frame with a NaN-bearing one ALSO gets its fit-time codes shifted to `1..K`, while the per-column map said `0..K-1` — every code off-by-one at transform for that partner. This is the SAME train/serve-skew bug class as the parent fix, for the mixed-block case the original sweep never exercised (its cat fixtures were all NaN-free or had NaN in BOTH cat columns symmetrically). End-to-end repro through real `MRMR.fit/transform` (wide p≈292, NaN in cat_a + a numeric signal, binary + multiclass): the NaN-free Categorical partner `cat_b`'s engineered cell-codes were off-by-one at serving, skewing thousands of held-out rows for the `kway(cat_a__cat_b)` feature. Fix: `build_category_code_map(values, block_has_nan=None)` gains a block-level override — when `block_has_nan=True`, real categories of a NaN-free column are shifted to `base + 1` (NO `_NAN_CODE_KEY`, since that column owns no NaN cell and never receives code `0`); `block_has_nan=None` keeps the legacy per-column behaviour (correct for a single-column block). The stamping site in `_mrmr_fit_impl.py` computes the block flag ONCE over `_x_for_cat.select_dtypes(include=("category","object","string","bool"))` (mirroring `categorize_dataset`'s block selection exactly) and threads it into every map build. Verified bit-exact vs `categorize_dataset` across object/Categorical × NaN-on-one/both/neither, and serve-time pair mismatches drop from thousands to 0 end-to-end. Regression: `tests/feature_selection/test_mrmr_cov_sweep_four_paths.py` — `test_block_nan_shift_applies_to_nanfree_partner_column[Categorical|object_string]` (unit, bit-exact replay), `test_combined_cat_nan_wide_clf_no_serve_skew[binary|multiclass]` (combined cat+NaN+wide+clf e2e, 0 serve skew), `test_combined_nan_does_not_break_numeric_relevance`, `test_nan_in_cat_at_serve_not_fit_and_vice_versa` (NaN/shift train↔serve asymmetry edges). All FAIL on the pre-fix per-column path and PASS after.
- **Target-rebin guard: the adaptive `nbins_strategy` (default `"mdlp"`) no longer degrades the TARGET encoding (2026-06-10).** The per-column adaptive bin strategy is meant for FEATURE columns; applied to the injected TARGET it is SELF-REFERENTIAL (MDLP bins y supervised on y) and on a heavy-tailed continuous y produced a DEGENERATE encoding — measured on the F2 fixture (`y = 0.2a²/b + f/5 + log(2c)·sin(d/3)`, n=20000): 37 bins with **83.7% of all rows in ONE bin** (legacy quantile: 10 × 2000). Every downstream MI/CMI (screening, pair gates, FE acceptance) is computed against those target codes, so the bulk of the signal was invisible — the genuine (c,d) term's measured conditional MI dropped ~6x (0.209 → 0.034 nats) and its debiased excess fell below the S5 relative bar. Continuous targets (raw unique count > `quantization_nbins`) are now re-binned with the legacy `quantization_method`/`quantization_nbins` equal-frequency codes right after `categorize_dataset`; classification/discrete labels are untouched; `nbins_strategy=None` fits are untouched.
- **Univariate FE stages no longer build 1-deep-unreplayable NESTED recipes on engineered columns (2026-06-10).** The default-ON wavelet stage (and the conditional-dispersion / rankgauss stages — same bug class, fixed in the same pass) scoped their default column set over the ALREADY-AUGMENTED frame, so e.g. a Haar leg was built ON an engineered Fourier column (`x0__p2sin1__haar_j3k5`); when the engineered parent was not itself selected its recipe was pruned and `transform()` raised `KeyError('x0__p2sin1')` (the cross-fold stability vote was also silently skipping folds on the same error). All three stages now exclude the `hybrid_orth_features_` ledger from their default scope (the hinge/extra-basis stages' existing guard), keeping every emitted recipe 1-deep and replayable.

- **SUFFICIENT-SUMMARY EARLY-STOP for the MRMR Feature-Engineering search (`fe_sufficient_summary_early_stop`, DEFAULT-ON; backlog #22, 2026-06-10).** The user's "compare-to-theoretical-max" idea, realised cheaply via a Data-Processing-Inequality (DPI) residual test. After each MRMR feature SELECTION (once per fit/screen pass, NOT per candidate pair), a CHEAP ridge of `y` on the SMALL selected set `E_hat[y|selected]` (1–5 cols — engineered features linearise the signal so a linear fit captures `E[y|selected]`; the design is tiny because the SELECTED set is small) forms the residual `r = y − E_hat`. The FE search STOPS iff `MI(r; x_j) ≤ the maxT permutation null` for EVERY raw feature AND the residual is small relative to `y` (`Var(r)/Var(y) ≤ fe_sufficient_summary_residual_frac`, the H(y)-relative size guard). **Why it is correct (DPI):** any future engineered candidate is a deterministic function of the raws, so by the Data-Processing Inequality it cannot carry more MI with `r` than the raws do — a residual that is pure noise w.r.t. the observables certifies the selection reached `I(observables;
