y)`, the THEORETICAL MAX, so the remaining search is provably pointless. **The early-stop NEVER changes the final selection** (it only skips work that could find nothing): on the genuine multi-signal F1 fixture `y=a²/b + f/5 + log(c)·sin(d)` the stop conservatively does NOT fire (a raw still carries residual signal) → the selection is BYTE-IDENTICAL with the early-stop on vs off. **WORK-CUT:** on a fully-recoverable linear signal the stop fires before any FE step (0 FE steps vs 2 with it off), and the feature the off run would have added carries < 1e-3 incremental R² over the kept raws (the DPI guarantee made concrete). **CONSERVATIVE:** fires only when BOTH the variance guard AND the all-raws maxT test pass, so a genuine unfound second signal — INCLUDING a NONLINEAR leftover the linear `E_hat` underfits, caught by `MI(r; raw)` (the leftover test is MI, not a linear score, precisely so a non-linear residual a raw still explains BLOCKS the stop) — keeps the search going. **Decisive design points (the user iterated to this exact shape):** the residual is tested against the FULL selected set (NOT the 1-D top feature — `y=a+b` stops only after BOTH a,b are selected, never after a alone); the residual-size guard is the GRID-FREE unexplained-variance fraction `Var(r)/Var(y)` (faithful realisation of "small relative to H(y)" without the equi-frequency/fixed-grid binning artefacts that read ~1.0 for any non-constant residual); ridge regularisation handles collinear/redundant selected columns. Reuses the SHIPPED MI kernels + maxT permutation null (`pooled_permutation_null_gain_floor`). **NO FALSE STOP** on a pure-noise target (the variance guard holds where the maxT test alone would pass); **NO PREMATURE STOP** with an unfound second signal. **COST 0.06% of fit** (cProfile — one ridge solve + a few maxT shuffles over a handful of raws). New module `_fe_sufficient_summary.py` (pure helper `sufficient_summary_reached` + MRMR adapter `check_sufficient_summary_for_mrmr`), wired into the greedy FE loop in `_mrmr_fit_impl` after `screen_predictors` returns `selected_vars`, before `_run_fe_step`; verdict surfaced on `self.sufficient_summary_`, FE-step count on `self._fe_steps_executed_`. Knobs `fe_sufficient_summary_early_stop=True` / `fe_sufficient_summary_residual_frac=0.25` / `fe_sufficient_summary_maxt_permutations=25` / `fe_sufficient_summary_maxt_quantile=0.95` / `fe_sufficient_summary_ridge_alpha=1e-3`; set the first False to disable. Triad: `tests/feature_selection/test_fe_sufficient_summary.py` (12 unit: positive F1-composite + both-operands, negative pure-noise + unfound-signal, adversarial collinear/looks-complete-alone/weak-3rd-term/heteroscedastic/nonlinear-leftover, degenerate guards) + `tests/feature_selection/test_biz_value_mrmr_sufficient_summary.py` (work-cut + selection byte-identity + no-false/premature-stop matrix + cProfile-negligible). Numbers: `D:/Temp/earlystop_results.md`.
- **Heavy-tail-aware ROBUST WARP-COEFFICIENT FITTING for the 1-D operand pre-warp (`MLFRAME_ROBUST_WARP_FIT`, DEFAULT-ON; backlog #17, 2026-06-10).** **VALIDATED + SHIPPED (default-on, heavy-tail-gated).** The per-operand pre-warp `fit_operand_prewarp` (the closed-form 1-D orthogonal-polynomial warp the unary/binary pair search uses) fit its coefficients with ORDINARY least squares (`np.linalg.lstsq`), whose squared-error loss is dominated by a few extreme rows: under a heavy-tailed / outlier marginal the basis entry `x**k` explodes on an outlier row, so the OLS warp CHASES the outliers instead of recovering the true relationship (measured held-out R² 0.9999 → 0.92 on a monotone cubic with 1.5% injected outliers). The fix swaps the OLS solve for a Huber-IRLS (iteratively-reweighted least squares, `_huber_irls_lstsq` in `hermite_fe/_hermite_robust.py`) that down-weights outlier rows ~1/|r| past 1.345 robust-σ, **GATED on the SAME spike-contamination predicate `_detect_heavy_tail` the robust-axis path already uses** — so a clean / naturally-heavy-tailed (lognormal/Student-t/exponential, continuous tail) column does NOT trip it and is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the legacy OLS fit; only a spike-CONTAMINATED column takes the robust path. The OLS solver is kept under its own name (`_ols_lstsq`, keep-all-kernels rule) and the dispatcher (`fit_basis_coef_robust`) routes by the heavy-tail predicate. **MEASURED WIN (30 seeds, monotone-cubic warp, 1.5% outliers @ 12·IQR, n=2000):** robust beats OLS on held-out R² (vs the TRUE warp) in **30/30 seeds, mean dR² +0.0044, worst-case +0.0020 (NEVER regresses), 0% regressions.** **COMMON-CASE CONTROL:** on 5 clean/naturally-heavy families (uniform/gauss/lognormal/Student-t/exponential) the predicate does not fire → the fitted coefficients are BIT-for-BIT identical with the gate on or off. **NOISE CONTROL:** a robust warp on a pure-noise operand recovers ~0 OOS R² (no manufactured signal). **LEAK-SAFE REPLAY:** the robust fit's MAD-anchored winsor bounds (`winsor_lo/hi`) + a `robust_fit` flag are persisted FLAT in the recipe `extra` (`prewarp_{side}_robust_fit/winsor_lo/winsor_hi`) for provenance/audit; replay is closed-form on `coef` alone (no `y`), so transform-time is byte-identical to fit time. `MLFRAME_ROBUST_WARP_FIT=0` replays the legacy OLS path (independent knob from `MLFRAME_ROBUST_AXIS` — orthogonal robustness layers). **cPROFILE:** the IRLS solve is paid ONLY on contaminated columns (6.2 ms/fit at n=4000, IRLS_MAX_ITER capped at 6 = measured 4-5-iter convergence + margin, halved from the naive 12-iter ceiling with NO coef change); the clean common case adds +0.02 ms (gate never fires). Triad in `tests/feature_selection/test_robust_warp_fit_gate.py` (15 unit: gate default-on + env-override + axis-gate-independence / Huber-IRLS matches-OLS-clean + resists-outliers + degenerate-None / dispatcher clean-byte-identical + heavy-tail-robust+winsor + gate-off-forces-OLS / 1-D prewarp clean-byte-identical + heavy-tail-provenance + natural-heavy-not-robustified) and `tests/feature_selection/test_biz_val_robust_warp_fit.py` (7 biz_value: robust-beats-plain-OOS + clean-byte-identical×5-families + noise-no-recovery). Numbers: `D:/Temp/robust_warp_results.md`.

### Rejected experiments

- **Cross-fit recipe warm-start prior — BENCH-REJECTED, shipped OPT-IN / default-OFF (backlog #20, 2026-06-10).** Premise: within CV / `partial_fit` refits the folds overlap 80%+, yet MRMR re-searches the per-pair polynomial FE from scratch; seeding the CMA-ES with a prior-overlapping fold's surviving coefficients should cut iters-to-converge and stabilise selection. Implemented as `optimise_hermite_pair(cross_fit_prior_seeds=...)` — prior joint-coefficient vectors `concat(coef_a, coef_b)` injected as EXTRA optimiser warm-start seeds (clipped into `coef_range`, size-mismatched seeds silently skipped), the load-bearing CMA-init half of the idea. **The premise does NOT hold against the shipped optimiser.** The per-pair ALS warm-start (`warm_start_als`, default-on) already re-derives the true basin from *this fold's* data each call and lands CMA's `x0` there, so a cross-fold coefficient prior is strictly subsumed. Probe `profiling/bench_warmstart_probe.py` on 5 (and 12) bootstrap subsamples (85% overlap, n=4000, non-monotone-inner product target `(a³−2a)(b²−1)` — the regime where CMA must actually search): **median iters COLD=78 / WARM=79** (the prior seed adds ONE eval and saves ZERO generations), **wall −0.3%…−3.7%** (slightly SLOWER), and **selection NOT byte-identical on 8/12 folds** (6 higher-MI, 2 lower) — the extra seed perturbs the CMA population enough to land on a *different* optimum, failing the idea's "fewer-iters AND identical-or-stabler selection" ship gate on all three legs. The leak-control invariant ITSELF is preserved (the warm winner is always re-scored on the fold's data through the same gates), proven by `test_xfit_warm_start_prior.py::test_seeded_path_rescored_on_this_data`: a strong-target prior cannot manufacture an admission on a noise target (cold None == warm None). Kept opt-in per keep-all-versions; `cross_fit_prior_seeds=None` (default) ⇒ the injection block is a no-op and the warm-start population is BYTE-IDENTICAL to legacy (pinned by `test_default_off_byte_identical`). Do NOT flip default-on without a regime ALS cannot seed. Hook + rejection note in `_hermite_fe_optimise_pair.py`; tests `tests/feature_selection/test_xfit_warm_start_prior.py`; numbers `D:/Temp/warmstart_results.md`.

- **Gradient-interaction (mixed second partials) detector — SHIPPED OPT-IN, default-OFF (backlog #21, 2026-06-10).** New proposer `_gradient_interaction_seeder.py` (`fe_gradient_interaction_enable`, default OFF). Fits ONE smooth differentiable RFF+ridge surrogate on a row sample (≤2000 rows) and proposes the operands of pairs `(a,b)` whose `E[(∂²f/∂xₐ∂x_b)²]` is large — the calculus definition of a non-additive interaction (a sum `g(a)+h(b)` has mixed partial ≡ 0). SAME plug point as the #6 GBM seeder: it widens `numeric_vars_to_consider` (called right after `apply_surrogate_gbm_seeder` in `_run_fe_step`/`_mrmr_fe_step.py`) and the existing order-2 maxT floor + CMI/prevalence gates DECIDE. Carries its OWN self-gate: OOF-R² vs permuted-y (no learning → 0 proposals), a GAM additive-residual baseline (additive targets → ~0 mixed partials), and a permutation-null floor (q99 × 1.2 margin) on the max mixed-partial energy. Two kernels kept (keep-all-kernels): an EXACT closed-form RFF mixed partial (batched, the routed default — byte-equal to the per-pair loop kernel) and a model-agnostic central finite-difference fallback. Size dispatcher `_route_gradient_seeder` routes ON only in `[min_p=8, max_p=200]` (thresholds via kernel_tuning_cache; OFF below trivial, OFF above the O(p²) cost wall). **WIN (n=2000, p=60, `y=sin(x5)·x31+noise`):** the surrogate learns (OOF R² beats permuted-y), the (x5,x31) saddle ranks **#0/1770** and is the **ONLY** pair proposed (real-max energy > q99×1.2 null floor); end-to-end in a live REGRESSION MRMR fit (10-bin ordinal target) the seeder proposes exactly `[(x5,x31)]`. Engineering the proposed product lifts downstream Ridge OOS R² by **> +0.05** vs raw-only. **NOISE control HOLDS (both the raw-continuous AND the discretised ordinal target the gates score):** pure-noise → 0 proposals (OOF gate / null rail), purely additive `x1+…+x8` AND additive-nonlinear `sin(x0)+x1²+|x2|+…` → 0 proposals (the additive-residual baseline absorbs additive structure; mixed partials vanish by calculus). **WHY default-OFF (the decisive negatives):** (1) on the prescribed fixture the #6 GBM split-co-occurrence seeder ALSO ranks (x5,x31) **#0** — modern boosting represents a smooth 2-way product over N(0,1) fine, so the two are **equally good, NOT complementary**, and the GBM does **not** under-rank the saddle (`gbm_underranks=False`); (2) the full self-gated proposer (OOF gate + 12-shuffle permutation null) costs **~8 s** at n=2000/p=60 — too heavy to default-on (the single-fit routed core, surrogate + batched analytic energy, is **~93 ms** after a 5.8× batching speedup over the per-pair loop, but the load-bearing permutation null is the cost); (3) the FE gates score against a DISCRETISED ordinal target, on which the smooth-surrogate signal-to-null margin is thin (on a 2-class binary the median-thresholded `sin·` becomes a sign-product, not a smooth saddle → the detector legitimately does not fire — a real limitation, not a bug). Kept as a module routed OFF / opt-in; raise the flag to enable on regression tasks with smooth/rotated interactions. Triad: `tests/feature_selection/test_gradient_interaction_seeder.py` (13 tests — unit: mixed-partial-high-on-saddle / near-zero-on-additive / analytic==finite-diff (corr > 0.97) / batched==loop kernel (≤1e-9) / OOF-gate-blocks-noise / null-rejects-additive(+nonlinear) / dispatcher-routes-by-size; WIN: proposes-saddle-rank-0; e2e: regression-proposes-saddle + default-OFF-is-noop; biz_value: engineered-saddle-improves-R²; cProfile: core-cost-hotspot). Numbers: `D:/Temp/gradinteract_results.md`.

- **Confidence-weighted / class-balanced MI for FE relevance under imbalance — BENCH-REJECTED, opt-in only (backlog #18, 2026-06-10).** Premise: under heavy class imbalance the plain plug-in `MI(candidate; y)` is majority-dominated, so a feature that separates only the RARE class scores low and gets under-ranked / rejected; fix = inverse-prior class-balanced MI (each class reweighted to equal total mass) for the relevance score, gated on `n_rare` (rare-imbalance-needs-large-n). **The premise does NOT hold for the estimator as implemented.** Inverse-prior balancing is a near-UNIFORM MULTIPLICATIVE rescale of every feature's MI — Kendall tau(plain, balanced) = **0.989** (mean over 40 imbalanced n=20000 frames, min 0.956) — so it almost never changes a RANK-based selection and is invariant to the eng/raw uplift ratio. Across **120 imbalanced frames** (prior ~0.5–6 %, n_rare ≥ 150) the balanced top-5 selection differed from plain in only **13**; across those 13 flips the downstream rare-class metrics were a net-negative coin-flip — **mean dAP −0.0037 (AP improved in 6/13), mean drecall@3% −0.0122** — i.e. the reorders are noise at the weak-feature margin and do NOT track rare-class value. The stratified-balanced-subsample alternative reorders more (63/120) only because subsampling injects estimator variance, and is ALSO net-negative downstream (mean tree-dAP −0.0027, improved 26/63). Mechanistically (verified analytically for binary features): a high-precision rare detector ALWAYS carries higher plain MI than a broad/weak majority-correlated decoy, so plain MI compresses the rare signal's MAGNITUDE (a 0.78-AUC rare separator → plain MI ~0.004 vs balanced ~0.13, ~30×) but preserves its RANK — and a 0.87-AUC rare separator is never out-ranked by pure noise or weak majority decoys (so the idea's win-fixture (a) "plain ranks x_rare BELOW the decoys" cannot be built with honest decoys). Since there is no selection change and no downstream rare-AP improvement, the correction is **default OFF** (the unset `MLFRAME_FE_IMBALANCE_MI` path through `_orthogonal_univariate_fe._mi_classif_batch` is byte-for-byte the plain MI, +0.5 % wall on a single env read). The inverse-prior balanced-MI njit kernel + the prior/n_rare two-sided gate are KEPT (keep-all-kernels rule) as an opt-in scorer reachable via `MLFRAME_FE_IMBALANCE_MI=on` (still self-disables on balanced data and below `n_rare`, both byte-identical to plain); knobs `MLFRAME_FE_IMBALANCE_PRIOR=0.30` / `MLFRAME_FE_IMBALANCE_N_RARE=150`. New module `_orthogonal_univariate_fe/_imbalance_mi.py`; rejection rationale + numbers pinned in its docstring and at the `_mi_classif_batch` hook. Triad: `tests/feature_selection/test_imbalance_mi.py` (13 tests — gate semantics / kernel correctness / the rank-preservation rejection / noise control / default-OFF cost). Numbers: `D:/Temp/imbalmi_results.md`.

- **Robust (Huber-IRLS) substitution INSIDE the rank-1 ALS pair-warp sweep — BENCH-REJECTED (backlog #17, 2026-06-10).** The companion to the SHIPPED 1-D robust prewarp (above): robustify the alternating-least-squares solves in `warm_start_als_seed` / `fit_pair_prewarp_als` (the joint per-operand warp for product-structured pairs `y ~ P(a)·Q(b)`) the same heavy-tail-gated way. It does NOT ship — it is a NET LOSS for the common heavy-tail product case. Unlike the CONVEX 1-D solve, the rank-1 ALS is a NON-CONVEX alternating fit with a sign/scale ambiguity in its factors; per-iteration Huber reweighting destabilises the basin and SHRINKS genuine high-curvature warps whose signal lives in the SAME tail rows the Huber loss down-weights (a quadratic `a**2` warp is flattened toward linear). **30-seed OOS measure (n=2000, axis-robust ON both arms, 2% outliers @ 15·IQR in operand a):** product `a·b` mean dR² **−0.134, 30% of seeds regress >0.01, worst −0.89**; product `a²·b` mean +0.121 BUT 13% regress, worst −0.96 (high variance); product `a³·b` mean −0.004, worst −0.13. A guard — internal OOS train/val split + Huber-trimmed held-out criterion + 5% margin to keep OLS on ties — was prototyped; it CUT but did NOT eliminate the worst-case tail regressions (the val slice carries the same outliers; the ALS sign ambiguity maps near-equal held-out error to very different shapes). Since the policy forbids shipping a regression for the common case, the ALS path stays byte-identical OLS. The rejection rationale + numbers are pinned at the site (`# bench-attempt-rejected (2026-06-10)` in `warm_start_als_seed`) and in the backlog; the `_huber_irls_lstsq` solver stays live for the 1-D prewarp. Numbers: `D:/Temp/robust_warp_results.md`.

- **Isotonic (monotone-constrained) reshaping — BENCH-REJECTED (backlog #14, 2026-06-10).** Proposed as a Family-D `fe_isotonic_enable` sibling to RankGauss: fit `sklearn.isotonic.IsotonicRegression` (both directions, keep better by held-out fit) and emit the monotone step function as a downstream-usability (non-MI) feature. The decisive subsumption test (held-out Ridge R² of `[x, T(x)]` on the monotone link `y=sigmoid(3x)+noise`, 24 seeds) shows isotonic **LOSES to the SHIPPED 5-inner-knot (8-basis) quantile cubic B-spline in ALL 6 (n,noise) cells** [n∈{500,1500,4000} × noise∈{0.15,0.35}], by **−0.0014 … −0.0110 R²** (0/6 isotonic wins). The "monotone prior cuts small-n variance" claim is FALSIFIED against the production spline — the spline wins by the LARGEST margin at the smallest-n / highest-noise cell (n=500, noise=0.35: −0.0110), the opposite of the prediction. Isotonic only beats a 16-inner-knot OVER-parameterised spline that mlframe does not ship (and already chose against — see the supervised-knots bench-reject 2026-06-03). The cubic spline's own regularisation subsumes the monotone prior exactly as it subsumes RankGauss. Complementarity control passes (isotonic loses to the spline on non-monotone `y=x²` by −0.13…−0.29 R²); noise control passes (admits 0). No code / kind / flag was added — the rejection rationale + numbers are pinned at the integration site (`_extra_fe_families.py`, the Family-C RankGauss DPI block) and in the backlog. Numbers: `D:/Temp/isotonic_results.md`.

### Performance

- GPU joint-hist dispatcher: the HW-aware fallback (`_hw_aware_fallback` in `_benchmarks/kernel_tuning_cache/dispatch.py`) now memoises the GPU compute-capability MAJOR version for the process lifetime (`_cached_cc_major`) instead of re-probing it on every kernel-tuning-cache MISS call. The fallback table is keyed only on `cc_major` (immutable per process), but it called `gpu_capability_summary`, which re-reads LIVE free-VRAM by shelling out to `nvidia-smi` via GPUtil (~50 ms subprocess spawn). A cProfile of `mi_direct_gpu_batched` (n=5000, nperm=1024, 5 calls) on a cc 6.1 host showed that subprocess at **0.26 s of 0.578 s = 45% of the GPU-path wall**. Memoising the cc_major probe cuts the same workload to **0.338 s (1.7x, ~42% wall reduction)** and leaves `argsort`-of-uniform permutation generation as the dominant (legitimate) GPU cost. Kernel-variant routing is byte-unchanged (the memo returns the identical fallback dict); selection stays byte-identical (verified by the GPU==CPU equivalence suite). Regression: `tests/feature_selection/test_gpu_cpu_mi_selection_equivalence.py::test_hw_aware_fallback_probes_capability_once` (asserts ≤1 capability probe across 50 lookups — pre-fix probes 99 times / ~7.7 s, post-fix ≤1 / ~1.1 s). At n≤5000 (the 4 GB consumer-GPU fixture cap) the batched GPU permutation path remains slower than the CPU njit path in absolute terms — the GPU win materialises only at large n that does not fit a 4 GB device — but the dispatch-overhead bug above wasted ~45% of whatever time the GPU path did run.
- DCD pairwise-SU hot loop (wasted-work sweep follow-up): fused the per-pair joint histogram straight to the entropy SCALAR via a new `joint_entropy_2var` njit kernel, replacing the `entropy(joint_freqs_2var(...))` two-call form in `_dcd_metrics.pair_su` (the `distance="su"` default path). After the prior `joint_freqs_2var` win, the joint `H(X_a, X_b)` is still the only genuinely per-pair term (341,777 calls on the full scene fit) and EVERY call immediately collapsed the returned normalized-freqs array to a scalar -- so the two-call form allocated a pruned freqs array + a `/ n` normalized array per pair, then `entropy` re-ran the `freqs > 0` mask and built a `log(freqs) * freqs` temporary, all discarded for one float (textbook per-call wasted WORK once only the entropy is wanted). `joint_entropy_2var` walks the joint histogram ONCE and accumulates `-(p * log(p))` directly with NONE of those intermediates. The same fusion replaced the duplicate wasted-work site `friend_graph._joint_entropy_2vars` (the CPU friend-graph edge metric + the GPU twin's CPU reference), which built the full `merge_vars` 2-var output then `entropy`d it. Microbench ~1.24x per pair (8.34 -> 6.74 us incl. the downstream entropy; `D:/Temp/ww_micro_jointentropy.py`); full scene 2407x299 MRMR fit 555.55s -> 538.21s (-3.1%, both `--no-profile`, njobs=-1). BIT-IDENTICAL: verified max-abs-diff EXACTLY 0.0 vs `entropy(joint_freqs_2var(...))` AND `entropy(merge_vars(...)[1])` across uniform / sparse / heavy-skew / constant-column data at n=37/600/2407 (960 cases) plus unequal per-column bin counts, by `test_joint_entropy_2var.py`; full MRMR selection + engineered recipe bit-identical on the scene bed (650 selected / 540 engineered, exact JSON fingerprint match vs the pre-fix baseline). `joint_freqs_2var` is retained (keep-all-kernel-versions) for the byte-identity reference test. The summation is bit-exact because numpy's `.sum()` over the small (< 128) nonzero-bin count of a 2-var joint is a naive sequential add, which the scalar accumulation loop reproduces in the same ascending-class-id order.
- DCD pairwise-SU hot loop: replaced the per-pair `merge_vars` joint-histogram call in `_dcd_metrics.pair_su` (the `distance="su"` default path) with a dedicated 2-variable `joint_freqs_2var` njit kernel. The DCD cluster-discovery sweep calls `pair_su` per candidate-pair (~24k pairs on scene at 600 rows, ~345k at full 2407x299), and after the per-column marginal entropies are state-cached the joint `H(X_a, X_b)` is the ONLY genuinely per-pair term -- so the sole per-pair `merge_vars` ran on every cache-miss pair. `merge_vars` allocates a length-n `final_classes` array + an `expected`-length `lookup_table` and walks every sample twice (accumulate + dense-renumber remap), all of which the SU path discards (it consumes only the pruned freqs for `entropy`). `joint_freqs_2var` returns the IDENTICAL normalized nonzero frequencies (same `cls = ca + cb*nb_a` integer encoding, same ascending-class-id prune order) WITHOUT that wasted alloc+remap, then hands them to the canonical `entropy` njit verbatim, so the log-sum reduction is reused unchanged. Microbench (scene shapes, joint term incl. downstream `entropy`): 171.9 -> 7.4 us/pair (~23x). BIT-IDENTICAL: verified max-abs-diff EXACTLY 0.0 vs `merge_vars`'s 2-var output across uniform / sparse / heavy-skew / constant-column data at n=37/600/2407 (14160 pairs each) plus unequal per-column bin counts, by `test_joint_freqs_2var.py`; full MRMR selection + engineered recipe bit-identical on the scene bed. (Tested-irreducible companion: adding explicit eager numba signatures to the warm hot kernels gave only ~17 ns/call -- within measurement noise -- because a warm `@njit(cache=True)` already resolves one cached overload via an O(1) dict lookup; the ~230 ns/call array-boundary cost is intrinsic boxing/NRT, identical for eager vs lazy signatures.)
- Metrics: the descending argsort shared by every metric kernel (`_argsort_desc_for_metrics`) gained a cupy GPU path, size-gated at N >= 50k (env `MLFRAME_METRICS_ARGSORT_GPU_MIN_N`; cupy-probed, byte-identical AUC incl. tied data since `fast_aucs` uses tie-order-invariant fractional ranks). cupy argsort is 1.95x@50k..4.94x@1M vs numpy in isolation. A metric run issues MANY argsorts of MIXED size (full-array per-class AUC plus small per-bin/bootstrap sorts), so the gate is decisive: routing ALL of them to GPU regressed the c0023 200k suite ~8% (the small sorts pay transfer/sync overhead), but size-gating at 50k -- GPU only for argsorts >= 50k -- nets a CONSISTENT ~10% end-to-end win at 200k (A/B: CPU 9.37/8.29s vs GPU 7.98/7.89s, both GPU runs beat both CPU). Stable-sort opt-in stays on CPU.
- Suite phase: the post-pipeline forced 2x `gc.collect()` (in `_phase_pandas_conversion_and_cat_prep`) is now size-gated on the released frame (`_should_force_post_pipeline_gc` / `_FORCED_GC_MIN_DF_MB`, default 256 MB, env `MLFRAME_FORCED_GC_MIN_DF_MB`). The forced collect relieves Windows commit-charge pressure before composite-discovery on large polars inputs, but its ~0.85s cost is object-count- (not frame-size-) driven, so on small frames it was pure overhead -- ~17% of a 1k-row suite run (4.32s -> 3.59s on combo c0023). Large frames keep the forced collect (gate); set the env to 0 to restore legacy always-force.
- MRMR FE pair-search OPT-B: narrowed the FE ordinal-code matrices from the int32 `quantization_dtype` default to int8 (n_bins<=127) / int16. (a) `disc_2d` -- via `_narrow_code_dtype` at the three batched `discretize_2d_quantile_batch` call sites (chunk / per-pair / external-validation). (b) `classes_dense` -- the `(n, K)` densified-code buffer inside `batch_mi_with_noise_gate`, via a new `classes_dtype` kernel param sized to `disc_2d.dtype` by the dispatcher. FE engineered columns are binned to `quantization_nbins` (~10), so both arrays hold non-negative ordinals that fit int8; the int32 storage carried 4x the bandwidth it needed in the two memory-bound hotspots that gather a column out of a row-major buffer (the searchsorted code WRITE / the `disc_2d[:, k]` histogram READ; and the strided `classes_dense[:, k]` re-read once per permutation in `_relevance_from_dense`). On the scene 2407x64152 chunk each of `disc_2d` and `classes_dense` drops from 589 MiB int32 to 147 MiB int8 (~880 MiB less peak RAM), which also removes the int32-allocation OOM that struck RAM-tight hosts. BIT-IDENTICAL MI: both arrays are non-negative ordinals only ever READ as histogram indices; the actual counter `joint_counts` stays int32 (its `dtype` arg is unchanged), and every GPU twin re-casts `disc_2d` to int32 on upload. `classes_dtype` defaults to int32 (legacy width preserved for non-opted callers). Verified by `test_fe_narrow_code_dtype.py` (int8 codes value-identical to int32/int16; `batch_mi_with_noise_gate` fe_mi byte-identical for int8-disc+int8-classes vs int32 across raw-MI + SU + permutation-gate) and a full-scene (2407x299) bit-identical MRMR selection check.
- MRMR FE pair-search OPT-A: parallelised the two serial-main-thread kernels. `_searchsorted_2d_right_njit` (discretization) and `_materialise_chunk_njit` (pair-FE) were deliberately serial (`nogil`, not `parallel`) to avoid nesting a numba prange inside the joblib `backend="threading"` FE dispatch (which deadlocks the threading layer). But that joblib path only fires at `len(X) >= 50000`; the common `len(X) < 50000` (and `<2 prospective pairs`) case runs `check_prospective_fe_pairs` on the MAIN thread with NO joblib threads, so those two dominant kernels ran single-threaded while the other cores sat idle. Added byte-identical `parallel=True` column-prange TWINS (`_searchsorted_2d_right_njit_parallel`, `_materialise_chunk_njit_parallel`) -- keeping the serial versions -- dispatched ONLY on the serial-main-thread path via a `serial_main_thread` boolean threaded down from the `_mrmr_fe_step` size branch, gated by a per-host serial-vs-parallel column-count crossover from the `kernel_tuning` cache (`@kernel_tuner` spec `mrmr_fe_kernel_parallelism`, no hardcoded threshold). The joblib (>=50000-row) path keeps the serial `nogil` kernels. BIT-IDENTICAL: each column is reduced independently (per-column edge slice / operand columns, per-column write), so the result is thread-count-invariant -- the same proof the already-shipped `_quantile_edges_2d_njit` column-prange relies on; verified by `test_fe_parallel_kernel_twins.py` (parallel == serial bytes across NaN/inf/float32/float64 and the dispatch-gating predicate) and a full-scene (2407x299) bit-identical MRMR selection check (650 selected / 540 engineered, unchanged).

### Refactor

- **ShapProxiedFS code consolidated into the `feature_selection/shap_proxied_fs/` subpackage (behaviour-preserving).** The 22 loose top-level `_shap_proxy_*.py` modules that lay flat next to `feature_selection/` (explain / treeshap(+gpu/+interactions(+gpu)) / heuristics / cluster(+su(+bitmap)) / interactions / search / objective / gpu / gradient / calibrate / catboost / compose / precomputed / preflight / prefilter(+univariate) / shap_prefilter), plus the `_shap_proxy_revalidate/` subpackage, were `git mv`d into `shap_proxied_fs/` so the whole selector lives under one package; the only loose module left behind is the benchmark-only `_benchmarks/_shap_proxy_regime_data.py` (it is a synthetic-data helper, not part of the selector). Every importer was repointed from `mlframe.feature_selection._shap_proxy_X` to `mlframe.feature_selection.shap_proxied_fs._shap_proxy_X` (350 import sites across src + tests), preserving lazy in-body import placement (cycle-safe). `restrict_artifacts` is now re-exported from the package `__init__` so the cross-package importer (`hybrid_selector.py`) uses the public surface instead of a deep underscore path. The 56 ShapProxied test files moved into `tests/feature_selection/shap_proxied/`. No public API change: `from mlframe.feature_selection.shap_proxied_fs import ShapProxiedFS` is unchanged.

### Added

- MRMR FE gained a SUCCESSIVE-HALVING / RUNG-SCHEDULE OPERATOR-SEARCH BUDGET (`MRMR(fe_rung_schedule_enable=True)`, **DEFAULT-ON**; backlog #16, `_fe_rung_schedule.py`; knobs `fe_rung_keep_frac=None` per-(n,p) dispatch, `fe_rung_rel_floor=0.40`, `fe_rung_min_pairs=6`). **VALIDATED + SHIPPED (default-on).** Routes the EXPENSIVE per-pair FE operator search (`check_prospective_fe_pairs` -- all unary x binary transforms / CMA-ES / full discretize / prewarp, ~4-50s per pair) via a CHEAP rung-0 SCREEN so the search goes DEEPER at equal wall-time. Mechanism (generalises `_cat_confirm_bandit`'s budget-allocation idea from cat-pair permutation confirmation to numeric operator-search budget): rung 0 ranks the gate-surviving prospective pairs by their JOINT MI `pair_mi` -- **the monotone-ish proxy the backlog asked for is ALREADY computed by the pair-MI gate, so the screen is FREE** (a sort + dict comprehension, no coarse-nbins re-score; cProfile-confirmed negligible) -- and keeps the top `keep_frac` fraction UNION every pair whose `pair_mi >= fe_rung_rel_floor * max_pair_mi`; rung 1 runs the unchanged expensive operator search ONLY on the survivors. The relative-MI floor is the LOAD-BEARING no-drop guard: it protects a moderate-MI genuine winner (e.g. a pair at 0.45*max_pair_mi) from a too-aggressive fractional cut while still cutting the spurious `(c,noise)` over-permissive survivors at ~0.17*max_pair_mi. **GATES UNCHANGED** -- this changes WHERE the operator-search compute goes, NOT admission (every kept pair still passes the same engineered-MI / external-validation / stability-vote gates; a pair the rung-0 screen drops simply never gets the operator search, exactly as the existing `fe_synergy_max_pairs` already caps synergy pairs -- this GENERALISES that per-pair budget to the whole prospective pool). The keep fraction is dispatched per (n_rows, n_pairs) through `pyutilz.performance.kernel_tuning.cache` with a measurement-backed fallback (0.34 large pool / 0.50 moderate / 1.0 small) -- the iron rule (never hardcode one threshold across hardware / data shapes); `MLFRAME_FE_RUNG_KEEP_FRAC` overrides. **BENCHMARK (n=5000, p=40, canonical `a**2/b + log(c)*sin(d)` fixture + 35 noise cols, wide relaxed-gate pools of 7-51 prospective pairs, 5 seeds).** The proxy is SAFE: Spearman(pair_mi, full-fidelity survival)=0.66 and every genuine signal pair (a,b)/(a,c)/(b,c) ranks #1-2 by pair_mi in EVERY seed. **SPEEDUP: 1.7-2.2x at keep_frac=0.5, 3.3-11.2x at keep_frac=0.25, with NO genuine signal pair dropped in ANY seed** -- the rung cuts ONLY spurious `(c,noise)` over-permissive survivors (a denoising bonus, not a loss); the canonical default-fit selection is IDENTICAL with vs without the rung (`test_biz_value_mrmr_fe_canonical` 16/16 green). **DEEPER-SEARCH WIN at equal wall-time:** because rung-0 is free and rung-1 runs on a fraction, a LARGER input pool (`fe_synergy_max_pairs` 3->60) can be fed at the same rung-1 cost -> recovers a needle a hard flat top-K budget misses. **DEGENERATE all-noise pool** (max_pair_mi≈0.07, no gradient): the relative floor keeps EVERY pair -> 1.0x / 0 dropped (no blind cut where there is no signal gradient to exploit; XOR-only all-zero-pair_mi pools likewise pass through untouched, never blind-cut). Self-gates to a byte-identical flat sweep below `fe_rung_min_pairs` pairs; `fe_rung_schedule_enable=False` byte-reproduces the flat top-K sweep. Triad in `tests/feature_selection/test_fe_rung_schedule.py` (14 tests: 10 unit -- keep-logic top-frac+relative-floor / self-gating no-ops below-min-pairs+keep_frac=1+all-zero-pair_mi / dispatch fallback monotone-in-pool-size + env-override / ctor-knobs-exposed+pickle-safe / default-on; + biz_value selection-identical-canonical + speedup-on-wide-pool-at-identical-selection + equal-wall-deeper-needle; + cProfile rung-screen-negligible). Findings in `D:/Temp/halving_results.md`.
- MRMR FE gained CROSS-FOLD RECIPE STABILITY VOTING (`MRMR(fe_stability_vote_enable=True)`, **DEFAULT-ON**; backlog #15, `_fe_stability_vote.py`; knobs `fe_stability_vote_k=5`, `fe_stability_vote_quorum=0.6`). **VALIDATED + SHIPPED (default-on).** A near-FREE CONSENSUS layer OVER the existing FE gates: the expensive pair-FE search still runs ONCE on the full data (unchanged), then each surviving numeric-pair (`unary_binary`) recipe is REPLAYED on K held-out folds and its uplift gate recomputed per fold; a recipe is admitted into `support_` / `_engineered_recipes_` only if it clears the gate in `>= ceil(quorum*K)` folds (3 of 5 by default). Failed recipes are dropped from BOTH the `engineered_recipes` dict and `selected_vars` as a post-gate filter in `_run_fe_step` BEFORE the fit-end copy into `_engineered_recipes_`. **No refit** -- only K plug-in-MI replays per recipe (`apply_recipe` is leak-safe: the recipe's quantile edges / prewarp coeffs / gate medians are frozen at full-fit time, so replaying on a held-out fold uses NO y reference; the per-fold statistic compares `MI(engineered_fold; y_fold)` against the fold's source-operand marginal MIs -- a held-out re-evaluation of the SAME uplift the in-fit gate used). This is DISTINCT from `_stability_fe.py` (Layer 36), which REFITS MRMR N times as a separate opt-in estimator. It COMPLEMENTS the order-2/order-3 Westfall-Young maxT floors: maxT kills the chance-MAX candidate WITHIN a fold (best-of-pool selection bias); cross-fold voting kills a recipe that won only on a fold-specific QUIRK of the full-data split (its uplift carried by a handful of rows in the training split, collapsing on the held-out folds -- a failure maxT cannot see). TARGET ANCHOR RE-BINNING (load-bearing): the held-out gate re-bins the target equi-frequency to `quantization_nbins` inside the voter (the `drop_redundant_raw_operands` precedent) so the target, the engineered column and the source operands share ONE balanced binning -- WITHOUT it the high-cardinality, imbalanced screening `classes_y` (26 unequal levels on a skewed regression target) inflated the 2-D engineered leg's finite-sample joint-MI bias asymmetrically vs the 1-D source legs, collapsing the uplift ratio and DROPPING the genuine `div(sqr(a),abs(b))` / `mul(log(c),sin(d))` recipes (passes 1/5). **DEFAULT-ON is the correct call** (opt-in hides the win): the vote SELF-GATES to a structural no-op below 2 `unary_binary` survivors / k<2 / quorum<=0 / tiny n, so on data without fold-specific winners it costs ~0; only `unary_binary` recipes are voted (the fold-prone numeric-pair family), other kinds (orth-basis / hinge / wavelet / cat-FE encoders) pass through untouched. **BENCHMARK (small fixtures, n<=4000).** NOISE-SURVIVOR REDUCTION (the win): on pure-noise frames with the FE gates relaxed so chance-max noise pairs slip the in-fit per-pair gate (maxT off, lenient prevalence -- the regime the consensus layer targets), the vote cut fold-specific engineered survivors **44 -> 1 across 10 frames (98% fewer)**. SIGNAL PRESERVATION: on the canonical `y=a**2/b + log(c)*sin(d)` the WITH-vote engineered set is IDENTICAL to WITHOUT-vote (all three genuine recipes -- `div(sqr(a),abs(b))`, `mul(log(c),sin(d))`, `sub(sin(a),sin(d))` -- clear the quorum, transform output unchanged); the standalone `a**2/b` and `log*sin` fixtures both preserve their recipe. COST: replay-only, -0.4% fit time (no refit). The just-shipped hinge / dispersion / wavelet suites stay green (those recipe kinds pass through untouched). Pre-#15 pickles default the flag ON but byte-reproduce the prior support when the vote is a no-op; `fe_stability_vote_enable=False` byte-reproduces the pre-vote support exactly. Triad in `tests/feature_selection/test_fe_stability_vote.py` (8 tests: unit fold-partition disjoint+cover / per-fold gate fires-on-signal+rejects-noise / no-op gating below 2 recipes+k<2+quorum<=0 / ctor-knobs-exposed+pickle-safe + biz_value noise-survivor-reduction + signal-preservation[ratio,logsin] + cProfile replay-cost-share). Findings in `D:/Temp/stability_vote_results.md`.
- MRMR FE gained a HAAR WAVELET / LOCALIZED MULTIRESOLUTION BASIS operator (`MRMR(fe_wavelet_enable=True)`, **DEFAULT-ON**; backlog #13, `_wavelet_basis_fe.py`; recipe kind `orth_wavelet`, also registered in `_EXTRA_BASIS_KINDS` + `build_orth_wavelet_recipe` so the extra-basis path emits it alongside spline/Fourier). **VALIDATED + SHIPPED (default-on).** It is a NEW univariate operator for a signal shape NOTHING in the catalog captures: a LOCALIZED bump / multiscale piecewise structure -- `y` jumps only inside a narrow sub-window of x, or has step/contrast structure at SEVERAL scales at once. The closest existing operators all have the WRONG form -- Fourier is GLOBAL (a localized bump forces a long tone sum and the truncated series RINGS, Gibbs, around the discontinuity), the cubic B-spline's knots sit at FIXED quantiles of x (a bump that falls between knots is SMOOTHED AWAY), and `numeric_rounding` is a global flat-step quantiser blind to location. Wavelets are simultaneously localized in x AND multiscale, so a small dyadic Haar set captures a sharp local contrast at the right scale with a couple of legs where a global basis needs many (and still rings). MECHANISM: on x's support normalise `z = clip((x-lo)/span, 0, 1)` and emit a SMALL dyadic set of HAAR WAVELET INDICATORS `psi_{j,k}(z)` -- `+1` on the LEFT half / `-1` on the RIGHT half of the dyadic interval `[k/2^j, (k+1)/2^j)`, `0` outside -- at scales `j=0..3`, positions `k=0..2^j-1` (<= 15 candidate legs before selection). CANDIDATE-EXPLOSION CONTROL (the load-bearing risk the backlog flags): two self-limiting bounds keep the emitted set small -- (1) HELD-OUT SCALE-SELECTION ranks each candidate leg by its TRAIN-side (`%3` stride) marginal MI and admits it only if its HELD-OUT marginal MI clears a NOISE-AWARE MAD floor (`median + 3.5*1.4826*MAD`) over the candidate legs' held-out MIs, then keeps the top `fe_wavelet_max_legs` (default 6) per column; pure noise -> every leg sits in the noise band -> 0 emitted; (2) the pool-level admission gate below. GATE -- the key design finding: a Haar leg is NON-monotone in x (it is +1 then -1 then 0), so unlike the MI-INVARIANT monotone hinge (#11) / isotonic (#14) it IS MI-VISIBLE -- but the NAIVE leg-MI-vs-raw-MI uplift (the spline/Fourier path) MIS-FIRES here: a localized `y` is a FUNCTION of x in a sub-window, so binned raw x ALREADY scores high marginal MI and a single leg's marginal MI sits BELOW it (uplift < 1 -> wrongly dropped, the same trap the hinge hit but for a non-monotone leg). The correct, more honest statistic is the HELD-OUT INCREMENTAL MI of the leg OVER binned raw x (`MI(y; [bin(x), leg]) - MI(y; bin(x))` on the `%3` slice): does the leg's sign within a dyadic cell sharpen `y` BEYOND what the raw column already says? On top of that a COMPLEMENTARITY GUARD requires the leg's increment to beat `0.5 x` the SMOOTH-refinement gain (what merely finer location-binning of raw x adds) -- so a SMOOTH (sin / monotone) column, where finer binning captures the signal, admits 0 legs (the global Fourier basis owns that regime), while a LOCALIZED step/contrast, where the leg nails a sharp discontinuity finer uniform binning resolves only slowly, admits legs. DEFAULT-ON is the correct call (not opt-in -- opt-in hides the win): the operator is MI-VISIBLE so it routes through the MI-based gate (no deferred-materialise / re-add dance the MI-invariant hinge needs) and it is SELF-LIMITING (held-out MAD floor + max-legs cap + incremental gate + complementarity guard), so default-on costs ~0 columns on data without localized structure. LEAK-SAFE replay: `orth_wavelet` stores only `(lo, span)` + the dyadic `(j, k)` -- NO y -- so transform replay is the closed-form indicator `_dyadic_haar_leg(clip((x-lo)/span,0,1), j, k)` (verified byte-exact, 0.0 replay error + pickle round-trip; structurally identical to `orth_spline`). The scale SELECTION consumes y at FIT time (like spline knot placement / Fourier frequency detection / hinge breakpoint search) but the emitted COLUMN VALUE does not, so the replayed feature is leakage-free by construction. **BENCHMARK (n=4000, x~U(0,1), SET-level held-out Ridge R^2 -- the real operator behaviour, a small scale-selected leg SET not a single leg; `D:/Temp/wavelet_bench.py`).** LOCALIZED-STEP WIN `y=1[x>0.5]+noise`: Haar set **0.747** > Fourier set 0.715 > B-spline set 0.666 (Haar beats BOTH). MULTISCALE WIN `y=1[x>0.5]+0.8*1[0.75<x<0.875]+noise` (step + narrow bump): Haar **0.869** > Fourier 0.768 (Gibbs) > spline 0.738 -- the multiresolution showcase. BUMP `y=1[0.4<x<0.5]+noise`: Haar 0.389 > spline 0.360 > Fourier 0.186 (two adjacent legs span the symmetric bump). SMOOTH COMPLEMENTARITY CONTROL (mandatory, proves it is not a Fourier-clone) `y=sin(2*pi*x)+noise`: Fourier **0.839** WINS, Haar 0.713 LOSES. GATE STABILITY (12 seeds): the admission gate admits legs on STEP 12/12 + MULTISCALE 12/12 + BUMP 11/12 and admits 0 on SMOOTH 0/12 + pure NOISE 0/12. CANONICAL `y=a**2/b + log(c)*sin(d)`: the gate admits 0 wavelet legs so it does NOT perturb genuine pair-FE recovery (the canonical biz regression suite stays green; mirrors the dispersion self-limit, no deferred-materialisation needed). End-to-end a plain `MRMR()` (no opt-in flag) selects a Haar leg into support on a localized-step target and `transform()` replays it leak-free. cProfile (p=8, n=4000): the stage is ~242 ms, hotspot the per-candidate-leg binned-MI in scale-selection, bounded by the held-out MAD floor + max-legs cap (within budget; no numba/cupy ladder warranted at this size). Pre-#13 pickles default the flag OFF (byte-identical legacy reload); the fitted-attr `wavelet_features_` defaults empty. Triad in `tests/feature_selection/test_wavelet_basis_fe.py` (13 tests: unit dyadic-indicator closed-form / leak-safe bit-exact replay + pickle / dispatcher routing / noise-rejects-0 / scale-selection-bounds-count + biz_value step-beats-Fourier-and-spline / multiscale-beats-Fourier / smooth-Fourier-wins / smooth-admits-0 / step-admits-legs + default-on-selects-on-step / canonical-non-perturbation + cProfile guard). Findings in `D:/Temp/wavelet_results.md`.
- MRMR FE gained a CONDITIONAL-DISPERSION / 2nd-moment operator (Family D, `MRMR(fe_conditional_dispersion_enable=True)`, **DEFAULT-ON**; backlog #12). The catalog (L21-L104) models conditional **location** only -- Family B `conditional_residual` emits `x_i - E[x_i | bin(x_j)]` -- but NOTHING modelled conditional **scale** (volatility / dispersion regimes), so a small mean-residual that is a huge outlier vs a TIGHT conditional spread (a fraud amount anomalous FOR that merchant, a latency anomalous FOR that payload size) was invisible. The operator (`_extra_fe_families_dispersion.py`, re-exported from `_extra_fe_families.py` to stay under the module-size limit; recipe kind `conditional_dispersion`) bins `x_j` into `n_bins` quantile bins (the SAME edges Family B uses), per bin stores BOTH the conditional mean `mu_hat` AND the conditional std `sigma_hat` of `x_i`, and emits the conditional z-score fold `|z| = |(x_i - mu_hat_bin)/sigma_hat_bin|` and `z**2` (the load-bearing NON-monotone dispersion anomalies; the signed `z` is a near-duplicate of the mean-residual under dedup so the default emissions are `(absz, z2)`). **DEFAULT-ON is the correct call** (not opt-in -- opt-in hides the win): unlike the MI-INVARIANT hinge (#11) / isotonic (#14) monotone reshapes, the `|z|` fold is genuinely NON-monotone in `x_i`, so it carries real MUTUAL INFORMATION about a heteroscedastic target and routes through the NORMAL MI-uplift gate; and it is SELF-LIMITING via a dual-uplift gate that admits a column only when its `MI(y; |z|)` beats BOTH the raw `x_i` MI AND the `|mean-residual|` Family-B sibling MI -- on homoscedastic data `sigma_hat ~= const` so `|z|` is a scaled `|mean-residual|` (no uplift -> dropped, also caught by the cross-stage Spearman dedup), on pure noise nothing clears the floor, and on the canonical pair-FE fixture the gate admits 0 so the operator does NOT perturb genuine-feature recovery. Leak-safe replay (kind `conditional_dispersion`) extends Family B's `bin_mean` payload with `bin_std`: it stores the `x_j` quantile edges + per-bin `(mu_hat, sigma_hat)` (NO y) and replay digitises `x_j` + computes `z = (x_i - mu_hat)/sigma_hat` closed-form, reads only X (verified byte-exact, 0.0 replay error). A bin with too few rows / (near-)constant `x_i` falls back to the global std (no /0). **BENCHMARK (n=4000), SHIPPED.** HETEROSCEDASTIC two-bin (bin A `x_i~N(0,1)`, bin B `x_i~N(0,5)` selected by `x_j`, `y=1[|x_i|>2*sigma_bin]`): **MI(y;|z|)=0.168 > MI(y;raw x_i)=0.140 > MI(y;mean-resid)=0.126** -- the dispersion `|z|` is the TOP-MI feature, capturing heteroscedastic signal BOTH the raw column and the mean-residual sibling miss; downstream LogReg AUC lift over raw >= +0.03 (3 seeds). HOMOSCEDASTIC control (constant spread, conditional-mean shift only): the dual-uplift gate admits **0** genuine dispersion cols (`|z|` rank-identical to `|mean-resid|`, Spearman 0.997). NOISE control (pure noise x, random y): admits **0**. CANONICAL `y=a**2/b + log(c)*sin(d)`: dispersion admits **0** and the MRMR-fit engineered set is BYTE-IDENTICAL to dispersion-OFF (verified ON vs OFF) -- recovery UNPERTURBED (the canonical biz regression suite stays green); end-to-end on a heteroscedastic REGRESSION target the dispersion col is selected into support AND fed into a downstream pair-FE composite. cProfile: the generation is a few `np.add.at` scatter-reductions per pair (one for the per-bin sum, one for the centred SS), O(n) per pair, well within budget on n<=4000. Triad in `tests/feature_selection/test_conditional_dispersion_fe.py` (16 tests: unit closed-form/leak-safe-replay/pickle + biz_value hetero-MI-beats-mean-residual/AUC-lift/homoscedastic-self-limit/noise-admits-nothing/canonical-non-perturbation + cProfile guard). Pre-#12 pickles default the flag OFF (byte-identical legacy reload). A pre-existing roster-reconciliation flake in `test_biz_value_mrmr_layer104.py::test_mrmr_rankgauss_enabled_adds_columns` (the rankgauss col is consumed into a pair-FE composite so its standalone roster reconciles empty -- failed at n=4000 with dispersion ON *or* OFF) was fixed in the same change by isolating the family under test (disable the general-FE competitors, mirroring the sibling rare-category test).
- MRMR FE gained a SURROGATE-GBM SPLIT-CO-OCCURRENCE INTERACTION SEEDER (`MRMR(fe_gbm_seeder_enable=True)`, DEFAULT OFF; backlog #6, the synergy-frontier SCALING lever) plus its mandatory ORDER-3 Westfall-Young maxT permutation-null floor rail (#7, ships in the same change). The univariate-MI `seed_count` that the prospective-pair sweep + triplet FE pick source columns by is BLIND to pure synergy: a zero-marginal interaction operand (`y = sign(x_a*x_b*x_c) + noise`, every marginal MI ~= 0) is never ranked top-N, so the pair is never enumerated and the triple never seeded -> the needle is MISSED. The seeder (`_surrogate_interaction_seeder.surrogate_gbm_interaction_seeds`) fits ``fe_gbm_seeder_self_gate_reps`` shallow LightGBMs (default 5 x ~300 depth-4 trees) on the discretised screening matrix, walks every root-to-leaf path, and AGGREGATES a DEPTH-DISCOUNTED split-gain co-occurrence weight (`min` of the per-split `split_gain * depth_discount**depth` along the path) over all the surrogates for every co-splitting PAIR + TRIPLE: a zero-marginal operand still appears as a split partner CONDITIONED on its co-splitter, and the true operands co-occur CONSISTENTLY across the per-seed surrogates (noise triples vary), so the aggregated co-occurrence ranks the true interaction members at the top. Top-K pairs' operands are MERGED into `numeric_vars_to_consider` so their JOINT MI is screened by the existing pair pipeline (the `seed_count` bypass); top-K triples are gated by the order-3 floor and forwarded to the triplet FE as `explicit_triplets` (new param on `hybrid_orth_mi_triplet_fe[_with_recipes]`, which then enumerates EXACTLY the floored survivors instead of C(seed_k,3) over the univariate-MI pool). Cost is O(n*trees*depth) to fit -- INDEPENDENT of p^2/p^3 (the large-p lever). The surrogate trains on the CANDIDATE-ONLY submatrix (the target column is EXCLUDED -- the discretised target rides in the screening matrix, so training on the full matrix would let the tree split on `y` itself and leak a perfect OOF that auto-passes the self-gate; the surrogate's local feature indices are remapped back to global candidate columns). SELF-GATE is a PERMUTATION SIGNIFICANCE z-test ASYMMETRIC by the information content of the OOF statistic at each order: PAIRS are emitted only when the real-OOF MEAN over K splits sits `fe_gbm_seeder_self_gate_min_z` sigma (default 2) above the permuted-y OOF null (a genuine 2-way lifts held-out OOF far above chance, z >> 0; pure noise z ~ 0 -> no pair pollution); TRIPLES are ALWAYS emitted from co-occurrence because a hard 3-way `sign(a*b*c)` is barely learnable by a shallow tree -- its held-out OOF sits at ~chance (z indistinguishable from noise) EVEN THOUGH its split co-occurrence still ranks the operands top, so an OOF gate that rejected noise triples would also reject the genuine 3-way. The #7 order-3 maxT floor is therefore the BINDING noise guard for triples (architectural through-line: proposer GENERATES, the maxT floors GATE). The #7 rail (`_permutation_null.pooled_triple_permutation_null_joint_mi_floor` + `info_theory.batch_triple_mi_prange`, the order-3 twin of `batch_pair_mi_prange` that DENSE-RENUMBERS the 3-way joint so its cardinality stays <= n) shuffles y K times, takes the per-shuffle MAX 3-way joint MI over the proposed-triple pool, and floors at the q-quantile -- gating every seeded triple so a wide noise matrix's best-of-pool chance-max 3-way joint MI cannot survive; `fe_triple_maxt_*` knobs mirror the order-2 floor (`=0` disables). **VALIDATED + SHIPPED (opt-in).** On the backlog fixtures (n=4000, p=200 iid): the 2-way needle `y=sign(x5*x30)` (marginal MIs ~0.001, univariate seed_count top-8 recall 0/2) is recovered ROBUSTLY -- OOF ~0.95 (z~38) passes the pair self-gate, (x5,x30) is the TOP co-occurrence pair, and the full MRMR fit engineers `add(sign(x5),sign(x30))`. The 3-way needle `y=sign(x7*x42*x113)` (all three marginal MIs ~0.001, univariate seed_count recall 0/3) is the CO-OCCURRENCE win recovered as the RANK-0 aggregated co-occurrence triple AND clears the order-3 maxT floor -- but only on the MAJORITY of surrogate seeds: the hard `sign(a*b*c)` is at the DETECTABILITY BOUNDARY for a shallow tree at n=4000/p=200 (recovered in the seeded top-K in 3/5 random seeds at the production nbins=5; the multi-booster aggregation + ne=300 lift it from 2/5; nbins=10 / deeper trees reach 4/5 but cannot change the fixed production discretisation). The MECHANISM is correct -- WHEN the surrogate's splits find the operands, co-occurrence ranks them and the order-3 floor passes the needle -- the residual gap is the surrogate's sample-limited ability to FIND the right 3 columns among 200, NOT the seeder logic. On a PURE-NOISE frame the production guard CHAIN admits nothing END-TO-END: the OOF pair self-gate FAILS (z~1.3 < 2.0) so 0 pairs are emitted, and the order-3 maxT floor curbs the always-emitted noise triples to a small best-of-pool residual that the downstream per-triplet uplift / abs-MI gates then reject (the e2e engineers NOTHING on noise). The seeder's `max_depth=4` (NOT the backlog's "depth-3" first guess) is load-bearing for the 3-way: depth 3 can only form the conjunction at the leaf with no purity margin so the operand split-gains stay buried; depth 4 lifts them; depth 5+ over-fragments. cProfile (`D:/Temp/cprofile_gbm_seeder.py`): the per-step cost is ``2*reps`` LightGBM fits (real + permuted-y) dominated by `lightgbm.basic.update` (irreducible library code; no numba/cupy ladder warranted since the hotspot is inside LightGBM); the order-3 floor is ~10 ms over the seeded triples at K=25. OPT-IN (default OFF) per the iron rule's "ship behind a flag if cost-risky on small p" -- the fixed multi-fit cost is not worth paying on narrow pools where `seed_count` already sees every operand; `fe_gbm_seeder_min_features` (default 30) self-routes it OFF there. Needs lightgbm (extra-deps-OK). Covered by `test_biz_value_gbm_seeder_order3_floor.py` (kernel bit-consistency with the merge_vars MI path; order-3 floor noise-FP over the full C(p,3) pool + genuine-needle clearance; 3-way co-occurrence rank + order-3-floor clearance; 2-way needle recall vs the univariate seed_count miss; pure-noise pair-gate block + order-3-floor curb). The order-2 maxT floor suite + `create_keep_drop` default path stay GREEN (opt-in -> default byte-identical).
- MRMR FE joint-prevalence RATIO gate gained an OPT-IN Miller-Madow debias (`MRMR(fe_mm_debias_prevalence=True)`, DEFAULT OFF; backlog #1 + #4). The gate `best_mi / pair_mi > fe_min_engineered_mi_prevalence` compares a 1-D engineered MI (over ~`quantization_nbins` bins) against a 2-D joint MI (over ~`nbins^2` bins); both are RAW plug-in MIs whose positive finite-sample bias is `(k_x-1)(k_y-1)/2n`, and the JOINT denominator carries ~`nbins`x more bias, so the raw ratio is structurally depressed below 1.0 at small/moderate n even when the 1-D feature captures all the joint information. When enabled the new `info_theory.mi_miller_madow_correct` (closed-form `I - (k_x-1)(k_y-1)/2n`) is subtracted from BOTH sides before the ratio, using the OCCUPIED bin counts (#4: nominal `nbins` over-corrects heavy-tailed columns that collapse to few occupied bins -- `_pairs_gates._occupied_k` / `mm_debiased_prevalence_ratio`), with a denominator-positivity guard that falls back to the raw ratio when the joint bias swamps the finite-sample joint MI; `->` raw ratio as `n -> inf` (large-n byte-untouched). The order-2 maxT permutation-null floor (the outer best-of-pool guard) is co-debiased CONSISTENTLY on the SAME per-pair occupied-joint-K scale (`_permutation_null.pooled_pair_permutation_null_joint_mi_floor(mm_debias=...)` + `pairwise_mm_joint_bias`, the per-pair bias subtracted from both the per-shuffle joint MIs inside the kernel AND the observed `pair_mi` at the gate comparison in `_mrmr_fe_step`), so lowering the ratio bar does NOT weaken the floor (the FE-backlog IRON RULE). **bench-rejected (2026-06-09) as a DEFAULT.** The ISOLATED ratio fix is real -- on the He2(a)*b fixture the raw ratio is 0.555 / 0.841 / 1.003 at n=500 / 2000 / 8000 and the MM(occupied-K) ratio is 1.99 / 1.15 / 1.10 (crosses the 0.90 bar at small n where raw fails; n=8000 raw~=corrected), and a pure-NOISE frame stays below the bar both raw AND MM-corrected (0.27 / 0.28 / 0.56, no isolated FP) -- but it does NOT translate to an END-TO-END win and it ADMITS NOISE on the realistic weak target: (1) on the CLEAN He2(a)*b end-to-end the existing marginal-uplift FALLBACK gate already recovers the genuine (a,b) pair 5/5 at n=500 and n=2000 with MM OFF, so MM adds 0 incremental recovery; (2) on the user's WEAK F2 (`0.2*a**2/b + log(c*2)*sin(d/3)`, 10 seeds uniform n=20000) MM REGRESSES genuine_ab 10/10 -> 8/10, does NOT recover genuine_cd (0/10 -> 0/10), and nearly TRIPLES cross-mix admission 3/10 -> 9/10 seeds (n_cross 6 -> 11) -- the MM over-correction (ratio -> ~2.0) makes the prevalence gate uniformly too permissive so cross-signal artefacts like `sub(reciproc(b),sin(d))` / `add(reciproc(b),log(d))` clear the relaxed ratio. This is the IRON-RULE failure mode and matches the prior-session interaction-information-routing rejection on the SAME target (the cross-mix carries HIGHER interaction information than the genuine pair, so NO ratio threshold separates them). The maxT-floor co-update is itself correct + green (6/6 order-2 biz tests, noise 100% at/below the MM floor) -- the reject is the RATIO gate relaxation, not the floor. Default OFF (byte-stable; `False` == legacy raw-plug-in ratio + raw floor); `True` enables the full mechanism for re-bench on other (fine-binned / strong-signal / large-n) data. Covered by `test_mrmr_mm_debias_prevalence.py` (closed-form + occupied-K + denominator-guard unit tests, the maxT-floor MM consistency biz_value test, and a default-OFF byte-stability regression).
- FE backlog #5 (PERMUTATION-NULL-CALIBRATED PREVALENCE BAR -- replace the hardcoded `fe_min_engineered_mi_prevalence` (0.90) ratio gate with a SELF-CALIBRATING per-pool null ratio) **bench-rejected (2026-06-09)**; NOT shipped (no production knob / kernel added -- only an in-code `bench-attempt-rejected` note at the prevalence-gate site in `_feature_engineering_pairs/_pairs_core.py` + the 4th-lever entry in `test_mrmr_weak_f2_seed_stability.py`). #5's plan, conceptually distinct from #1 (which subtracts a DETERMINISTIC Miller-Madow bias -> uniformly relaxes the bar -> admits noise), was to CALIBRATE the bar to what NOISE actually produces: in the SAME K y-shuffles the order-2 maxT floor runs, ALSO mirror the max-over-transforms search (discretise the elementary binary bank mul/add/sub/div/max/min over the CONTINUOUS operands ONCE -- permutation-invariant -- then per shuffle take max 1-D engineered MI / joint pair MI), pool the per-pair null ratios over all shuffles, and gate `best_mi/pair_mi` at the q95 of that null-ratio distribution (the chance ceiling). Prototyped + benchmarked (`D:/Temp/probe_null_ratio.py` / `probe_e2e.py` / `probe_weakf2.py`; N_BINS=8, K=25, q=0.95). In ISOLATION the mechanism is SOUND and STRICTLY BETTER than #1: on PURE NOISE (n=2000, p=12) the null ceiling is ~0.16 and only the (1-q)=~5% chance rate of noise pairs clears it (the HARD noise-FP gate PASSES, unlike a naive relaxation); on He2(a)*b genuine synergy the real ratio 0.28/0.275/0.268 at n=500/2000/8000 clears the null bar (0.15/0.16/0.17) while the hardcoded 0.90 bar REJECTS at every n; on a mixed He2-signal+8-noise frame the bar admits the genuine (a,b) pair and 0-1/28 noise pairs (chance rate). **BUT bench-rejected on the case that matters -- the user's WEAK F2** (`0.2*a**2/b + f/5 + log(c*2)*sin(d/3)`, the SAME target that rejected #1/#8/#19): the null ceiling calibrates to CLEAN-noise pairs (~0.167), but EVERY weak-F2 pair sits FAR above it (5 seeds, n=20000) -- genuine_ab ~0.81, genuine_cd ~0.73, AND all four cross-mix pairs 0.56-0.72 (cross(b,d) ~0.717 >= genuine_cd ~0.726). So the null bar ADMITS every cross-mix on EVERY seed -- the SAME IRON-RULE failure mode as #1 (cross-mix 3/10 -> 9/10). ROOT CAUSE is the documented FUNDAMENTAL detectability limit (see `test_mrmr_weak_f2_seed_stability.py` "FOUR DIRECT LEVERS EXHAUSTED"): the cross-mix smuggles the dominant MONOTONE predictor `c` across the pair boundary (`invsqrt(c)` monotone in `c`), so its 1-D engineered summary recovers most of its (real, cross) joint -- a HIGH ratio no MI-threshold can separate from genuine synergy. The null bar measures the NOISE floor, but the weak-F2 problem is NOT noise admission -- it is a real-monotone-predictor cross-mix whose ratio is nowhere near the noise floor. AND end-to-end the existing marginal-uplift/prewarp FALLBACK already recovers the genuine F2 pairs (`div(.,sqrt(b))` 5/5 seeds at n=500/2000/8000), so #5 adds ZERO incremental recovery while WEAKENING cross-mix rejection. #5 is structurally a 4th MI-threshold/ratio lever and fails by construction like #1/#8/#19; do NOT re-attempt an MI-threshold fix here -- the only remaining direction is an out-of-sample / linear-usability correction on the FINAL model, not the per-pair MI gate. Numbers + verdict in `D:/Temp/null_prev_results.md`; pinned by the gate-site note + the weak-F2 lever ledger.
- MRMR FE pair gate gained an OPT-IN signed interaction-information (co-information) router (`MRMR(fe_ii_routing_enable=True)`, DEFAULT OFF; `_interaction_information.py` + `_mrmr_fe_step_helpers.apply_interaction_information_routing`). For each prospective pair it computes the signed `II(a;b;y) = I((a,b);y) - I(a;y) - I(b;y)` from the terms the gate ALREADY caches (marginals `cached_MIs[(a,)]`/`[(b,)]`, joint `pair_mi`), Miller-Madow corrects all three on their own cardinality before differencing (the joint term has `nbins_a*nbins_b` bins, so an un-corrected difference would manufacture positive II out of finite-sample inflation), floors positive II with a permutation null on the per-shuffle MAX II over the pool (same maxT machinery as the order-2 joint-MI floor), and routes: `II>floor` -> synergy (kept), `II<0` -> redundant (cluster-aggregate-eligible), `0<=II<=floor` -> additive (the no-interaction cross-mix, DEMOTED out of the FE search when the operand is a speculative synergy-bootstrap add). Tags surface on `fe_interaction_routes_` / `fe_interaction_ii_` / `fe_interaction_ii_floor_`. Changes routing/ranking only; the maxT floor + ratio gate stay as the detection guards, and while disabled (default) the call is a structural no-op (byte-stable -- verified default selection unchanged, no attrs set). The mechanism is correct on STRONG signal (synthetic n=3000: synergy `y=sign(a*b)` II=+0.55 vs additive `y=a+c+2*noise` II=+0.03 below the null floor vs redundancy `b~=a` II=-1.10; the null floor rejects chance-positive noise pairs; 10 unit + biz_value tests in `test_interaction_information_routing.py`). **bench-rejected (2026-06-09) as a DEFAULT for the user's weak-F2 cross-mix surrogate** (`y=0.2*a**2/b + f/5 + log(c*2)*sin(d/3)`, the motivating F2 target): per-pair II measured on the 3 cross-mix seeds {0,6,8} at n=20000 shows the CROSS-MIX pair `(b,c)` has the HIGHEST interaction information on EVERY seed (+0.0132/+0.0135/+0.0139) -- ABOVE the genuine `(a,b)` a**2/b pair (+0.0114/+0.0120/+0.0132) -- with the y-shuffle null floor at ~0.0007, two orders below every real pair. Because the cross-mix II EXCEEDS a genuine-interaction II in this coarse-binned weak-signal regime (the "completion synergy" of two weakly-informative columns is information-theoretically as large as the genuine 2-way signal), NO II threshold can demote the cross-mix without also dropping the genuine pair: F2 10-seed result was NEUTRAL (cross_mix 3/10 -> 3/10, genuine_ab 10/10, genuine_cd 0/10 unchanged -- the same too-weak-signal detection floor the FE backlog flags, which II routing cannot lift since it is MI-invariant by design). Kept default-off as a sound router for strong-signal / large-n / fine-binned pools rather than removed; cProfile (`profiling/bench_interaction_information_routing.py`): the opt-in null floor is ~1.8s at n=30k/p=25/K=25 (Python per-(shuffle,pair) MI re-score), so default-on would add cost for zero benefit on the target -- a numba `prange` kernel mirroring `batch_pair_mi_prange` is the noted path if ever promoted.
- FE backlog #19 (KSG / k-NN CONTINUOUS-MI as the F2 cross-mix tie-breaker) **bench-rejected (2026-06-09)** as the LAST direct MI-based lever for the weak-F2 cross-mix; NOT shipped (decisive test only, no code added). #19's plan was: where the binned prevalence ratio is in a near-tie margin band, recompute numerator+denominator with sklearn's shipped Kraskov-Stogbauer-Grassberger estimator (`feature_selection.mutual_info_regression`, raw continuous values, no coarse bins) and re-rank, to recover a genuine engineered joint whose heavy-tailed binned MI collapses. Step 1 (the decisive test, n=20000, 10 seeds, the user's exact uniform `y=0.2*a**2/b + f/5 + log(c*2)*sin(d/3)`) measured CONTINUOUS KSG MI vs the production binned MI (`_quantile_bin(nbins=10)` + `_cmi_from_binned` marginal branch, Miller-Madow corrected) for the genuine forms and the cross-mix surrogates: KSG does **NOT** separate them -- 0/10 seeds separate under EITHER estimator, identical ranking. The strongest cross-mix `sub(invcbrt(b),invsqrt(c))` scores binned 0.611 / KSG 0.708 vs the genuine `mul(log(c),sin(d))` joint 0.353 / 0.448 and the genuine `a**2/b` ratio 0.146 / 0.214; KSG WIDENS the cross-mix lead rather than closing it. ROOT CAUSE (single-operand MI decomposition): `c` enters `y` MONOTONICALLY through `log(c*2)`, so RAW `c` ALONE has MI 0.625 binned / 0.735 KSG -- the single most informative variable about `y`; the cross-mix smuggles `c` across the pair boundary almost cleanly (`invsqrt(c)` is monotone in `c`, MI 0.625/0.739 == raw `c`), while the GENUINE engineered form CORRUPTS `c` by multiplying in `sin(d)` -- the wrong frequency (the generator uses `sin(d/3)`; the TRUE `log(c*2)*sin(d/3)` form scores 0.869/1.070 but is unreachable in the unary-operator basis). So the genuine pair form is information-theoretically LESS predictive of `y` than a column that merely carries `c`, and this ordering is estimator-independent -- a finer (k-NN/KSG) estimator measures the SAME ranking and resolves `c`'s monotone signal even more sharply. The #19 margin-band tie-breaker therefore never fires (no tie -- the cross-mix leads by ~0.26) or, if widened, PROMOTES the cross-mix; the backlog's validation fixture (`mul(log(c),sin(d))` binned ~0.14 vs KSG ~0.32) reproduced only the premature-int-cast collapse ALREADY fixed in `_pairs_core.py` (float kept through to the quantile discretiser; on raw float the binned joint is ~0.35 here, nothing left to recover). VERDICT: the F2 cross-mix is a CONFIRMED FUNDAMENTAL weak-signal DETECTABILITY limit, and all THREE direct MI-based levers are now exhausted -- #1 MM-debias (over-corrects, triples cross-mix admission), #8 II-routing (cross-mix carries higher interaction information than the genuine pair), #19 KSG (cross-mix carries the dominant monotone predictor `c`). The F2 resolution thread is CLOSED; the only remaining direction is an out-of-sample / linear-usability correction on the FINAL model rather than the per-pair MI gate. Documented with the per-candidate table in `tests/feature_selection/test_mrmr_weak_f2_seed_stability.py` (module docstring) and `D:/Temp/ksg_results.md`; no production code or default changed.
- FE backlog #9 (RFF / random-projection interaction PRE-SCREEN -- detect-without-enumerate) **bench-rejected (2026-06-09)**; NOT shipped (no production module / flag added -- only an in-code `bench-rejected` note at the `_mrmr_fe_step._run_fe_step` proposer site + a characterization test). #9's plan was a sibling proposer to the #6 GBM seeder: draw R random SPARSE (support 2-4 cols) hyperplane projections `phi_r(x)=cos(w_r^T x_std + b_r)`, score each `phi_r`'s MI vs y, and PROMOTE supports whose best-of-trials MI uplift over the additive baseline (max single-col marginal MI in the support) clears a permutation-null maxT floor -- meant to recover the zero-marginal / smooth interaction supports the all-pairs synergy bootstrap SKIPS when `p > fe_synergy_screen_max_features`, in `O(R*n)` with `R << p^2`. Prototyped + benchmarked end-to-end (`D:/Temp/rff_proto.py`, `rff_proto2.py`, `rff_coverage.py`, `rff_vs_allpairs.py`, `rff_smooth_check.py`, `rff_largep_birthday.py`; n=2000, p=500-3000, the backlog's exact `y=x3*x400` zero-marginal and `y=sin(x1*x2)` smooth fixtures + a pure-noise p=500 control). The MECHANISM is SOUND -- when the needle support is COVERED, the best-of-T RFF ranks it #0 with uplift 0.146 vs a permutation-null floor 0.0145 (10x), and it is the ONLY support clearing the floor (0 noise false-promote) -- but it loses to the DIRECT all-pairs joint-MI sweep in EVERY tested regime, for four measured reasons: **(1) COVERAGE, not enumeration, is the binding constraint.** A SPECIFIC localized k=2 needle in p=500 has hit-probability `R / C(p,2)` over R random size-2 draws: R=2000 -> 1.6%, R=16000 -> 12%, R=124750 (== full all-pairs) -> 63%; reaching 0.95 recall of ONE needle needs `R ~ C(p,2)*ln(20) ~ 374k` draws -- MORE than the full enumeration. Random sparse supports cannot guarantee recall of a specific needle without ~enumerating all pairs. **(2) The BIRTHDAY-PARADOX rationale is WRONG for sparse planted needles.** With K=30 planted zero-marginal needles at p=3000 (C(p,2)=4.5M), R=20000 x T=6 = 120k RFF evals (65 s) DREW 0/30 needles -> recall 0/30. Hitting a fixed K-needle set needs draws `~ C(p,2)/K` (~150k here), not `R << p^2`; the birthday paradox is about collisions AMONG the draws, not hitting a fixed small TARGET set. **(3) COST.** One RFF best-of-T (T=8) support evaluation is 17x the cost of one direct joint-MI pair evaluation (4.1 ms vs 0.24 ms at n=2000); 0.95-recall of one needle is ~1533 s of RFF vs ~30 s for the full DETERMINISTIC C(500,2) joint-MI sweep. **(4) There is NO exclusive smooth-interaction sweet spot.** The 2-D joint-MI estimator is a NONPARAMETRIC (x_i, x_j, y) contingency MI, so the direct sweep recovers the SMOOTH `sin(x1*x2)` needle (rank #0/2000, joint MI 1.29 vs 0.34 next) exactly as well as the zero-marginal product `x3*x400` (rank #0/2000, joint MI 1.51) -- the "smooth/rotated interactions that axis-aligned trees miss" argument applies to the GBM seeder's TREES (#6/#21), NOT to the joint-MI sweep #9 was meant to replace. VERDICT: the correct fix for "needle missed when `p > fe_synergy_screen_max_features`" is to RAISE/relax that cap (and its `fe_synergy_max_sweep_cost ~ n*p^2` budget) -- the direct all-pairs joint-MI bootstrap recovers BOTH the zero-marginal and the smooth needle deterministically and ~17x cheaper at p<=1000 (full sweep ~30 s at p=500, ~120 s at p=1000); RFF random-support prescreen would only conceivably help at `p >~ 5000` where C(p,2) joint-MI is itself infeasible, but even there it is coverage-bound (test (2)) and never recovers a SPECIFIC needle, so it is not implemented. Do NOT re-attempt as a default or opt-in proposer without a fundamentally different (non-random-support) coverage scheme. Pinned by `tests/feature_selection/test_rff_prescreen_bench_reject.py` (the joint-MI sweep recovers both needles as rank-0; the `R/C(p,2)` coverage wall, analytic + empirical) and `D:/Temp/rff_prescreen_results.md`.
- FE backlog #10 (CONDITIONAL-MI COMPLEMENTARITY GROWTH -- the Apriori lattice) **bench-rejected (2026-06-09)**; NOT shipped (no production module / flag -- only an in-code `bench-rejected` note at the `_mrmr_fe_step._run_fe_step` proposer site + a bench-reject test). #10's plan, complementary to the shipped #6 GBM seeder, was to grow triples from the ORDER-2 SURVIVOR frontier (the prospective pairs) by testing only third columns `c` maximising the conditional-MI uplift `I((a,b,c);y) - I((a,b);y)` (reusing `batch_triple_mi_prange` for scale-consistency with the #7 order-3 maxT floor, which gates the kept triples), Apriori-pruned (`|surviving_(k-1)| x |shortlist_c|`, not O(p^3)), to catch a 3rd operand that matters ONLY given `(a,b)` -- pure higher-order synergy the univariate triplet `seed_count` misses. Prototyped + benchmarked end-to-end (`D:/Temp/cmi_lattice_proto.py` ... `proto5.py`, `cmi_lattice_cost.py`; n=3000, p=40, the backlog's exact `y=sign(x1*x2)*x3` fixture + a pure-noise control). REJECTED for three MEASURED reasons: **(1) the Apriori ANTI-MONOTONE premise is FALSE for the exact signal class #10 targets.** The backlog fixture `y=sign(x1*x2)*x3>0` is, mathematically, a PURE 3-way sign XOR (verified 100% agreement with `sign(x0)*sign(x1)*sign(x2)>0`), NOT a "detectable 2-way (x1,x2) + conditional x3" as the backlog text states. For a pure k-way interaction EVERY (k-1)-way sub-tuple is buried in the noise cloud (no distinguished joint MI): the needle PAIR never enters a realistic top-N order-2 frontier (its three sub-pairs sit deep in the joint-MI ranking, none in the top-12) so the needle TRIPLE is NEVER grown -- the same conditional-MI-is-not-greedy-trappable wall that killed beam-search for MRMR. **(2) the GROWTH KERNEL itself is sound but moot.** GIVEN a base pair holding 2 of the 3 needle legs, the CMI uplift ranks the true 3rd operand RANK-0 every time (6/6, uplift 0.21-0.61 >> noise) -- but that base pair is exactly what the frontier never contains (reason 1). On a CONSTRUCTED mixed fixture where the lattice CAN grow the needle (a marginal-signal base `x0` + a conditional-only `x2`, `y=(x0>0)^((x1>0)&(x2>0))`) it grows `(0,1,2)` as the #1 triple (jMI 0.4495) BUT the order-3 maxT floor admits 31/31 grown noise triples, ALL sharing the marginal-signal leg `x0` -- noise admission that is the downstream per-triplet uplift gate's job, NOT the floor's, and is identical for #6's seeded triples on that fixture (so the lattice adds no admission advantage). **(3) FULLY REDUNDANT with the shipped #6 GBM seeder.** On the pure-3-way-XOR fixture #6's split-co-occurrence proposer recovers the SAME needle {x1,x2,x3} as its RANK-0 triple (z=5.46), order-3-floored -- because path co-occurrence conditions a zero-marginal operand on its co-splitter WITHOUT requiring a detectable (k-1)-tuple, so it is immune to the anti-monotone wall that blocks #10. #10's whole purpose (reach a 3-way the univariate seeder misses) is ALREADY delivered by #6. The lattice's only edge is cost (19.5 ms growth vs 1641 ms for the GBM fit+self-gate at n=3000/p=40) -- but that merely buys a proposer that misses its own target signal class. Do NOT re-implement as the backlog specifies; a non-Apriori scheme (seed triples from #6's co-occurrence pool, THEN refine the 3rd operand by CMI uplift) could compose with #6, but the recovery is already complete via #6 alone, so there is no measured gap to close. Pinned by `tests/feature_selection/test_bench_reject_cmi_lattice.py` (the pure-3-way needle's sub-pairs are not in the top-N frontier; the growth kernel ranks the true 3rd operand rank-0 given the right base; the full lattice misses the needle end-to-end; #6 already recovers it) and `D:/Temp/cmi_lattice_results.md`.
- MRMR FE gained a HINGE / PIECEWISE-LINEAR CHANGE-POINT BASIS operator (`MRMR(fe_hinge_enable=True)`, DEFAULT OFF; backlog #11, `_hinge_basis_fe.py`). **VALIDATED + SHIPPED (opt-in).** It is a NEW univariate operator for a signal shape NOTHING in the catalog captures: a SLOPE CHANGE at a data-dependent threshold `y = a*x + b*max(x-tau,0)` (pricing tiers / dose-response / saturation). The closest existing operators all have the WRONG form -- `numeric_rounding` is piecewise-CONSTANT, the cubic B-spline is smooth + rounds off a sharp kink at its FIXED quantile knots (a non-adaptive knot misses the kink location), and an orth-poly needs a high degree + RINGS (Gibbs) around the kink. MECHANISM: detect 1-2 breakpoints `tau` per column by scanning inner-quantile candidate cuts (10th..90th pct, 24 cuts, >=30 rows each side) for the max DROP in a CONTINUOUS 2-segment linear-fit SSE `y ~ [1, x, max(x-c,0)]` (a slope-aware stump), greedily adding the relu leg of each found `tau` to the design so a second DISTINCT slope change is detected in the residual; emit `relu(x-tau)=max(x-tau,0)` + `relu(tau-x)=max(tau-x,0)` (optional `1[x>tau]` indicator via `fe_hinge_emit_indicator`). GATE -- the key design finding: a single relu leg is MONOTONE in x, hence MI-INVARIANT by the data-processing inequality, so the standard MI-uplift gate (the spline/Fourier path) DROPS it exactly as it drops the isotonic/RankGauss monotone reshapes (the project's MI-vs-linear-usability principle, and backlog #14's explicit caveat). The backlog's "clears MI-uplift over raw x" premise is therefore WRONG for a monotone relu leg; admission is instead the HELD-OUT INCREMENTAL LINEAR-R^2 over raw x on the `%3` stride slice (`_heldout_incremental_r2`: does `[1, x, relu(x-tau)]` beat `[1, x]` OOS? -- the hinge's value is the SECOND SLOPE it hands a downstream linear/shallow model, not MI). On top of that the DETECTOR runs a held-out tau-validation (the 2-segment fit must beat plain linear OOS R^2 by `fe_hinge_min_heldout_r2_uplift`, default 0.02) so a chance breakpoint / pure noise admits NO hinge. The detector is fed RAW continuous y (NOT the qcut-to-10-bins coercion the MI FE stages use -- quantile-binning a monotone slope-change target collapses the saturating top tier into one bin and DESTROYS the kink: measured qcut y -> 0 breakpoints, raw y -> tau recovered). LEAK-SAFE replay: new recipe kind `hinge_basis` stores only `{tau, side}` -- NO y -- so transform replay is the pure function `np.maximum(x-tau,0)` / `np.maximum(tau-x,0)` / `(x>tau)` (`_apply_hinge_basis`, registered in the `apply_recipe` dispatcher + the `EngineeredRecipe` kind Literal + the `engineered_recipes` facade re-export; mirrors `build_orth_spline_recipe`). Independent of `fe_hybrid_orth_enable` (runs after the extra-basis stage, restricted to RAW numeric source columns so every recipe is 1-deep replayable). BENCHMARK (n=4000, x~U(0,1), `D:/Temp/bench_hinge_11.py`): on the SLOPE-CHANGE fixture `y=2x+5*max(x-0.7,0)+noise` the detector recovers tau_hat=0.688 (true 0.7) and the held-out Ridge R^2 of `[x, hinge]` is **0.9888**, BEATING raw x (0.9071), the best Chebyshev block deg<=4 (0.9857), AND the best cubic B-spline block (0.9883) -- it wins because the knot sits AT the data-dependent kink while the spline's fixed quantile knots round it off and Chebyshev rings around it. COMPLEMENTARITY CONTROL (mandatory, proves it is not a spline-clone): on the SMOOTH `y=x^2` the hinge (0.9412) correctly LOSES to a degree-2 poly `[x, x^2]` (0.9737). NOISE CONTROL: 0/20 pure-noise frames admit any hinge (held-out tau-validation rejects the chance breakpoint). DEDUP CONTROL: a purely-linear monotone target `y=3x+noise` emits 0 hinge legs (the 2-segment fit does not beat plain linear OOS, so the near-collinear-with-x risk never even materialises); when a genuine saturating-monotone target DOES emit a leg it is near-collinear with x (Spearman |rho| 0.93-0.94) and the existing cross-stage Spearman dedup (|rho|>=0.99) is the backstop for true near-duplicate legs (e.g. the two close taus a basis stage can emit). DEFAULT OFF (opt-in justified per the dispatcher rule): it is a NICHE operator helping only on a genuine slope-change signal, and its per-column cost is a quantile-cut SSE scan (~2.2 ms/col, the stage hotspot per cProfile) -- distinct from the near-free default-on fixed-grid Fourier path; cProfile bench-attempt-rejected (2026-06-09): a normal-equations 3x3 solve replacing the per-candidate `lstsq` was 2.2x SLOWER (the `A.T@A` over n=4000 rows costs more than `lstsq`'s SVD), documented in-code.
