Because a hinge leg is MI-invariant, MRMR's final MI-based selection does NOT keep it (same as RankGauss) -- the operator's value is demonstrated on the standalone held-out Ridge R^2 (the biz_value test), exactly how RankGauss's biz_value is judged. Triad: unit + biz_value (Ridge-R^2 win on slope-change + complementarity loss on smooth) + cProfile in `tests/feature_selection/test_hinge_basis_fe.py` (9 tests: breakpoint recovery, noise rejection 0/20, leak-safe bit-exact recipe replay + pickle roundtrip + y-independence, the two biz_value gates, default-off byte-stability, opt-in produced-recipe + replay). Findings in `D:/Temp/hinge_results.md`.
- MRMR now applies an n-INVARIANT raw-vs-engineered CONDITIONAL-REDUNDANCY DROP as the final selection step (`MRMR(fe_drop_redundant_raw_operands=True)`, default on; `_fe_raw_redundancy_drop.drop_redundant_raw_operands`). The greedy MRMR order admits a raw operand on its high MARGINAL relevance BEFORE the engineered child built from it is in support, so the redundancy penalty never fires against it, and the small-n protective retention / Fix-B augmentation then re-add it -- leaving a fully-subsumed operand beside the engineered feature that determines `y` from it (e.g. raw `a, b` beside `div(neg(a),sqrt(b))` for `y=(a**2)/b`, since `(a/sqrt(b))**2 = a**2/b`). The final sweep removes such operands using the SAME debiased excess-CMI idea the engineered-vs-engineered S5 gate validated: a selected raw operand of a surviving engineered child is dropped iff its CMI given the engineered survivors sits within its conditional-permutation floor OR its DEBIASED EXCESS (`max(0, cmi - null_mean)`) is below `fe_raw_redundancy_retain_frac` (TAU=0.15) of the weakest consuming survivor's own debiased excess. Three binning hazards are handled so the verdict is faithful at every n: (1) the engineered survivor is binned FROM ITS CONTINUOUS values (a fit-time snapshot of `_engineered_continuous_`, not the lossy ~10-code screening bins in `data`, which leave a subsumed DENOMINATOR operand a spurious residual CMI); (2) it is binned at the TARGET cardinality so it resolves `y` as finely as the target codes; (3) the target is RE-BINNED equi-frequency from the continuous `y` for the anchor (the screening `classes_y` is heavily imbalanced on a skewed regression target -- ~89% of rows in one bin for `(a**2)/b` -- which crushes the engineered anchor's MI and inflates the operand's apparent residual fraction). The downstream Fix-B augmentation and the empty-RAW rescue both now defer to this verdict (excluded names are never resurrected; an intended engineered-only support is flagged so the rescue does not mistake it for a "screen returned 0 raw" emergency and pull in the next pure-noise column). Validated drop of `a, b` for `y=(a**2)/b` across n=1000..50000 while keeping a genuine independent additive raw (`c` in `y=a**2+0.30*c`) -- no over-correction. Pure function (continuous snapshot is a fit-time local, never an attr -> fitted MRMR stays picklable, transform parity max-abs-diff 0.0). Covered by `test_biz_value_mrmr_fe_canonical.py::test_subsumed_ratio_operands_drop_at_small_n` (+ opt-out / genuine-signal / pure-noise companions).

- Neural (flat MLP / tabular PyTorch-Lightning) models now have NATIVE categorical-feature support via LEARNABLE entity embeddings (Guo & Berkhahn 2016): one `nn.Embedding` per categorical, trained end-to-end, for ALL target types (regression / binary / multiclass / multilabel). A new `CategoricalEmbedding` module (`mlframe.training.neural._categorical_embeddings`) holds an `nn.Embedding(card+1, d)` per cat (the `+1` row is the reserved unknown/overflow slot; per-cat `embed_dim` defaults to the fastai heuristic `min(50, round(1.6*card**0.56))` or a fixed `categorical_embed_dim`); its `forward` embeds the leading `k` integer-code columns and passes the trailing numeric block through, exposing `out_features`. `generate_mlp` gained `categorical_cardinalities` / `categorical_embed_dim` params that prepend the embedding (before the optional numerical PLR embedding, since cats are the leading columns) and update the trunk's input width accordingly. The estimator factorizes raw cats at its fit boundary: `_FitMixin._factorize_cats_fit` pops `cat_features` from `fit_params`, `pd.factorize`s each named column to int codes (NaN/unseen -> the reserved code = cardinality), reorders the code columns leading, stashes picklable `_cat_code_maps_` / `_cat_cardinalities_` / `_cat_cols_`, and threads the cardinalities into `generate_mlp`; `_apply_cat_codes` replays the identical mapping at predict (`_PredictMixin._predict_raw`). This runs BEFORE `_validate_no_nan_inf` so the validator never sees object dtype, and there is no target leakage (embeddings are learned, not target-derived). A new estimator knob `use_learnable_cat_embeddings: bool = True` (default ON for the MLP) gates the in-estimator factorization; when ON, `NeuralNetStrategy.requires_encoding` is a property returning `not use_learnable_cat_embeddings` so `build_pipeline` SKIPS the `CatBoostEncoder` and the cats reach the estimator RAW, while imputation/scaling are restricted to the numeric block via a new `_NumericOnlyTransformer` (raw cat codes must not be scaled -- it would destroy them as embedding indices). The suite threads `cat_features` into the MLP's fit_params via `_neural_extra_fit_invariant` (scoped to `mlframe_model_name=="mlp"`; NGB shares the strategy but cannot consume learnable embeddings, so it never receives raw cats). Set `use_learnable_cat_embeddings=False` to restore the legacy target-encoder path verbatim. Fit->pickle->unpickle->predict round-trips bit-identically (maps/cardinalities are plain dict/list; the `nn.Embedding` weights ride in the torch state_dict). biz_value: on a non-monotone `y = lookup[cat] + noise` synthetic (8 cats) the learnable-embedding MLP scores held-out R^2 ~0.982 vs ~0.68-0.72 for the same cat fed as a single ordinal numeric column (delta 0.265-0.300 over 3 seeds). The RECURRENT model is out of scope (follow-on). cProfile: the cat path is not a hotspot (`forward` is a per-cat torch gather over a handful of columns; `_factorize_cats_fit`/`_apply_cat_codes` are one-shot per fit/predict) -- no numba/cupy ladder warranted (harness: `neural/_benchmarks/profile_categorical_embeddings.py`).
- Neural (RECURRENT / sequence PyTorch-Lightning) models now have the SAME native learnable categorical-embedding support as the flat MLP, applied to the TABULAR (auxiliary, non-sequence) feature block in HYBRID and FEATURES_ONLY modes, for all supported target types (regression / binary / multiclass / multilabel). The HYBRID tabular head does NOT call `generate_mlp` -- `RecurrentTorchModel._build_model` builds an `MLPHead` directly on the concatenation of the RNN/Transformer sequence encoding and the aux features -- so the reused `CategoricalEmbedding` (`mlframe.training.neural._categorical_embeddings`, unchanged) is prepended on the AUX block: when `aux_categorical_cardinalities` is set, the embedding maps the leading `k` integer cat-code aux columns + passes the trailing numerics through, and the head's input width becomes `sum(embed_dims) + (aux_input - k) + seq_encoding`; the embedding lives BEFORE the shared output head (target-type-agnostic). The recurrent estimator has its OWN fit/predict path (NOT `_FitMixin`/`_base_predict`), so the factorization is mirrored in a new `_RecurrentCatEmbeddingMixin` (`_recurrent_cat_embeddings.py`) that the wrappers mix in: `_factorize_cats_fit` pops `cat_features`, `pd.factorize`s each named TABULAR column to int codes (NaN/unseen -> reserved code = cardinality), reorders the code columns leading, stashes picklable `_cat_code_maps_` / `_cat_cardinalities_` / `_cat_cols_`, and threads the cardinalities into `RecurrentTorchModel`; `_apply_cat_codes` / `_prepare_predict_features` replay it at predict. Crucially the SEQUENCES tensor is never touched, and SEQUENCE_ONLY mode (no aux block, `features=None`) no-ops cleanly. The numeric-only scaler fit (`_scaler_fit_numeric_only` + the numeric-slice transform in `_create_dataset`) leaves the leading cat-code columns unscaled (scaling integer codes would corrupt them as embedding indices). A new wrapper knob `use_learnable_cat_embeddings: bool = True` (default ON) gates the in-estimator factorization; when ON, `RecurrentModelStrategy.requires_encoding` is now a property returning `not use_learnable_cat_embeddings`, so `build_pipeline` SKIPS the `CatBoostEncoder` and the cats reach the estimator RAW with imputation/scaling restricted to the numeric block via `_NumericOnlyTransformer` (the legacy CatBoostEncoder path is preserved verbatim when the knob is OFF). The suite threads `cat_features` into the recurrent fit at its actual call site (`train_recurrent_models` in `_phase_recurrent.py`, gated on a tabular features frame being present + the model's `input_mode != sequence`, with a TypeError-fallback that drops the kwarg for older wrapper signatures) and additionally broadens the body's `_neural_extra_fit_invariant` scoping to the recurrent model names (`lstm`/`gru`/`rnn`/`transformer`) for the path where a recurrent name is placed in `mlframe_models`. Fit->pickle->predict round-trips bit-identically (max-abs-diff 0.0); `save`/`load` persist the cat maps/cardinalities and reconstruct the aux embedding. biz_value: on a non-monotone `y = lookup[cat]` zig-zag signal (sequence + numerics carry no target info) the learnable-embedding HYBRID regressor beats the same cat fed as an ordinal numeric code by >=1.10x train RMSE. `recurrent.py` stayed under the 1k-LOC monolith threshold via the single flat sibling carve (`_recurrent_cat_embeddings.py`) that also took the predict-cache-key helper. Tests: `tests/training/neural/test_recurrent_categorical_embeddings_boundary.py` (9 tests: HYBRID regression/binary/multiclass, SEQUENCE_ONLY no-op x2, pickle round-trip, unseen-category, knob-off, biz_value). Set `use_learnable_cat_embeddings=False` to restore the legacy target-encoder path.
- Neural (MLP / tabular PyTorch-Lightning) models can now consume embedding-vector (`List(Float32)`) and free-text columns. `NeuralNetStrategy` inserts a `NeuralEmbeddingTextEncoder` preprocessing step (`mlframe.training.neural.feature_prep`) that expands embedding vectors into their numeric components (fed directly to the MLP) and turns text columns into dense semantic vectors via a real HuggingFace transformer (the project's `HuggingFaceProvider`: `AutoTokenizer` + `AutoModel`, default `intfloat/multilingual-e5-small`, mean/cls/max pooled) -- NOT bag-of-words TF-IDF, which discards word order/semantics and isn't learned. Target-type-agnostic (runs before the network, so regression/binary/multiclass/multilabel/learning-to-rank heads all see numeric input). The encoding is applied at the neural estimator's own fit/predict boundary (`_FitMixin._encode_emb_text_fit` + `_predict_raw`): it pops `embedding_features`/`text_features` from `fit_params`, encodes train + eval X before validation/input-dim, stashes the fitted encoder, and replays it at predict. Validated with real models: a real Lightning MLP fits + predicts on an embedding-`List` column and on a real-HF-embedded text column, and a biz_value test shows HF text embeddings generalize to unseen synonyms (>=0.80 held-out) and beat TF-IDF by >=0.15. `transformers` added to the `[neural]` extra. NOTE: full suite auto-routing (so embedding/text columns survive feature selection to reach the MLP) is gated off for now -- `NeuralNetStrategy.supports_text_features`/`supports_embedding_features` stay False, because MRMR's discretiser/selector can't process non-scalar embedding `List` columns (raises on ndarray cells); enabling end-to-end suite routing requires MRMR to pass non-scalar embedding columns through. The boundary encoder applies whenever such columns do reach the estimator.
- MRMR multi-step FE now discovers COMPOSITES of two engineered features (`fe_max_steps>=2`). On `y = a**2/b + log(c)*sin(d)` the step-2 search builds the additive `add(div(sqr(a),abs(b)), mul(log(c),sin(d)))` -- ~the entire deterministic signal -- and selects it as the TOP factor (it correctly makes the raw operands + the two partial engineered features redundant). Three causes blocked it: (1) `check_prospective_fe_pairs` skipped any operand not in `original_cols` (raw `feature_names_in_` only), so an `(eng_i, eng_j)` pair never produced a candidate -- engineered operands are now resolved by name (new `allow_engineered_operands` param, default on); (2) the augmented frame carries the DISCRETISED bin codes of engineered columns and combining bin codes is severely lossy (the additive composite kept MI 0.88 from codes vs 1.81 -- the full signal -- from continuous values, failing the engineered-MI gate at 0.51<0.994), so the CONTINUOUS engineered values are now fed through (new `engineered_operand_values` param, backed by a fit-time `self._engineered_continuous_` store cleared at the end of `fit`); (3) a composite whose parents are themselves engineered had no replayable recipe and was dropped from `transform()` -- nested-parent recipes (`build_unary_binary_recipe(nested_parent_a/b=...)` stored in `extra`, recursively replayed in `_apply_unary_binary` from raw columns alone) now make it fully replayable (verified Spearman 0.995 vs the true signal on held-out data, pickle round-trip transform-parity max-abs-diff 0.0). A principled feed-forward cap (`MRMR(fe_max_engineered_operands=8)`, top-K engineered operands by marginal MI; 0 disables, <0 = no cap) bounds the O(k^2) pair blow-up. `fe_max_steps=1` (the default) is unchanged -- no step-2 composites. Covered by `test_mrmr_fe_composite_feedforward.py`.
- MRMR engineered-feature acceptance gained a principled, constant-free CONDITIONAL-MI REDUNDANCY gate (strategy S5), now the DEFAULT (`MRMR(fe_acceptance="conditional_mi")`). It replaces the hardcoded `fe_min_engineered_mi_prevalence` joint-prevalence RATIO as the final acceptance criterion: after the per-pair search picks one best engineered column per pair, a greedy CMI-MRMR runs over the surviving pool (`_fe_cmi_redundancy_gate.apply_cmi_redundancy_gate`, wired into `_mrmr_fe_step._run_fe_step` before materialisation). A candidate is admitted iff its conditional MI with `y` GIVEN the already-admitted ENGINEERED features clears BOTH (1) a conditional-permutation floor (within-stratum shuffle null, the significance leg) AND (2) a scale-free fraction `fe_engineered_cmi_retain_frac` (TAU, default 0.15) of the WEAKEST admitted feature's CMI (the order-of-magnitude redundancy separator -- a redundant feature sits a few x above its own floor yet ~12-84x below every genuine one, so the floor alone misses it; both legs are load-bearing). The decisive conditioning is on the OTHER admitted engineered features -- NOT a candidate's own operands (CMI given own operands is ~0 for every feature, a data-processing-inequality trap) and NOT raw top-k (operand-coverage collisions kill real features). This drops a redundant cross-signal engineered column whose information is wholly carried by the admitted genuine features (e.g. `sub(exp(a),invcbrt(c))` once `div(sqr(a),abs(b))`+`mul(log(c),sin(d))` are in) while keeping every genuine column carrying a private interaction term -- WITHOUT any per-dataset ratio constant. On the user formulas `y=a**2/b+f/5+log(c)*sin(d)` and `y=0.2*a**2/b+f/5+log(c*2)*sin(d/3)` at n=30k it accepts the two genuine engineered forms and rejects the redundant one on BOTH (the weaker-signal F2 now recovers its real features, which the 0.97->0.90 prevalence stopgap under-recovered); `fe_max_steps=2` composition is unaffected (the high-CMI composite is not rejected). TAU is a fraction of an in-data CMI quantity, NOT an MI-nats constant; measured robust window [0.084, 1.0). All MI/CMI reuses the production primitives (`_cmi_from_binned`/`_quantile_bin`/`_renumber_joint`), no MI reimplementation; the gate is a pure function (no live state -> fitted MRMR stays picklable). Degenerate fallback: <2 candidates or tiny n -> admit on marginal significance (never reject everything). The legacy ratio path is preserved as `MRMR(fe_acceptance="prevalence_ratio")` for fallback / byte-reproduction of old fits. Covered by `test_fe_cmi_redundancy_gate.py`. Validated 10/10 vs four failing acceptance strategies across 16 (seed, formula) cells. n-INVARIANT (2026-06-08 hardening): the relative-gap leg now compares DEBIASED EXCESS CMI -- each candidate's observed CMI minus the MEAN of its OWN conditional-permutation null (the within-stratum shuffle preserves the candidate's conditional marginal, so the null reproduces the SAME finite-sample plug-in bias). The bias is present in both terms and cancels, so the excess is ~unbiased at any n; the TAU fraction and the admitted-feature anchors (including the seed's marginal-permutation-debiased excess) are taken on the excess, not the raw CMI. This removes the prior finite-sample limitation: a redundant feature's excess collapses to ~0 while genuine features keep a large positive excess, so the spurious feature is REJECTED at EVERY n -- validated 40/40 over n in {1000, 5000, 20000, 50000} x {F1, F2} x 5 seeds (the prior raw-CMI gate wrongly admitted the weaker-signal F2 spurious feature at n~=20k: raw cmi 0.040 > raw rel_bar 0.037; debiased excess 0.008 << 0.036). The significance FLOOR leg is unchanged (already a same-bias comparison). n=100k follows by construction (the bias cancels regardless of n); an 8GB box OOMs the downstream FE buffer at 100k, so the parametrized sweep tops out at 50k.
- GPU (cupy + numba.cuda) acceleration of the friend-graph BUILD (`friend_graph_gpu.py`): the O(k^2) pairwise feature-feature MI edge pass + the k node entropy/relevance stats run on the GPU for a selected set of `k` features (the explicit-diagnostic build and the `friend_graph_prune`/cluster path). The GPU does only the integer joint/marginal histogram counting (the pair joint code `va + vb*nbins[a]` exactly reproduces `merge_vars`' dense encoding); the Shannon entropy + MI (`H_a + H_b - H_ab`) + every keep/drop decision (significance floor, ADC arrow direction, garbage classification) stay on the bit-exact CPU path, so the result is BIT-IDENTICAL to the legacy CPU build (verified by `array_equal` of node H/relevance and every edge MI, plus full nodes/edges/classification/prune parity at k up to 50). CPU-vs-GPU dispatch is keyed by `(k, n)` via the per-host `kernel_tuning` cache (`get_or_tune` + a registered `@kernel_tuner` spec `friend_graph_build`), defaulting to the CPU path (safe) and preferring the numba.cuda atomic-histogram backend in the GPU region (it scales with the O(n*k^2) edge work; the cupy dense pair-index buffer blows up at large k). New `MRMR(friend_graph_gpu_backend=...)` knob (`None`=auto-dispatch, `"cpu"`/`"cupy"`/`"cuda"`=force) and `build_friend_graph(gpu_backend=...)` parameter. Dev-box (GTX 1050 Ti) measured cuda speedup over the CPU njit build: 1.35x at n=5000/k=64 up to 3.61x at n=20000/k=128, bit-identical at every cell.
- Anonymized default kernel-tuning seed + its generator. `mlframe-gen-default-tuning` (console entry / `python -m mlframe.feature_selection._benchmarks.gen_default_tuning`) discovers every registered `@kernel_tuner` spec, ensures a measured tuning exists on the running host for each kernel's current `code_version` (reading the pyutilz v3 per-host immutable cache; running the kernel's sweep synchronously via `tune_spec(skip_existing=...)` only when missing -- `--force` re-sweeps all), then writes an anonymized `src/mlframe/feature_selection/_benchmarks/default_kernel_tuning.json`. Anonymization drops the `hw_fingerprint`, prunes stale `code_version`s (keeps only the live one per kernel), strips per-host `wall_ms`, and tags every region with an abstract `device` profile (`cpu`/`gpu`, derived from `backend_choice`) so a future multi-host merge can vote/average per region. The file is sorted (kernels + nested keys) for clean diffs. `mlframe.feature_selection.filters._kernel_tuning` registers it once at FS-package import via `pyutilz`'s `register_default_cache`, so a fresh host gets measurement-derived dispatch on a local cache MISS (before the hand heuristic) while its own background sweep runs; the local measured cache always wins. Missing file / missing pyutilz is a silent no-op. A pre-commit hook (`gen-default-tuning-check`) runs the generator with `--check` (no sweep) so a kernel edit that moved a `code_version` fails the commit until the defaults are regenerated; escape with `git commit --no-verify`, regenerate via the manual stage `pre-commit run --hook-stage manual gen-default-tuning`. The initial committed `default_kernel_tuning.json` was generated on the dev host (7 kernels / 66 anonymized regions: batch_mi_noise_gate, batch_pair_mi, batch_rmse, cat_fe_perm_kernel, discretize_2d_array, dtw_dispatch, unary_elementwise); kernels whose offline sweep returned no regions there are omitted until regenerated on a fully idle box.

### Changed

- MRMR FE HINGE / change-point operator is now **DEFAULT-ON** (`fe_hinge_enable=True`, was `False`; `_hinge_basis_fe.py` + `_mrmr_fit_impl.py`), fixing a hidden-champion default-policy mistake: the operator WON its benchmark (held-out Ridge R^2 0.9888 on the slope-change fixture, beating raw / best-Chebyshev / best-B-spline) yet shipped opt-in, so the win was hidden from users who do not flip the flag. The naive flip alone would have been wrong -- a single relu leg is MONOTONE in x, hence MI-INVARIANT by the DPI, so MRMR's greedy MI screen DROPS it as redundant with raw x (verified: with the old default-on the legs were generated then dropped, 0 surviving into `support_`). The CORRECT fix routes retention through linear-usability, not MI, with DEFERRED MATERIALISATION so the legs never perturb pair-composite recovery: (1) the hinge stage runs early (it needs the raw source columns before pair-FE rewrites them) but BUFFERS its held-out-validated legs instead of appending them to the candidate matrix -- so the pair-FE / screening loop recovers genuine composites untouched (a naive append let the pair search consume a leg as an operand AND let a leg's high marginal MI crowd the real composites out of selection: measured on `y=a**2/b+log(c)*sin(d)` the legs on b/d displaced `div(sqr(a),abs(b))` / `mul(log(c),sin(d))`, breaking the canonical FE-recovery contract); (2) after the FE loop settles, a support-finalisation block MATERIALISES the buffered legs (bin-codes into `data`/`cols`/`nbins`, values into `X`, recipes into the registry) and re-adds the deserving ones, gated by a SELECTED-SET INCREMENTAL-R^2 test: a leg is kept ONLY if it lifts a held-out linear fit over the already-selected feature set PLUS the source and its degree-2 poly `[src, src^2]`. That gate is the self-limit -- a leg subsumed by a surviving composite (b/d on the canonical) adds ~0 and is dropped, a smooth `y=x^2` is fit by `src^2` so its kink adds ~0 and is dropped, while a GENUINE slope change with no competing composite (and not a smooth curve) clears the floor and is kept (the hidden-champion win). On the DEFAULT path now: the slope-change fixture RETAINS its hinge legs in `support_` and `MRMR().fit().transform()` lifts held-out Ridge R^2 to 0.979 vs raw 0.923; pure noise / a purely-linear column / a smooth `y=x^2` retain ZERO hinge legs (no spurious columns on neutral data); and the canonical `y=a**2/b+log(c)*sin(d)` recovery is byte-restored (both pair composites recover, no hinge crowding) -- canonical + hinge suites green. COST DISPATCH (the legitimate concern behind the old opt-in): a cheap 3-cut SSE PRE-CHECK (`_hinge_slope_change_plausible`) short-circuits the full 24-cut scan on any column without a plausible slope change, so default-on does not bloat wide / large-p fits -- measured on a wide p=50 n=4000 neutral fit the hinge stage drops from 452 ms (no gate, 9.0 ms/col) to 100 ms (with gate, 2.0 ms/col), a 78% reduction; a no-kink column's detect cost falls ~12x (15 ms -> 1.2 ms). Tests reframed to the default-on behaviour (default-ON assertion, default-path retain + biz-value-win, self-limiting on noise/linear/smooth, the cost pre-check) in `tests/feature_selection/test_hinge_basis_fe.py`. HIDDEN-CHAMPION AUDIT (same pattern, the user's "something smells bad" generalised): the other MI-invariant / benchmark-proven OPT-IN operators were checked -- RankGauss (`fe_rankgauss_enable`) is NOT a hidden champion (already a documented, benchmark-backed opt-in: REDUNDANT with the cubic-B-spline quantile-knot block, `|corr(qnorm(x),y)|` matches the spline-linear extract to ~3 decimals on heavy-tailed monotone x, the lone OOS lift a fixed-alpha-Ridge artifact RidgeCV closes; it beats raw but NOT the spline that subsumes it -- whereas the hinge BEATS the spline, which is exactly why the hinge was the genuine hidden win); the order-2 Miller-Madow ratio gate (`fe_mm_debias_prevalence`) and the synergy / II-routing levers (`fe_pairwise_ratio_enable`, `fe_ii_routing_enable`) all carry dated in-code `bench-rejected as default` notes with numbers showing default-on REGRESSES the common case (admits noise on the weak-F2 cross-mix). No other MI-invariant operator is wired (isotonic is referenced only as a conceptual example). Net: 1 genuine hidden champion fixed (hinge -> default-on + self-limiting routing), the rest confirmed as justified opt-in decisions.
- MRMR FE (backlog #4 / frontier-idea #9): the order-1 maxT permutation-null gain floor (`pooled_permutation_null_gain_floor`) now uses the EFFECTIVE OCCUPIED bin count (`k_eff = #non-empty cells`) instead of the nominal cardinality `nbins` in its Miller-Madow bias term `(k_x-1)(k_y-1)/2n`. Heavy-tailed engineered columns (e.g. `a**2/b`) bin to ~8 occupied cells out of `nbins=16`; the nominal-K term charged bias for empty cells that contribute no plug-in inflation, OVER-correcting the per-candidate MI and understating the chance floor. Occupied-K is the statistically correct Miller-Madow (matching the already-occupied-K `entropy_miller_madow`) and tracks the true (null=0) MI 3.1-3.2x tighter on heavy-tailed columns (mean abs residual: nominal 0.04516/0.01780 -> occupied 0.01403/0.00571 at n=2000/5000, 20 seeds). The integrated q95-max floor is unchanged (0.0) on a pure-noise heavy-tailed pool (both clamp to >=0), so there is no noise-floor regression; the accuracy gain surfaces in mixed signal/noise pools where occupied-K stops over-charging genuine heavy-tailed candidates. Pure estimator-accuracy refinement, no new knob, default-on. Verified by `test_perm_null_occupied_mm.py`; all 59 permutation/floor biz-value tests stay green. (frontier-idea #7, the permutation-null-calibrated PREVALENCE bar, was investigated and BENCH-REJECTED: under shuffle the `best_1d_mi/pair_mi` ratio is not a stable null statistic -- the genuine `He2(a)*b` pair already scores ratio ~0.30 << the hardcoded 0.97 bar so 0.97 does NOT reject it, and the null q05 ~0.17 is STRICTER than 0.97 and would mis-reject the genuine pair; no clean genuine-vs-noise separation. No source change.)
- Reorganized the flat `models/` ensembling family into a cohesive `models/ensembling/` subpackage: the former `ensembling.py` facade became `models/ensembling/__init__.py`, and the nine `_ensembling_*.py` siblings plus `_per_member_tuning.py` moved into the package with the redundant `_ensembling_` prefix dropped (`base.py`, `member_metrics.py`, `predict.py`, `process_method.py`, `quality_gate.py`, `score.py`, `score_gate.py`, `score_flavours.py`, `score_validate.py`, `per_member_tuning.py`). The public surface is unchanged -- `from mlframe.models.ensembling import score_ensemble / combine_probs / ensemble_probabilistic_predictions / compute_member_quality_gate / EnsembleLeaderboard / compare_ensembles / ...` resolves exactly as before. Test importers that reached the old deep-private flat modules (`mlframe.models._ensembling_*`, `mlframe.models._per_member_tuning`) repointed to the new public submodule paths (`mlframe.models.ensembling.<submodule>`); the W14A facade-LOC source-grep sensor repointed to `models/ensembling/score.py`; the annotation baseline refreshed (six entries, file-path shift only). Behaviour-preserving.
- Reorganized the flat `metrics/` regression modules into a cohesive `metrics/regression/` subpackage: `_regression_metrics.py`, `_regression_extras.py`, and `_regression_deviance.py` now live under `metrics/regression/`, re-exported through the new `metrics/regression/__init__.py`. The public surface is unchanged -- `mlframe.metrics` and `mlframe.metrics.core` continue to expose every regression metric exactly as before -- and `mlframe.metrics.regression` is the new public path for direct imports.
- Reorganized the flat `metrics/` calibration modules into a cohesive `metrics/calibration/` subpackage: `_calibration_plot.py` and `_calibration_metrics.py` now live under `metrics/calibration/`, re-exported through the new `metrics/calibration/__init__.py`. The public surface is unchanged -- `mlframe.metrics.core` still exposes `show_calibration_plot` / `fast_calibration_metrics` / `integral_calibration_error_from_metrics` etc. and `mlframe.metrics.show_plots_unless_agg` is preserved -- and `mlframe.metrics.calibration` is the new public path for direct imports.
- Reorganized the flat `metrics/` classification modules into a cohesive `metrics/classification/` subpackage: `_classification_report.py`, `_classification_extras.py`, and `_classification_calibration.py` now live under `metrics/classification/`, re-exported through the new `metrics/classification/__init__.py`. The public surface is unchanged -- `mlframe.metrics.core` still exposes `format_classification_report` / `fast_classification_report` / `ks_statistic` / `hosmer_lemeshow_test` / `fast_ice_only` etc. -- and `mlframe.metrics.classification` is the new public path for direct imports.

### Refactor

- Split the three MRMR monolith modules into subpackages (behaviour-preserving clean moves): `_mrmr_fe_step.py` (1.6k LOC) -> `_mrmr_fe_step/` (`_step_core` holds `_run_fe_step` verbatim + `_helpers` + facade), `mrmr.py` (3.7k) -> `mrmr/` (`_mrmr_class` holds the `MRMR` class verbatim + facade; class identity, `_FIT_CACHE`, pickle, and the method bindings all preserved), `_mrmr_fit_impl.py` (7.5k) -> `_mrmr_fit_impl/` (`_fit_impl_core` holds `_fit_impl` verbatim + `_helpers` + facade). Each giant single function/class moved verbatim (LOC-budget exempt, like `_pairs_core`); the facade `__init__` re-exports the full public surface so every importer + the `MRMR._fit_impl` / `MRMR._run_fe_step` bindings resolve unchanged. Lazy-import depths fixed; source-inspection audit tests + LOC budget updated. Verified facade/binding/identity/pickle + a real MRMR fit per split.
- Carved the BorutaShap shadow-feature construction + statistical / hit-test helpers out of the 1011-line `feature_selection/boruta_shap.py` into a sibling `_boruta_shap_shadow_stats.py` (same method-binding split already used for `_boruta_shap_fit_explain.py` / `_boruta_shap_io_plot.py`), dropping the facade to 703 LOC (under the 1k ceiling). Thirteen methods moved verbatim (`calculate_hits`, `create_shadow_features`, `calculate_Zscore`, `feature_importance`, `isolation_forest`, `get_5_percent`, `get_5_percent_splits`, `find_sample`, `binomial_H0_test`, `symetric_difference_between_two_arrays`, `find_index_of_true_in_array`, `bonferoni_corrections`, `test_features`) and are bound back onto `BorutaShap` at the parent's bottom (instance methods directly; the static ones re-wrapped with `staticmethod`). `binomial_H0_test` lazy-imports the parent's memoized `_binom_test_cached` in-body to avoid the parent<->sibling import cycle. The class's public method surface is unchanged. Behaviour-preserving; verified bit-identical (same 29 selected features + byte-identical importance-history hash before/after on the breast-cancer demo with a fixed-seed surrogate).
- Carved the three 2-D quantile-discretisation numba kernels (`_quantile_edges_2d_njit`, `_searchsorted_2d_right_njit`, `_searchsorted_2d_right_njit_parallel`) out of the 1045-line `feature_selection/filters/discretization/__init__.py` into a sibling `discretization/_kernels.py`, dropping the package facade to 870 LOC (under the 1k ceiling). The kernels moved verbatim (decorators / docstrings / the `fastmath` bench-rejected note preserved); the parent imports them back so `discretize_2d_quantile_batch` is unchanged and re-exports them, so the package's import surface is identical -- `from mlframe.feature_selection.filters.discretization import _quantile_edges_2d_njit / _searchsorted_2d_right_njit / _searchsorted_2d_right_njit_parallel` resolves exactly as before. Behaviour-preserving; MRMR selection verified bit-identical.
- Split the 7858-line `tests/training/_fuzz_combo.py` fuzz harness into a cohesive `tests/training/_fuzz_combo/` subpackage: `axes.py` (the `MODELS` + `AXES` grid), `combo.py` (the `FuzzCombo` dataclass + canonicalisation), `xfail.py` (`KNOWN_XFAIL_RULES` + `xfail_reason`), `enumerator.py` (`enumerate_combos` / `enumerate_combos_3way` + the pairwise/triple-greedy sampler), `builders.py` (the shared suite-config builders), `results_log.py` (`log_combo_outcome` / `read_fail_summary` + `RESULTS_LOG`), `perf_mode.py` (`apply_perf_mode`), and `frame_builder.py` (`build_frame_for_combo`), all re-exported through `_fuzz_combo/__init__.py`. The public surface is unchanged -- `from tests.training._fuzz_combo import AXES / FuzzCombo / enumerate_combos / build_frame_for_combo / apply_perf_mode / xfail_reason / ...` resolves exactly as before for `test_fuzz_suite.py`, `test_fuzz_3way_suite.py`, the W11C cross-axis sensors, the profiling scripts, and the 1M-run harness. `RESULTS_LOG` keeps resolving to `tests/training/_fuzz_results.jsonl` (via `Path(__file__).parents[1]`). Behaviour-preserving.
- Carved the non-test helpers out of the 1590-line `tests/training/test_fuzz_suite.py` into a sibling `tests/training/_fuzz_suite_helpers.py` (per-combo config builders, recurrent/outlier/preprocessing config helpers, BorutaShap-kwargs builder, prediction-invariant + serialization-roundtrip checkers, the dependency-skip utility), imported back into the test module. The `@pytest.mark.parametrize`d test functions, the `COMBOS = enumerate_combos(...)` enumeration, and the autouse cleanup fixture stay in `test_fuzz_suite.py`, which drops to 729 LOC (under the 1k ceiling).

### Fixed

- MRMR raw-retention re-add now passes a PERMUTATION-SIGNIFICANCE gate, so a PURE-NOISE raw column flagged as `_prefe_screened_raw_` by a small-n false positive is no longer re-injected into `support_`. The coarse-binning plug-in MI is upward-biased, so a column NOT in the target equation (e.g. `e` in `y=log(a)*c+0.4*f`) can leave a tiny residual debiased MI the screen confirms and retention then re-adds, padding the support with noise (`['e', 'mul(sin(a),neg(c))']` instead of the clean signal at n=2000). The re-add now runs the SAME within-data permutation-significance test the empty-RAW rescue uses (`mi_direct` on the screen's own `data`/`nbins`, 32 perms, `MLFRAME_MRMR_NULL_SIGNIF_ALPHA`); a candidate sitting WITHIN its own null (p>=alpha) is withheld, while a genuinely weak-but-real raw above its null still passes. Best-effort (a kernel failure falls through to the permissive re-add -- never drop a screening-confirmed raw on an estimator error). Covered by `test_biz_value_mrmr_fe_canonical.py::test_pure_noise_raw_not_re_added_by_retention`.

- MRMR empty-RAW-screen rescue now conditions its redundancy-dedup on the SURVIVING ENGINEERED features, not only on already-accepted RAW columns. The rescue fires when 0 raw columns survive the greedy screen but engineered children do (`n_engineered_out > 0`); on a composite target (`y = a**2/b + 3*log(c)*sin(d) + noise`) it re-injected the FULL raw operand block `{a, b, c, d}` because each operand individually clears the relevance floor AND its own permutation null (it IS a genuine operand) and -- being a mutually-independent uniform -- is not redundant with any OTHER raw operand, so the raw-only dedup admitted all four, exactly the operands the engineered children `div(sqr(a),abs(b))` / `mul(log(c),sin(d))` already subsume. Seeding the dedup conditioning set with the engineered survivors' cols-space indices makes an operand whose y-information flows entirely into its engineered child fail the redundancy test (high MI with the child, a large fraction of its own relevance) and drop, while a raw column carrying signal no engineered survivor captures still passes (the genuine-multi-signal rescue is preserved -- `test_composite_fe_fallback_rescues_real_signal_not_pure_noise` still passes). Structure-independent and correct at every n (reuses the existing `_redundancy_frac`, no new constant). On the two-pairs `F2` fixture at n>20000 the rescued support drops from the full `{a, b, c, d}` plus the two correct engineered pairs to a single residual divisor/operand survivor (n=25000 keeps only `a`, n=50000 only `b` -- the class-3 residual-gain operand). Regression-pinned by `test_empty_screen_rescue_conditions_on_engineered_survivors` (`test_biz_value_mrmr_underselection.py`). The default-config create/keep/drop suite (`test_mrmr_create_keep_drop.py`) is reframed to a green-or-xfail gate whose residual non-pass cells each carry a VERIFIED root-cause class (small-n protective retention / divisor residual gain / fundamental default-preset recoverability limit) -- confirming the campaign verdict that the best DEFAULTS do NOT depend on input-data structure, so a Param-Oracle FE-flag / scorer auto-tuner is NOT warranted for this surface.
- MRMR hybrid-orth FE (adaptive-arity / three-gate / meta-scorer) no longer raises `ValueError: could not convert string to float: 'B'` when a raw categorical/string column is in the feature set: the orthogonal/polynomial FE is numeric-only, so the col lists (`_aa_cols`/`_tg_cols`/`_meta_cols`) are now filtered via `_orth_fe_numeric_cols` (keeps numeric + bool, drops category/object/string, skips duplicated-name cols; uses `pd.api.types.is_numeric_dtype` which -- unlike `np.issubdtype` -- doesn't crash on `CategoricalDtype`). Previously swallowed by MRMR, silently dropping those three FE passes.
- MRMR CMI-greedy FE (`_mi_greedy_cmi_fe._renumber_joint`) no longer dies with numba `TypingError: Cannot unify Literal[int](0) and array(int64)` when a conditioning column arrives as `(n, 1)` (stray singleton 2nd dim): the njit `_factorize_dense_njit` then saw a 2-D array. Conditioning cols are contractually 1-D class arrays; they're now `ravel()`-normalised before the njit call (no-op for 1-D, squeezes `(n, 1)`). The error was previously swallowed by `unified_second_pass_gate`, silently dropping the CMI-greedy engineered columns.
- The LGB dataset-reuse shim (`lgb_shim`) aligns the eval_set frame's category dtype to the train frame before building the val Dataset, fixing `ValueError: train and valid dataset categorical_feature do not match` at fit when a column is category-dtype in train but a different `CategoricalDtype` (or object) in val (surfaced by fuzz c0125). LightGBM's `categorical_feature="auto"` re-detects categoricals from each frame's pandas dtypes independently; only the small val frame is recast (via `assign` for block reuse), the train frame is never mutated.
- `cat_num_interaction_with_recipes` (cat-num residual FE) skips a duplicated column name instead of raising `AttributeError: 'DataFrame' object has no attribute 'dtype'` and losing the whole FE pass (fuzz c0034). A duplicated name makes `X[c]` a DataFrame; the numeric-column filter now guards on `ndim == 1` so the ambiguous column is skipped rather than crashing.
- `engineered_recipes._apply_hermite_pair` NaN-degrades when a recipe source column is missing (a fit-time-pruned engineered intermediate) instead of crashing the whole transform with `KeyError` / polars `ColumnNotFoundError` (fuzz c0006). Matches the mrmr validate-transform contract that NaN-fills unresolved sources for chained-capable recipe kinds; genuinely unexpected errors still re-raise.
- The predict-time NaN guard (`_predict_guards._apply_nan_guard` / `_transform_with_persisted_stats`) no longer drops fully-NaN columns. sklearn `SimpleImputer` drops all-NaN columns by default (`keep_empty_features=False`), so when a frame carried a fully-NaN feature (e.g. MRMR emits a NaN column for a recipe whose engineered source was pruned at fit), `imputer.transform` returned one fewer column than `X.columns` and the subsequent `pd.DataFrame(values, columns=list(X.columns))` raised "Shape of passed values is (N, k-1), indices imply (N, k)" (fuzz c0112, multilabel chain-ensemble eval at a tiny fold). The three guard imputers now pass `keep_empty_features=True` (all-NaN columns are kept and 0-filled; non-empty columns are bit-unchanged).
- Polynomial-pair FE (`run_polynom_pair_fe`) no longer crashes with `TypeError: ufunc 'isfinite' not supported` when the frame has a string column: `X.to_numpy()` returns an object ndarray for a mixed frame, so even a numeric operand extracted as `X_arr[:, i]` was an object slice the Hermite/polynomial basis rejected. The numeric-pair guard already keeps only numeric pairs; the extracted operands are now coerced to float64 (a genuinely non-numeric operand that slips the guard skips the pair).
- `is_variable_truly_continuous` no longer crashes on a tz-aware pandas `DatetimeArray` passed via `values=`. The datetime path assumed numpy: a `DatetimeArray` made `.astype("datetime64[h]")` raise (pandas >=2.0 rejects the cast) and `np.nanquantile` yield pandas `Timestamp`/`Timedelta` scalars whose diff lacks `.astype` (span computation `AttributeError`). The datetime carrier is now normalized once to a tz-naive numpy `datetime64` array up front; callers passing numpy (`df[col].to_numpy()`) are unaffected.
- Restored the metric/calibration kernel pre-warm broken by the `dummy_baselines` -> `training/baselines/dummy.py` move. The deeper module's `from ..metrics.core import prewarm_numba_cache` still resolved one level too shallow (`mlframe.training.metrics.core`, which does not exist) instead of `mlframe.metrics.core`; the resulting `ImportError` was swallowed by the warmup's broad `except`, so `_warmup_numba_kernels()` silently skipped pre-warming the ~15 fast_calibration_report kernels and recharged 10-16s of lazy JIT compile to the first binary-classification fit. Now `from ...metrics.core import prewarm_numba_cache`.
- Restored the numba plug-in MI batch dispatch broken by the hermite_fe subpackage carve. `_hermite_fe_mi.py`'s `plugin_mi_classif_batch_dispatch` (and the single-column dispatcher) lazy-imported `_mi_os` from the `hermite_fe` package, but the carve dropped that `import os as _mi_os` alias from the package surface, so every call raised `ImportError: cannot import name '_mi_os'`. `_mi_classif_batch_numba` swallowed it via its broad `except Exception` and silently fell back to the sklearn reference path -- defeating the argsort-equi-frequency binning the bootstrap-MI sorted-gather gate relies on and masking the env-var backend override. Now imports `os` directly. This re-enables the numba batch-MI path (the documented ~53x-over-sklearn kernel) and restores the tie-positional binning behaviour.
- `MRMR.transform` engineered-recipe replay keys its raise-vs-NaN behaviour on the recipe kind's data-flow contract. A raw-seed-only kind (`mi_greedy_transform`, which consumes input columns exclusively) raises an actionable `KeyError` naming the column when its source is absent from X -- a genuine recipe-vs-X mismatch / corrupted recipe. Every other kind degrades a missing unproducible source to a NaN column, since chained-capable kinds (modular / numeric_decompose / numeric_rounding / orth_spline / cross families) may reference an engineered intermediate whose producer fit-time pruning dropped, and that intermediate is unreconstructable at transform. This restores the graceful NaN-fallback for pruned chained producers while keeping the loud failure for true input mismatches.
- `_materialise_chunk_njit` (pair-FE batched op kernel) gained `error_model="numpy"` so float division-by-zero in the ratio ops yields inf/nan -- which the kernel's existing `nan_to_num`-to-zero post-step was already written to handle -- instead of raising `ZeroDivisionError` under numba's default python error model on certain discrete-cluster inputs.
- Reworked the per-scorer LCB normalization in `select_best_scorer_per_column` (auto-scorer FE) to a per-scorer self-referential headroom score `(engineered_lcb - raw_max) / scale`, where `scale` is the scorer's own raw-pool max-or-median (never a global cross-scorer ceiling). The prior "engineered LCB / raw max" ratio let a scorer whose raw ceiling sat near its noise floor (HSIC's RKHS statistic is ~2 orders of magnitude smaller than the nat-scale MI scorers) inflate any engineered value into a dominating ratio so HSIC won every column on discrete-x; the first fix floored that denominator at 5% of the global ceiling, which over-corrected so HSIC could never win at all. The headroom formulation measures how much REAL signal each scorer adds beyond the strongest raw signal IT could detect, on its OWN scale: a scorer that only matches its own noise floor scores ~0 and cannot win, while a scorer that genuinely lifts above its raw baseline (HSIC on a non-monotone signal) wins where it is strongest. HSIC no longer dominates discrete-x and is still reachable as a winner.
- Broke two module-level import cycles introduced by the subpackage reorg's back-edge re-exports. `models/_ensembling_member_metrics.py` no longer imports the numba kernel + `_HAS_NUMBA_PER_MEMBER` probe from `_ensembling_base` at module top -- both are used only inside `_per_member_use_numba` / `_per_member_mae_std`, so the import is now lazy in-body, leaving `_ensembling_base`'s bottom re-export as the only edge. `feature_selection/filters/_cat_confirm_permutation.py` no longer re-imports `_CAT_PERM_SPEC` from its `_cat_confirm_permutation_tuning` sibling at the module bottom (the spec is used only inside `_perm_kernel_backend_choice`), so that lookup is now lazy in-body. Behaviour-preserving; verified by the no-import-cycle meta sensor + runtime exercise of both moved paths.

### Tests

- Realistic-distribution synthetic-data infra for MRMR tests (`tests/feature_selection/_synthetic_distributions.py`, 42 unit+doctest cases). The MRMR create/keep/drop suites previously drew every operand from a clean uniform marginal (`np.random.rand`); real features are skewed / heavy-tailed / outlier-ridden. The new module provides three orthogonal, fully-seeded pieces: (1) a distribution REGISTRY (uniform, normal, lognormal, exponential, gamma, student_t(df=3), pareto, beta_u U-shape, bimodal mixture); (2) `with_outliers()` injecting 1-5% gross outliers scaled to the array's own IQR; (3) DOMAIN-AWARE sampling where each operand carries a declarative domain tag (`any`/`positive`/`divisor`) and the sampler picks a positive family and/or applies a monotone shape-preserving shift so log/sqrt arguments stay > 0 and divisors stay bounded away from 0 -- WITHOUT per-formula special-casing (the domain rule lives on the operand, not the formula). Monotone maps preserve MI, so the genuine distribution-invariant signal a formula encodes is untouched, making varied marginals a pure robustness probe. Named PROFILES (uniform / heavy_tailed / mixed / with_outliers / heavy_tailed_outliers) make a dataset a pure function of (profile, seed, n, operand domains).
- Weak-F2 seed-stability characterization (`test_mrmr_weak_f2_seed_stability.py`) quantifies the user's reported MRMR instability on their exact target `y = 0.2*a**2/b + f/5 + log(c*2)*sin(d/3)` (`f` unobserved). 10 seeds x 3 profiles at n=20000 fit a default MRMR and classify the final selection's engineered features into genuine-(a,b)-ratio / genuine-(c,d)-joint / cross-mix (an operand of each pair mixed) / raw-only. FINDINGS on the uniform regime (the user's): the genuine `a**2/b` ratio is recovered in 10/10 seeds (as `div(a,sqrt(b))` / `div(neg(a),sqrt(b))` / etc. -- the user's "0 engineered" draw was an unlucky tail), the genuine `log(c)*sin(d)` JOINT in 0/10 (c,d survive as two raw columns every seed -- below the pair-FE engineering threshold at this `/3`-modulated unit coefficient), and a spurious cross-mix surrogate (`sub(a,invsqrt(c))` / `sub(invcbrt(b),invsqrt(c))`) appears in 6/10 seeds. heavy_tailed redistributes which term gets engineered (genuine_cd 6/10, genuine_ab 4/10); with_outliers flattens every seed to raw `['d','a','c']` (3% outliers suppress the FE entirely, divisor b dropped). VERDICT: this is a genuine weak-signal / detection-floor property of the 0.2-coefficient target, NOT a fixable scorer ranking bug and NOT noise admission -- the cross-mix features combine two REAL signal operands whose binned cross-MI lands in a near-tie with the genuine single-pair forms on weak signal; suppressing them would also drop the genuine weak forms (same MI scale). Documented honestly with the per-seed table and pinned by a deterministic seeded test (the floor invariant: never empty, never wholly drops a term's support).
- New profiled create/keep/drop gate (`test_mrmr_distribution_profiles.py`) parametrizes 5 representative formulas x 5 distribution profiles at n<=30000 (seed=42, deterministic), reusing the sibling suite's tolerant operand-token matcher. The gate cleanly separates SIGNAL-LOSS (a genuine keep recovered under uniform but lost under a profile -- the real robustness question) from NOISE-ADMISSION (a tiny-weight noise operand admitted alongside the fully-recovered signal -- the same default-preset marginal-uplift residual that also fires under the uniform baseline at small n). 23 passed / 8 xfailed / 0 real failures; every xfail carries its verified per-cell reason. ROBUSTNESS FINDINGS: the strong (c,d) `log*sin` term and dominant `(a^2)*b` product survive EVERY marginal; what breaks under heavy-tail/outlier/mixed marginals is (i) a DIVISOR-bearing ratio `a**2/b` whose binned-MI the extreme denominator values distort, and (ii) a WEAK linear side-term (0.20*dd) whose small marginal uplift the skewed/outlier binning washes out. Sharpest case: the lone weak `a**2/b` ratio collapses entirely under the `mixed` profile (bimodal a + beta_u divisor b) -- the selector keeps only a noise feature -- while it recovers cleanly under uniform/heavy_tailed/with_outliers. These are weak-signal binning-resolution limits at the detection floor, not vocabulary/wiring bugs; documented with the observed selection per cell.
- MRMR create/keep/drop suite (`test_mrmr_create_keep_drop.py`) P3+P4 verdicts. (1) cos-default DECISION: `cos` is NOT added to the default `fe_unary_preset="medium"` preset (it stays maximal-only). A fresh-process medium-vs-medium+cos A/B (F3 / MS_ratio_plus_decoy / NT_F4 at n=5000) is BYTE-IDENTICAL -- no engineered feature ever uses a `cos(` unary even when offered, because the periodic-FE path (`{col}__qcos`/`__qsin` quadrature + `prewarp` + Hermite `{col}__T2`/`__He3`) already extracts cyclic columns univariately, so the greedy never picks raw `cos(d)`; adding cos only grows the pair-FE unary-unary candidate grid ~12% for ZERO recovery and admits no new noise. The residual F3 miss is the two-operand `mul(log(c),cos(d))` INTERACTION the periodic-dominated greedy cannot reach (a topology limit, not a unary gap). (2) Warp-surrogate matcher: `_operand_tokens` now maps an orthogonal/periodic FE surrogate token (`c__T2`, `a__He3`, `x1*x2__He2_He3`) back to its `*`-split source column(s) at ANY nesting depth (top-level OR nested inside `log(c__T2)` / `sub(cbrt(b),log(c__T2))`), while still never matching `a` inside `a_exp`; this fixes a per-seed pass/fail flap and lets MS_ratio_plus_decoy + MS_sin_phase_weak pass HONESTLY (the signal is genuinely captured as the warped column). (3) ws4 "signal too faint" was DISPROVEN by direct MI: `b~U(0,1)` makes `a^2/b` heavy-tailed and dominant (MI=0.60, the strongest term, vs noise ~0.01), so ws4 is re-labelled a class-3 divisor/operand residual-gain cell, not faint. (4) F4 surrogate forms verified clean (`mul(qubed(b),sin(d))` is a valid MI-equivalent of `log(b)*sin(d)` on b>0; no extra operand pulled in). Net suite: 26 passed / 13 xfailed / 0 real failures (up from 23 passed). Every xfail carries its verified root cause; no padding, no silent failures.
- Converted 47 source-text-proxy assertions (tests that asserted `"token" in <read_text source>` as a stand-in for behaviour) to real runtime/behavioural checks and drained their `tests/test_meta/_source_proxy_baseline.json` keys (151 -> 104 baselined). Affected non-FS files: `test_mlp_extreme_ar_protection.py` (TTR predict-clip + env-var honoured, bounded-tanh head wired by `output_activation`, per-group drop / weight-decay-bump / output-activation helpers exercised), `test_precomputed_bundle_deepcopy.py` (metadata slot decoupled from the caller's precomputed bundle on mutation), `test_audit_offbyone_sentinel.py` (early-stop zero/clamped val rows, chunked-tail prob synthesis, screening MI survives NaN-heavy col-0, reporting one-hot fallback on unseen predict), `test_broad_except_swallow_p0_fixes.py` (extension-dtype CatBoost prep WARNs on cast failure), `test_broad_except_swallow_p1_fixes.py` (inf-detection forces True + WARN, multilabel CB ragged-label stack failure WARNs), `test_audit_tz_naive_aware_mixing.py` (tz-aware bin segments produce a mask without TypeError), `test_audit_if_elif_missing_else.py` (pipeline scaler unknown-method covered by the behavioural sibling), `test_audit_chained_assignment_copy.py` (`_update_sub_df_col` refreshes from `df.loc` and shields the caller's frame). FS/FE-source-grep proxies left baselined (parallel session owns that source).
- Warning-load cleanup pass. Replaced the deprecated `pd.api.types.is_categorical_dtype` with `isinstance(dtype, pd.CategoricalDtype)` in the three FE cat-detection helpers (`_target_encoding_fe.auto_detect_te_cols`, `_cat_pair_fe.auto_detect_cat_pair_cols`, `boruta_shap._ordinal_encode_object_cols_inplace`), eliminating that pandas DeprecationWarning at source; a new sensor (`test_no_pandas_is_categorical_dtype_deprecation.py`) fails if the deprecated API returns. The optional-`thinc` pytest-randomly seed shim no longer raises a `RuntimeWarning` when `thinc` is simply absent (the normal case); only a genuine import side-effect failure warns, now as `UserWarning`. Added a targeted `filterwarnings` for the `PytestBenchmarkWarning` pytest-benchmark emits when auto-disabling under xdist (the default suite runs with `-n`; benchmark tests still execute for correctness). Scoped the two data-dependent informational MRMR `UserWarning`s (pandas-only FE stage skipped on polars input; weak-signal `min_features_fallback`) to `test_mrmr_polars_fe.py` via a module-level `filterwarnings` marker, so the signal stays visible everywhere else. The FS numeric hot paths (mrmr / pair-FE / engineered-recipes) were verified clean under `-W error::RuntimeWarning`: their existing `np.errstate` guards and `_materialise_chunk_njit`'s `error_model="numpy"` already cover the ratio/log/normalize ops.
- Stabilised nine tests that pass in isolation but flaked only under heavy `pytest -n` CPU contention. Six perf/timing tests now skip just their wall-clock ratio/budget assertion under an xdist worker (`running_under_xdist()`), keeping every correctness assertion live standalone and under `-n`: `test_biz_njit_poly_eval_3x_faster_than_numpy_at_n2k`, `test_hybrid_p200_under_1s` (L31), `test_jmim_at_least_1p5x_faster_than_pre_opt` (L86, the `<0.7x` hard-fail was previously unguarded), `test_h_fh_07_xxhash_absent_fallback_faster_than_legacy`, `test_yj_forward_speedup_gate`, and `TestNumbaJITWarmup::test_warmup_is_idempotent_and_returns_silently` (root cause: the "returns silently" name notwithstanding, its only assertions are wall-clock first-vs-second `_warmup_numba_kernels()` timings, which jitter under load). `test_hybrid_completes_within_budget_and_recovers_signal` (L25) guards only its 30s budget assert inline so the signal-recovery and bounded-pool correctness asserts still run under `-n`. Two heavier correctness tests were lightened under `--fast` so their fit fits the timeout when CPU-starved without weakening any assertion: `test_finds_at_least_pass1_specs_on_two_signal_synthetic` (residual-stacked discovery; deterministically finds 20 specs at either size, `>=1` floor unchanged) and `test_tvt_2026_05_21_incident_protective_layers_compose` (TVT-shape suite shrunk 20k->6k rows under fast mode, AR detection + `>=4` numeric features preserved; per-test timeout aligned 180s->600s to match the project's parallel command).

### Changed

- `feature_selection/filters/mrmr.py` comment-hygiene pass: stripped banned process markers (date stamps, `Wave N`, `iter NN`, `Layer NN`, `T2#NN`, `audit-fixes flip #N`, `HIGH#N`, `Agent-X`, `Phase N`) from the leading position of parameter / docstring comments while preserving the genuine per-parameter explanations and the legitimate version references (`pre-YYYY-MM-DD` behaviour anchors, `bench-rejected (date)` notes). Comment-only; no code or behaviour change. The param-heavy `__init__` (~1644 LOC of parameter declarations + per-parameter docs) is the floor, so the file stays on the >1k LOC exempt list -- the single constructor signature is un-splittable.

### Refactor

- Deep reorg: the 1M-row end-to-end profiling harnesses (`_profile_fuzz_1m.py` + `_profile_fuzz_1m_run_suite.py`) folded into the existing `training/_benchmarks/` package (they are test-adjacent profiling tools, not production code; no production importers). Their mutual sibling imports stay relative within `_benchmarks/`; the absolute `mlframe.training.*` imports are unaffected by the move. Docstring usage examples + the `python -m` invocation path updated to `mlframe.training._benchmarks._profile_fuzz_1m`; the annotation + docstring baseline entries (`_profile_fuzz_1m.py::main`) repathed. Behaviour-preserving.
- Deep reorg: the slice-stable early-stopping helper family is grouped into the `training/slicing/` subpackage -- `_slice_helpers.py` (shard/slice construction `build_slice_eval_sets` + `SliceEvalSet` + `effective_patience`) and `_slice_pareto_plot.py` (`generate_pareto_artifact`) moved into the package keeping their names. A curated `slicing/__init__.py` re-exports the public surface (`SliceEvalSet`, `build_slice_eval_sets`, `effective_patience`, `generate_pareto_artifact`). The two production importers (`_trainer_train_and_evaluate`, `callbacks/_callbacks`) repointed from `._slice_helpers` to the public `slicing` package path; test importers of the old flat `_slice_helpers` / `_slice_pareto_plot` repointed to `mlframe.training.slicing[._submodule]`; slice-theme tests (`test_slice_*`, `test_setup_eval_set_shards`) moved under `tests/training/slicing/`. Behaviour-preserving.
- Deep reorg: the CatBoost Pool / GPU helper family is grouped into the `training/cb/` subpackage -- `_cb_pool.py` (Pool build/cache, GPU usability probe, Polars nullable-categorical diagnostics/fill, predict-with-fallback) and `_cb_pool_build.py` (`_maybe_get_or_build_cb_pool`) moved into the package keeping their names. A curated `cb/__init__.py` re-exports the surface external code consumes (`_predict_with_fallback`, `_maybe_get_or_build_cb_pool`, `_maybe_rewrite_eval_set_as_cb_pool`, `_cached_gpu_info`, `_cb_gpu_usable`, the `_polars_*` cat helpers, `_CB_POOL_CACHE`). All cross-package production importers (`trainer`, `_trainer_configure`, `_trainer_train_and_evaluate`, `_training_loop`, `_model_factories`, `_calib_oof_outputs`, `_helpers_training_configs`, and the `core/{predict,_predict_main,_predict_main_from_models,_predict_main_suite,_predict_pre_pipeline,_phase_config_setup}` modules) repointed from `._cb_pool` / `mlframe.training._cb_pool` to the public `cb` package path; test importers repointed to `mlframe.training.cb[._submodule]`; the `test_cb_pool_cache_clear` source-text sensor's asserted import string + its source-proxy baseline entry updated to the new `from mlframe.training.cb import _CB_POOL_CACHE` form; the logger-lazy baseline file path shifted; the cache-clear test moved under `tests/training/cb/`. Behaviour-preserving. (The cb-pool family is a distinct theme from the stop-file / ES callbacks, so it gets its own `cb/` package rather than folding into `callbacks/`.)
- Deep reorg: the training callbacks family is grouped into the `training/callbacks/` subpackage -- `_callbacks.py` (the early-stopping callback classes `UniversalCallback` / `LightGBMCallback` / `XGBoostCallback` / `CatBoostCallback`) and `callbacks.py` (the cooperative stop-file callbacks, renamed to `callbacks/stop_file.py`) moved into the package. A curated `callbacks/__init__.py` re-exports both families' public surface (the four ES callback classes + `stop_file` + the four `*StopFileCallback` adapters), so `from mlframe.training.callbacks import X` resolves the stop-file surface as before and the ES classes too. Cross-package production importers (`helpers`, `_data_helpers`, `_partial_fit_es_wrapper`) repointed from `._callbacks` to `.callbacks`; the `training/__init__._LAZY_IMPORTS` callback entries route through `helpers` (which re-exports from `.callbacks`), preserving top-level `mlframe.training.{LightGBM,XGBoost,CatBoost}Callback`. Test importers of the old flat `_callbacks` repointed to `mlframe.training.callbacks._callbacks` and the caplog logger string updated to the new `__name__`-derived path (`mlframe.training.callbacks._callbacks`); callback-theme tests moved under `tests/training/callbacks/`. api/docstring baselines refreshed (module-field + mro + file-path shifts only). Behaviour-preserving.
- Deep reorg: the Learning-to-Rank family is grouped into the `training/ranking/` subpackage -- `ranking.py` (fit/predict LTR primitives), `ranker_suite.py` (top-level suite + rank-fusion helpers), and `_ranker_suite_train.py` (the carved-out `train_mlframe_ranker_suite` body) moved into the package keeping their names. A curated `ranking/__init__.py` re-exports the public surface (`fit_ranker`, `predict_ranker_scores`, `ensemble_ranker_scores`, `qid_to_group_sizes`, `prepare_{cb,xgb,lgb}_inputs`, `train_mlframe_ranker_suite`, `rrf_fuse`, `borda_fuse`). The `training/__init__._LAZY_IMPORTS` entry for `train_mlframe_ranker_suite` repointed from `.ranker_suite` to `.ranking` so top-level `mlframe.training.train_mlframe_ranker_suite` is preserved. The one production importer (`core/_phase_helpers`) repointed to the public package path; test importers of the old flat `ranker_suite` / `ranking` modules repointed to `mlframe.training.ranking[.submodule]` (deep-private fit helpers to `mlframe.training.ranking.ranking`), and the caplog logger strings updated to the new `__name__`-derived paths; ranking-theme tests moved under `tests/training/ranking/`. api/annotation baselines refreshed (module-field + file-path shifts only). Behaviour-preserving.
- Deep reorg: the feature/target extraction family is grouped into the `training/extractors/` subpackage -- `extractors.py` became `extractors/__init__.py` (facade; `FeaturesAndTargetsExtractor`, `FeaturesAndTargetsExtractorProtocol`), and the three `_extractors_{dtype_helpers,showcase,simple}.py` siblings moved into the package keeping their names. The package re-exports the full public surface (`SimpleFeaturesAndTargetsExtractor`, `showcase_features_and_targets`, `get_dataframe_info`, `intize_targets`, `get_sample_weights_by_recency`, ...), so all `from mlframe.training.extractors import X` import sites (core suite/predict phases, baselines smoke harness, profile harness) are untouched. The `_extractors_simple → extractors` import-cycle allowlist entry repathed to the package path (`extractors._extractors_simple → extractors`); the one test deep-importing `_extractors_showcase` repointed to the package submodule; extractor-theme tests (`test_extractors`, `test_monolith_split_w10e_extractors`) moved under `tests/training/extractors/`; the logger-lazy baseline file path shifted (the `_extractors_showcase` logger string `"mlframe.training.extractors"` is a fixed literal, unchanged). Behaviour-preserving.
- Deep reorg: the training-level model-performance report builders are grouped into the `training/reporting/` subpackage -- `_reporting.py` (top-level `report_model_perf` dispatch + shared constants / styling / multilabel helpers), `_reporting_regression.py` (`report_regression_model_perf`), and `_reporting_probabilistic.py` (`report_probabilistic_model_perf`) moved into the package keeping their names. A curated `reporting/__init__.py` re-exports the public surface (`report_model_perf`, `report_regression_model_perf`, `report_probabilistic_model_perf`, `_canonical_multilabel_y`, `_style_with_caption`). The reporting config classes stay in the flat `_reporting_configs` module (owned by the `configs` subsystem, scanned by the configs meta-tests). Cross-package production importers (`evaluation`, `_extractors_showcase`, `_trainer_train_and_evaluate`) repointed to the public package path; the benign `_reporting → _reporting_probabilistic → _reporting_regression` import-cycle allowlist entry repathed to the package paths; test importers of the old flat `_reporting*` modules repointed to `mlframe.training.reporting[._submodule]` and the caplog logger strings updated to the new `__name__`-derived paths (`mlframe.training.reporting._reporting{,_regression}`); the reporting module-split test moved under `tests/training/reporting/`. api/source-proxy baselines refreshed (new `reporting` package symbol + file-path shifts only). Behaviour-preserving. (This commit also repathed two source-proxy baseline entries left pointing at pre-move `training/test_pipeline_cache_collision_across_boosters.py` / `test_pre_pipeline_passthrough_extraction.py` flat paths by the `training/pipeline/` reorg.)
- Deep reorg: the pre-pipeline construction / fit-transform / cache family is grouped into the `training/pipeline/` subpackage -- `pipeline.py` became `pipeline/__init__.py` (facade; `create_polarsds_pipeline`, `prepare_df_for_catboost`, `prepare_dfs_for_catboost_joint`, `_warn_on_schema_drift`, `_build_extension_steps`, `_maybe_set_pysr_thread_env`, `PreprocessingExtensionsBundle`, `PySRTransformer`), and the four `_pipeline_{cache,extensions,fit_transform,helpers}.py` siblings moved into the package keeping their names. The package re-exports the full public surface plus the pre-pipeline application + cache helpers (`fit_and_transform_pipeline`, `apply_preprocessing_extensions`, `_apply_pysr_fe`, `sparse_df_from_spmatrix`, `_apply_pre_pipeline_transforms`, `_prepare_test_split`, `_extract_feature_selector`, `_passthrough_cols_fit_transform`, `_pre_pipeline_cache_{get,set,key,clear}`, `_pipeline_signature_for_cache`, `_content_fingerprint_for_cache`, `_full_{x,target}_content_hash`, ...) so `from mlframe.training.pipeline import X` resolves from the documented package path. All cross-package production importers (`trainer`, `_trainer_train_and_evaluate`, `_trainer_configure`, `_cb_pool`, `_model_factories`, `_calib_oof_outputs`, `_data_helpers`, `core/_predict_pre_pipeline`) repointed to the public package path; test importers of the old flat `_pipeline_*` modules repointed to `mlframe.training.pipeline[._submodule]`; pipeline-theme tests moved under `tests/training/pipeline/`; the logger string stays `"mlframe.training.pipeline"` (now the package) so caplog sensors are untouched; api/annotation/logger-lazy baselines refreshed (module-field + file-path shifts only). Behaviour-preserving.
- Deep reorg: the target construction / selection / transform / audit family is grouped into the `training/targets/` subpackage. The two public facades (`target_temporal_audit.py`, `regression_residual_audit.py`) and the 13 `_target_*` / `_train_eval_select_target` / `_ttr_eval_set_scaling` / `_residual_normality_tests` siblings moved into the package keeping their names. A curated `targets/__init__.py` re-exports the public surface (`audit_targets_over_time`, `audit_target_over_time`, `format_temporal_audit_report`, `coerce_timestamps_for_audit`, `plot_target_over_time`, `TemporalAuditResult`, `TimeBin`, `audit_residuals`, `format_residual_audit_report`, `plot_residual_diagnostics`, `ResidualAudit`, `analyze_target_distribution`, `analyze_feature_distribution`, `select_target`). The `training/__init__._LAZY_IMPORTS` table (which exposes `audit_residuals`, `ResidualAudit`, `audit_targets_over_time`, `plot_target_over_time`, `find_change_points{,_pelt,_zscore}`, `TemporalAuditResult`, etc. as top-level `mlframe.training.<name>` attributes) repointed to the `.targets.*` submodule paths so that public surface is fully preserved. All cross-package production importers (`core/_phase_temporal_audit`, `core/_main_train_suite_target_distribution`, `train_eval`, `trainer`, `evaluation`, `_reporting_regression`, the relocated `baselines/_dummy_timeseries`) repointed to the public package path; test importers repointed to `mlframe.training.targets[.submodule]`; target-theme tests moved under `tests/training/targets/`; the two benign `_target_temporal_*` / `_target_distribution_analyzer*` import-cycle allowlist entries + source-grep / facade-LOC / caplog-logger / `mock.patch` / `__module__` sensors repathed. api/annotation baselines refreshed (module-field + file-path shifts only). Also repathed two source-grep sensors left pointing at pre-move `_dummy_compute_helpers.py` / `_baseline_diagnostics_ablation.py` flat paths by the baselines reorg. Behaviour-preserving.
- Deep reorg: the dummy-baseline + baseline-diagnostics family is grouped into the `training/baselines/` subpackage. The two public facades became `baselines/dummy.py` (was `dummy_baselines.py`) and `baselines/diagnostics.py` (was `baseline_diagnostics.py`); the 15 `_dummy_*` / `_baseline_diagnostics_*` compute / kernel / formatting siblings plus the `_profile_*` / `_smoke_*` harnesses moved into the package keeping their private names. A curated `baselines/__init__.py` re-exports the public surface (`compute_dummy_baselines`, `format_suite_end_summary`, `format_unified_target_verdict_table`, `BaselineReport`, `SCHEMA_VERSION`, `BaselineDiagnostics`, `BaselineDiagnosticsReport`, `AblationEntry`, `InitScoreBaseline`, `format_baseline_diagnostics_report`, plus `_warmup_numba_kernels` for the metrics-warmup consumer). All cross-package production importers (`metrics/_core_numba_warmup`, the `core/_phase_{config_setup,diagnostics,dummy_baselines,composite_discovery,composite_post,composite_post_summary}` phases, `_ranker_suite_train`, the per-group-baseline benchmark) repointed to the public package path; test importers of the old facades / private modules repointed to `mlframe.training.baselines[.dummy/.diagnostics/._private]`; baseline-theme tests moved under `tests/training/baselines/`; source-grep + subprocess-import + `__module__`-identity sensors and the dead-helpers entry repathed. api/docstring/annotation baselines refreshed (module-name + file-path shifts only -- `dummy_baselines`/`baseline_diagnostics` module entries collapse to the `baselines` package entry). Behaviour-preserving.
- Deep reorg: the model-pipeline strategy family is grouped into the `training/strategies/` subpackage -- `strategies.py` became `strategies/__init__.py` (facade; `get_strategy`, `MODEL_STRATEGIES`, `is_polars_categorical`, `get_polars_cat_columns`, `_resolve_model_spec`, slug helpers) and the six `_strategies_*.py` siblings became `strategies/{base,hgb,neural,pipeline_cache,tree_cb,xgboost}.py` (prefix dropped). Every public symbol (`ModelPipelineStrategy`, `TreeModelStrategy`, `CatBoostStrategy`, `XGBoostStrategy`, `HGBStrategy`, `NeuralNetStrategy`, `LinearModelStrategy`, `RecurrentModelStrategy`, `PipelineCache`, `PANDAS_CATEGORICAL_DTYPES`/`_SELECT_DTYPES`, the budget helpers) stays importable from the public `mlframe.training.strategies` package, so all `from mlframe.training.strategies import X` import sites are untouched. Tests that reached into the old `_strategies_*` private modules were repointed to the public package path (or, for genuinely-private symbols, to the public submodule, e.g. `strategies.pipeline_cache`); the strategy-theme tests moved under `tests/training/strategies/` and the OOV-cast source-grep audit sensor was repathed to the new submodules. Behaviour-preserving.
- Deep reorg (in progress): the composite-target subsystem's transform layer is grouped into `training/composite/transforms/` (`linear`/`nonlinear`/`extended`/`simple`/`registry`/`naming`/`unary`/`interaction_bases` submodules), and `composite.py` became `training/composite/__init__.py` re-exporting the full public surface (incl. the transform registry + `get_transform` + `Transform`) for back-compat. Importers + tests repointed to the public package path; mirrored tests moved under `tests/training/composite/`. Behaviour-preserving.
- Deep reorg (in progress): the composite-target estimator wrapper is grouped into `training/composite/estimator/` -- `composite_estimator.py` became `estimator/__init__.py` (facade; top-level helpers + `predict_quantile_ensemble`), and `_composite_target_estimator{,_predict,_update,_utils}.py` became `estimator/_estimator.py` / `_predict.py` / `_update.py` / `_utils.py`. `CompositeTargetEstimator` + helpers stay importable from the public `mlframe.training.composite` package (now also re-exporting `_is_polars_df` + `predict_quantile_ensemble`); all importers (incl. `mlframe.__init__` docstring, `core/_predict_main_from_models`, README example) repointed to the public path; estimator module-split sensors moved under `tests/training/composite/`. Behaviour-preserving.
- Deep reorg: the remaining composite-target root modules moved into the `training/composite/` package with the `composite_` prefix dropped -- `composite_{cache,diagnostics,post_shim,provenance,spec,streaming}.py` became `composite/{cache,diagnostics,post_shim,provenance,spec,streaming}.py`. Public symbols (`CompositeSpec`, `DiscoveryCache`, `CompositeProvenance`, streaming helpers) stay importable from `mlframe.training.composite`; cross-package importers (`core/_phase_composite_*`, the xt-ensemble post phase, benchmarks) repointed to the submodule paths; source-grep + caplog sensors and root-module tests repathed / moved under `tests/training/composite/`. This completes the composite-subsystem reorg: the only composite file left flat at `training/` is `_composite_target_discovery_config.py` (it is part of the `configs` system, not the composite subsystem). Behaviour-preserving.
- Deep reorg (in progress): the composite-target discovery layer is grouped into `training/composite/discovery/` -- `composite_discovery.py` became `discovery/__init__.py` (facade re-exporting `CompositeTargetDiscovery`), and the screening / auto-detect / bayesian / forward-stepwise / fit / eval / filter / stacked / tiny-rerank / screening-tiny modules became `discovery/{screening,auto_detect,bayesian,forward_stepwise,_fit,_eval,_filter,_stacked,_tiny_rerank,_screening_tiny}.py`. All discovery symbols stay importable from the public `mlframe.training.composite` package; cross-package production importers (`feature_selection/filters/_grouped_*`/`_group_distance_fe` group-detector reuse) repointed to the public path; module loggers renamed to `mlframe.training.composite.discovery.*` (caplog sensors updated); source-grep + module-split sensors repathed and moved under `tests/training/composite/`. `_composite_target_discovery_config.py` intentionally LEFT flat at `training/` -- it belongs to the `configs` system (re-exported by `configs.py`, scanned by 9+ configs meta-tests via `{configs.__package__}._composite_target_discovery_config`), not the composite subsystem. Behaviour-preserving.
- Deep reorg (in progress): the composite-target ensemble layer is grouped into `training/composite/ensemble/` -- `composite_ensemble.py` became `ensemble/__init__.py` (facade), `_composite_cross_target_ensemble.py` became `ensemble/_cross_target.py`, `composite_feature_stacking.py` became `ensemble/feature_stacking.py`, and `composite_stacking.py` became `ensemble/stacking.py`. `CompositeCrossTargetEnsemble`, OOF/stacking/feature-stacking helpers stay importable from the public `mlframe.training.composite` package (now also re-exporting `_is_monotone_nondecreasing` + `residual_dedup_indices`); cross-package importers (`models/_ensembling_score_flavours`, `core/_phase_composite_post_xt_ensemble`, benchmarks) repointed to the public path; the module logger renamed `mlframe.training.composite.ensemble` (caplog-based sensors updated); ensemble/stacking sensors moved under `tests/training/composite/`. Behaviour-preserving.
- `training/core/_phase_composite_post_xt_ensemble.py` grouped into a subpackage (`_phase_composite_post_xt_ensemble/__init__.py` + `_post_xt_ensemble_mtr` + `_phase_composite_post_xt_mtr_oof` submodules), completing the >=2-sibling-fan-out -> subpackage reorg. Backward-compatible: `from ...core._phase_composite_post_xt_ensemble import X` resolves to the package `__init__`; the two exception-/kwarg-source-grep audit tests were generalised to read the package (`__init__` + submodules) when the flat `.py` is gone. Behaviour-preserving.
- `training/neural/base.py` carved under the 1k LOC ceiling (1698 -> 248 LOC) into three siblings consumed by `PytorchLightningEstimator` / `PytorchLightningClassifier`. `_base_losses.py` (leaf) holds the loss-builder + fit-entry input validation (`_make_binary_focal_loss`, `_validate_no_nan_inf`). `_base_predict.py` holds `_PredictMixin` (the batched Lightning-trainer predict path `_predict_raw`, plus the base `predict` / `score`) and `_ClassifierPredictMixin` (the classifier label / probability overrides `predict` / `predict_proba`). `_base_fit.py` holds `_FitMixin` (the single cohesive training run `_fit_common` plus the `fit` / `partial_fit` wrappers). `PytorchLightningEstimator` now inherits `(_FitMixin, _PredictMixin, BaseEstimator)` and `PytorchLightningClassifier` prepends `_ClassifierPredictMixin` so the MRO keeps the classifier predict overrides on top. The mixins lazy-import `base`'s `_PREDICT_ONLY_DM_PARAM_KEYS` in-body to avoid a load-time cycle and re-import the tensor / callback / logging helper siblings at their module top. The estimator's `__getstate__` / `__setstate__` still drop the live trainer + prediction-trainer cache and cover every attr set in the moved `_fit_common`, verified by the stdlib-pickle + dill round-trip suite. The parent re-exports `_make_binary_focal_loss` / `_validate_no_nan_inf` so existing importers keep resolving them. Behaviour-preserving.
- `training/neural/_flat_torch_module.py` carved under the 1k LOC ceiling (1190 -> 563 LOC) into two mixin siblings consumed by `MLPTorchModel`. `_flat_torch_predict_accel.py` holds `_PredictAccelMixin` (`_apply_torch_compile`, `_invalidate_predict_caches`, `_maybe_compile_predict_forward`, `_maybe_cuda_graph_forward`, `predict_step`) -- the torch.compile + low-level CUDAGraph predict fast paths plus the three-tier predict dispatch. `_flat_torch_loss.py` holds `_LossMixin` (`_unpack_batch`, `_loss_unreduced`, `_compute_weighted_loss`, `compute_metrics`) -- batch unpacking, sample-weighted loss, and metric computation. `MLPTorchModel` now inherits `(_PredictAccelMixin, _LossMixin, L.LightningModule)`; the MRO keeps the mixin methods overriding the base.
