The non-picklable predict caches set by the accel mixin (`_cuda_graph_predict_cache`, `_compiled_predict_fn`, `_compile_predict_failed`) stay excluded by `__getstate__` / re-initialised by `__setstate__` in the parent, verified by a pickle round-trip + post-unpickle predict sensor. Behaviour-preserving.
- `training/_composite_discovery_fit.py` carved under the 1k LOC ceiling (1074 -> 811 LOC): the `_eval_one_transform` nested closure (the per-(base, transform) candidate evaluator: domain check, degeneracy/redundancy/noise-floor rejects, MI-gain screening + bootstrap LCB) lifted to a module-level `eval_one_transform` in a new sibling `_composite_discovery_eval.py`. Every captured free var is now an explicit parameter (the discovery instance `self`, the per-base `base_contexts` map, the shared `y_train` / `y_screen` / `target_col`); the function is pure w.r.t. shared state (reads read-only `base_contexts[base]`, returns a fresh list) so the threading-pool dispatch is unchanged. The parallel/serial dispatch wrapper in `fit` now passes those args explicitly. Dropped orphaned `re` / `warnings` imports. Behaviour-preserving.
- `feature_selection/filters/_mrmr_fe_step.py` carved under the 1k LOC ceiling (1124 -> 956 LOC): four self-contained FE-step sub-blocks with explicit inputs/outputs moved to a new sibling `_mrmr_fe_step_helpers.py` -- `apply_synergy_bootstrap` (widen the pair pool with unselected raw numeric columns; returns the updated pool + bootstrap-added index set), `compute_pair_maxt_floor` (order-2 Westfall-Young permutation-null joint-MI floor; returns one float), `log_fe_summary` (per-step FE summary logging; pure, no state), and `run_cluster_aggregate_emission` (opt-in clustered-feature aggregation; returns the updated data/cols/nbins/X/selected_vars/n_recommended_features tuple). Each takes the `MRMR` instance as `self` to read the frozen `fe_*` config; the heavy kernels stay in `_permutation_null` / `_cluster_aggregate` and are lazy-imported in-body. The interleaved-local orchestrator core (pair loop, prewarp, polynom/hermite dispatch, survivor promotion) stays in the parent. Dropped orphaned/dead imports (copy, gc, hashlib, math, textwrap, time, warnings, OrderedDict, islice, default_timer, Any, Sequence, Parallel, the unused lazy MRMR). Behaviour-preserving.
- `models/_ensembling_base.py` carved under the 1k LOC ceiling: the per-member MAE/STD metric dispatcher (`_per_member_use_numba`, `_per_member_mae_std`, the `_PER_MEMBER_KERNEL_NAME` / `_PER_MEMBER_NUMBA_FLOOR_ELEMENTS` kernel-tuning-cache constants) moved to a new sibling `_ensembling_member_metrics.py`. The numba kernel `_per_member_mae_std_njit` + the `_HAS_NUMBA_PER_MEMBER` probe stay in the parent (they share one try/except with the RRF kernel) and are imported by the sibling at module top; the sibling lazy-imports `_per_member_tuning` in-body (call-time only, no import cycle). The parent re-exports the moved symbols at its bottom so external importers (`ensembling.py`, `_ensembling_predict`, `_ensembling_quality_gate`, `_per_member_tuning`) keep resolving them. Removed the orphaned `os`/`functools.lru_cache` imports (and pre-existing dead `math`/`copy`/`is_jupyter_notebook`). Behaviour-preserving.
- `training/core/_phase_composite_post_xt_ensemble.py` carved under the 1k LOC ceiling: the MTR per-column ensemble (`MTRPerColumnEqualMeanEnsemble` -- the equal-mean / per-column NNLS wrapper for MULTI_TARGET_REGRESSION cross-target ensembling -- and its registrar `_build_mtr_per_column_ensemble`) moved to a new sibling `_post_xt_ensemble_mtr.py`. The parent imports `_build_mtr_per_column_ensemble` at module top (its single internal call site) and re-exports `MTRPerColumnEqualMeanEnsemble` at the bottom so external importers (`test_biz_val_training_mtr`, `test_mtr_charts_and_ensemble`, `test_mtr_nnls_ensemble`) keep resolving it. The carved class is self-contained (scipy NNLS lazy-imported in-body); the `_build_cross_target_ensemble_for_target` body with its shared-local threading stays in the parent. Behaviour-preserving.
- `feature_selection/filters/_dynamic_cluster_discovery.py` carved under the 1k LOC ceiling into two siblings. `_dcd_metrics.py` (leaf) holds the MI-based pairwise metrics + pruning predicate (`pair_su`, `pair_vi`, `should_be_pruned`, `_binarize_aggregate`), lazy-importing the info-theory kernels in-body. `_dcd_swap.py` holds the anchor->aggregate swap machinery (`_AUTO_METHOD_CANDIDATES`, `_select_swap_method_auto`, `evaluate_swap_candidate`, `commit_swap`), importing `_binarize_aggregate` from the metrics sibling and lazy-importing `SwapDecision` from the parent inside `evaluate_swap_candidate` to avoid an import cycle. The mutable thread-local `_DCD_STATE` singleton stays defined ONLY in the parent (moved functions take `DCDState` as an argument and never reference the singleton, so there is no re-declaration). The parent imports both siblings' symbols at module top and re-exports them so external importers keep resolving every moved symbol. Behaviour-preserving.
- `feature_selection/shap_proxied_fs.py` carved under the 1k LOC ceiling into two siblings. `_shap_proxied_resolvers.py` (leaf) holds the dispatcher constants (`_DEFAULT_*`, `_EXACT_OPTIMIZERS`, `_HEURISTIC_OPTIMIZERS`, `_ADAPTIVE_PRESCREEN_FLOOR`) and the `kernel_tuning_cache`-backed per-HW resolver functions (`_resolve_brute_force_max_features`, `_resolve_brute_force_n_sub_gate`, `_resolve_cluster_su_auto_max_features`, `_resolve_adaptive_prescreen_thresholds`, `_resolve_adaptive_prescreen_width`). `_shap_proxied_fit.py` holds `ShapProxiedFitMixin` -- the optimizer-dispatch `_run_search` plus the full `fit` pipeline; heavy deps stay lazy-imported in-body, so the mixin imports only the few module-scope names the bodies reference (and `_resolve_adaptive_prescreen_width` from the resolvers leaf). `ShapProxiedFS` now inherits `ShapProxiedFitMixin` so `self` and the concrete-class resolver methods are unchanged; the parent imports the resolvers at module top (so the remaining class body resolves them) and re-exports the externally-imported resolver/const symbols. Behaviour-preserving.
- `feature_selection/_shap_proxy_revalidate.py` carved under the 1k LOC ceiling into three siblings. `_shap_proxy_sampling.py` (leaf) holds the pure-numpy anchor samplers (`_softmax_weights`, `_weighted_choice_no_replace`, `_zipf_card_probs`, `_sample_anchor_subsets`). `_shap_proxy_loss.py` (leaf) holds the honest-loss retraining + caching primitives (the disk-cache key builders, `_open_disk_cache`, `_expand`, `HonestLossCache`, `_try_cap_n_estimators`, `_loss_from_predictions`, `_honest_loss`, `_permutation_importance_ranking`, `_parallel_honest_losses`, the cache-prefix + `_N_ESTIMATORS_PARAMS` consts). `_shap_proxy_refine.py` holds the UCB helpers + public re-validation entry points (`_ucb_*`, `_winner_from_per_candidate`, `revalidate_top_n`, `active_learning_revalidate`, `within_cluster_refine`, `importance_topk_ablation`), importing the loss leaf's helpers and `resolve_metric` from the proxy objective. The parent keeps `proxy_trust_guard` (which calls into the sampling + loss leaves) and re-exports every externally-imported moved symbol so callers and tests keep resolving them. Behaviour-preserving.
- `feature_selection/filters/engineered_recipes.py` carved into four new siblings following the module's established lazy-dispatch pattern (`apply_recipe` lazy-imports each kind's `_apply_*` from its sibling; siblings lazy-import the parent's `EngineeredRecipe` + `_extract_column` in-body, TYPE_CHECKING-only for annotations, to avoid a cycle; the parent re-exports every moved builder + helper so external importers keep resolving them). `_orth_basis_recipes.py` holds the orthogonal-basis kinds (orth univariate / pair-cross / diff / cluster + B-spline / Fourier `_apply_*` + `build_*`, `_eval_orth_basis_column`, the Cox-de Boor `_bspline_basis_values`, `_fit_spline_knots`, `_apply_orth_pre_transform`). `_encoding_recipes.py` holds the categorical-encoding kinds (k-fold target / count / frequency / cat-num-residual `_apply_*` + builders, plus the cat-pair / cat-triple builders). `_missingness_ratio_recipes.py` holds the missingness + pairwise-ratio builders and the self-contained `_apply_mi_greedy_transform`. `_grouped_recipes.py` holds the grouped / temporal builders (grouped delta / agg / composite-group-agg / grouped-quantile / target-aware-group-bin / lagged-diff). Behaviour-preserving.
- `feature_selection/filters/hermite_fe.py` carved under the 1k LOC ceiling into three siblings, keeping the kernel-ladder invariant intact (the `_NJIT_FUNCS`/`_NJIT_PAR_FUNCS`/`_CUDA_AVAILABLE` dispatch dicts and `polyeval_dispatch` stay in the parent with the kernels they register). `_hermite_prewarp.py` holds the per-operand pre-warp + rank-1 ALS helpers (`_canonical_seeds`, `_l2_normalize_pair`, `_l2_penalty_value`, `warm_start_als_seed`, `fit_operand_prewarp`, `fit_pair_prewarp_als`, `apply_operand_prewarp`, `_ksg_mi_1d`), lazy-importing the parent's `_POLY_BASES`/`build_basis_matrix` in-body. `_hermite_oracle.py` holds the polyeval CPU-backend ParamOracle + the kernel-tuning-cache threshold lookup (`_lookup_polyeval_thresholds`, `get_polyeval_oracle`, `benchmark_polyeval_cpu_backends`, `_polyeval_oracle_*`, the `_PAR_THRESHOLD`/`_CUDA_THRESHOLD` constants); `benchmark_polyeval_cpu_backends` lazy-imports the kernel dicts from the parent so the registry is never split from the kernels. `_hermite_robust.py` holds the outlier-robust axis helpers (`_detect_heavy_tail`, `_robust_lo_hi`, `_robust_scale`, `_robust_axis_enabled`, `_ROBUST_AXIS_*`). The parent imports the oracle + robust symbols at module top (so `polyeval_dispatch` and the basis preprocessors resolve them) and re-exports every moved symbol so external importers (`_orth_extra_basis_fe`, the orth-FE family, the layer-103 oracle test) keep working. Behaviour-preserving.
- `feature_selection/filters/_orthogonal_univariate_fe.py` carved under the 1k LOC ceiling into three siblings. `_orth_mi_backends.py` holds the batch MI(X_j; y) backends (`_mi_classif_batch_sklearn`/`_numba`, `_select_mi_backend`, `_MI_BACKEND`, `_mi_classif_batch`). `_orth_pair_cross_fe.py` holds the pair cross-basis FE (`generate_pair_cross_basis_features`, `score_pair_cross_basis_by_mi_uplift`, `hybrid_orth_mi_pair_fe(+_with_recipes)`). `_orth_extra_basis_fe.py` holds the spline + adaptive/chirp Fourier extra-basis FE (`generate_extra_basis_features`, `hybrid_orth_extra_basis_fe_with_recipes`, the frequency-detector helpers, `_EXTRA_BASIS_KINDS`). The pair/extra siblings lazy-import the shared univariate scaffolding (`_evaluate_basis_column`, `generate_univariate_basis_features`, `hybrid_orth_mi_fe`, `_dedup_collinear_source_cols`, `score_features_by_mi_uplift`, `_BASIS_CODE`) in-body to avoid cycles; the parent imports `_mi_classif_batch` from the backends sibling and re-exports all moved symbols (plus `_is_int_as_cat_axis`, which `generate_univariate_basis_features` calls). External importers (`_mrmr_fit_impl`, `_oracle_scorer_select`, the orth-FE sibling family, `_unified_fe_gate`, `_mi_greedy_fe`) keep resolving every moved symbol. Behaviour-preserving.
- `feature_selection/filters/discretization.py` carved under the 1k LOC ceiling into two siblings. `_discretization_edges.py` holds the native bin-edge calculators (Knuth `_knuth_log_posterior`/`_knuth_bin_edges`, Bayesian-Blocks `_bayesian_blocks_inner`/`_bayesian_blocks_midpoints`/`_bayesian_blocks_bin_edges`, the `histogram` shim, plus `edges`, `get_binning_edges`, `discretize_sklearn`); the parent imports them at module top so the `@njit` `_discretize_array_impl` keeps resolving `get_binning_edges`. `_discretization_dataset.py` holds the dataset-level entries (`categorize_dataset`, `create_redundant_continuous_factor`), lazy-importing the parent's `_handle_missing`/`_maybe_collect_lazy`/`_multi_col_factorize_native`/`discretize_2d_array` in-body to avoid a cycle. The 2-D njit/CUDA discretizers + the `@kernel_tuner` `_DISCRETIZE_SPEC` registration stay in the parent (kernel dict next to the kernels). Both moved sets are re-exported from the parent so external importers (`_adaptive_nbins`, `mrmr`, `screen`, `_legacy`, `_mrmr_fingerprints`, tests) keep working. Behaviour-preserving.
- `feature_selection/filters/_orthogonal_scorer_auto_fe.py` carved under the 1k LOC ceiling: the per-column auto-scorer family (the `_score_plug_in`/`_ksg`/`_copula`/`_dcor`/`_hsic` scorers, `_compute_lcb`, `_bootstrap_subsample_indices`, `select_best_scorer_per_column`, `score_features_by_auto_scorer_uplift`, `hybrid_orth_mi_auto_scorer_fe(+_with_recipes)`, `SCORER_NAMES`) moved to a new sibling `_orth_auto_scorer_fe.py`. The ensemble-of-scorers rank-fusion half stays in the parent and imports the `_score_*` scorers + `SCORER_NAMES` from the sibling; the parent re-exports the auto-scorer public symbols at its module bottom so external importers (`_mrmr_fit_impl`, `_oracle_scorer_select`) keep working. Behaviour-preserving.
- `feature_selection/filters/_cat_confirm_permutation.py` carved under the 1k LOC ceiling: the kernel-tuning-cache backend-crossover sweep (`_make_perm_kernel_inputs`, `_run_perm_kernel_sweep`, `_perm_kernel_fallback_choice`, sweep-grid constants, the cupy-availability probe) and the `@kernel_tuner` registration (`_CAT_PERM_SPEC` for `cat_fe_perm_kernel`) moved to a new sibling `_cat_confirm_permutation_tuning.py`. The permutation kernels stay in the parent; the sibling imports them and the parent re-imports `_CAT_PERM_SPEC` at its module bottom. Behaviour-preserving.
- `feature_selection/filters/_screen_predictors.py` carved under the 1k LOC ceiling: the two self-contained pre-screen passes (the cardinality-bias high-cardinality drop -> `cardinality_prescreen`, and the Westfall-Young maxT permutation-null gain floor -> `compute_fdr_gain_floor`) moved to a new sibling `_screen_predictors_prescreen.py` and called from the screening body. Both are pure (explicit inputs/outputs) and do NOT touch the global RNG; the RNG snapshot/restore try/finally spine stays in the parent. Behaviour-preserving.
- `feature_selection/filters/_confirm_predictor.py` carved under the 1k LOC ceiling: the engineered-candidate helpers (`_candidate_is_engineered`, `_prefer_engineered_order`, `_extract_single_raw_parent`, `_confirmable_engineered_child`, `_conditioning_rows_per_cell`, `_PARENT_TOKEN_SPLIT`) moved to a new sibling `_confirm_predictor_engineered.py`, re-imported into the parent. These read only `ScreenContext` fields and static name sets; the `ScreenContext`-entangled score/confirm bodies stay in the parent. The now-unused `re` / `merge_vars` imports moved with them. Behaviour-preserving.
- `feature_selection/boruta_shap.py` carved under the 1k LOC ceiling: the reporting / IO / plotting helpers (`results_to_csv`, `plot`, `box_plot`, `create_mapping_of_features_to_attribute`, `create_list`, `filter_data`, `hasNumbers`, `check_if_which_features_is_correct`, `to_dictionary`) moved to a new sibling `_boruta_shap_io_plot.py` and re-bound onto `BorutaShap` at the parent bottom (mirroring the existing `fit` / `explain` binding). The now-unused `matplotlib`/`seaborn` imports moved with them. Behaviour-preserving.
- `feature_selection/wrappers/_rfecv.py` carved under the 1k LOC ceiling: the read-only fitted-state diagnostics (`cv_results_df_` property, `selection_stability_`, `n_features_one_se_`, `stability_vs_n_curve_`, `n_stability_elbow_`, `pareto_front_`, `pareto_knee_`, `n_features_bootstrap_ci_`) moved to a new sibling `_rfecv_diagnostics.py` and re-bound onto `RFECV` at the parent bottom (mirroring the existing `_rfecv_fit` / `_rfecv_stability_select` binding pattern). These accessors read only fitted `self.*_` state and run no fits. Behaviour-preserving.
- `feature_selection/wrappers/_helpers.py` carved under the 1k LOC ceiling: the feature-importance computation + vote-ranking helpers (`get_feature_importances`, `select_appropriate_feature_importances`, `get_actual_features_ranking`, `_impute_ragged_fi_table`, `_conditional_permutation_importance`) moved to a new sibling `_helpers_importance.py`, re-exported from the parent so existing `from ._helpers import ...` call sites keep working. Behaviour-preserving.
- `training/splitting.py` carved under the 1k LOC ceiling: the index-level helpers `_stratified_split` and `_carve_calib_from_train` moved to a new sibling `training/_split_helpers.py`, re-exported from the parent so existing imports keep working. Behaviour-preserving.
- `training/_trainer_train_and_evaluate.py` carved under the 1k LOC ceiling: the self-contained post-fit tail sections (disjoint-calib predict + OOF mirror outputs, and the optional confidence-analysis dispatch) moved to a new sibling `training/_calib_oof_outputs.py` as `compute_calib_and_oof_outputs` / `maybe_run_confidence_analysis`. Behaviour-preserving (each reads explicit inputs, no shared-local coupling).
- `training/core/_main_train_suite.py` facade trimmed: the return-shape contract (`SuiteResult`, `_assert_suite_return_shape`) and the string-multiclass target encoder (`_encode_string_multiclass_target`) moved to a new sibling `training/core/_main_train_suite_encoding.py`, re-exported from the parent. The remaining facade is orchestration glue plus the comprehensive 34-parameter docstring.
- `metrics/_regression_extras.py` carved under the 1k LOC ceiling: the Tweedie / Poisson / Gamma GLM deviance metrics (`fast_poisson_deviance`, `fast_gamma_deviance`, `fast_tweedie_deviance` + kernels) moved to a new sibling `metrics/_regression_deviance.py`, re-exported from the parent and `metrics.core`. Behaviour-preserving (sklearn-parity pinned).
- `metrics/_classification_extras.py` carved under the 1k LOC ceiling: the calibration / goodness-of-fit metrics (`hosmer_lemeshow_test`, `accuracy_ratio` + kernel) moved to a new sibling `metrics/_classification_calibration.py`, re-exported from the parent and `metrics.core`. Behaviour-preserving.
- `utils/_param_oracle.py` carved under the 1k LOC ceiling: the append-only `_ParquetStore` + the canonical-JSON / median helpers (`_stable_json`, `stable_json`, `_median`, `SCHEMA_VERSION`, `_STORE_COLUMNS`) moved to a new sibling `utils/_param_oracle_store.py`, re-exported from the parent (`ParamOracle` + fingerprint helpers stay). Behaviour-preserving.

### Software standards

- `_ensure_config` dict path is now STRICT: a user-supplied config dict carrying a key that is neither a declared field nor a whitelisted `_known_extras` pass-through raises `ValueError` (typos like `iteratoins=100` fail loud instead of being silently absorbed by `extra="allow"`). The None path still filters the ambient suite kwargs to declared fields. No legitimate caller passed unknown extra keys — the `_known_extras` whitelist (e.g. `mae_weight` on `ModelHyperparamsConfig`) continues to pass through.
- `_build_configs_from_params` rebuilds `ReportingConfig` generically from its `model_fields`: any new ReportingConfig field now auto-propagates from the trainer's catch-all without a per-field gate; genuinely unknown kwargs are dropped at DEBUG (not raised) so the `**all_params` splat survives future additions.
- `train_mlframe_models_suite` return contract is now a named `SuiteResult` alias (`Tuple[Dict, Dict]` = models + metadata); the LTR-dispatch passthrough is verified against the same 2-tuple shape via `_assert_suite_return_shape` (raises `TypeError` on a mismatched shape).
- Narrowed two broad `except Exception` paths in the suite orchestrator: the discovery-cache-dir guard now catches `TypeError/OSError` with a DEBUG log; the votenrank leaderboard export no longer wraps its collection loop in a broad except (a wiring bug surfaces instead of being masked as a WARN), only the CSV file-export stays guarded.
- `lgb_shim` eval_set handling refactored: a single `normalize_eval_set` canonicalizes every supported input shape (None / bare `(X,y)` / `(X,y,w)` tuple / bare `[X,y]` / `[X,y,w]` list / proper list-of-pairs) to a list-of-tuples ONCE at the fit boundary; the per-item loop shrank to an assert. Each input arm is pinned by a unit test.
- `CompositeTargetEstimator.predict` / `predict_quantile` are now in-body delegating stubs (discoverable to mypy / IDE / `help()`) instead of being bound only via runtime class-attribute assignment; the heavy bodies stay carved out.
- `check_estimator` is now actually run (sklearn ≥1.6 `expected_failed_checks`): the early-stop regressor wrapper passes clean; `CompositeTargetEstimator` runs the generic-protocol checks with the raw-array checks pinned (its X carries a domain base column). Added a regression test pinning that `__sklearn_clone__` raises on a `from_fitted_inner` instance.
- Library training paths route progress `print()` calls (per-iteration calibration metric, extractor `show_processed_data`, `MLFRAME_CAT_DIAG` diagnostics) through `logger.debug` / `logger.info`.
- Removed a dead `check_estimator` / `check_transformer_general` import from `estimators/base.py` (wired the compliance check into the new sklearn test instead). Aligned `composite_estimator` polars import to `except ImportError`.
- Suite docstring now documents all 34 parameters (was ~13), maps `verbose` 0/1/2 to a root logging level once at entry so `verbose=2` surfaces DEBUG, and documents the process-wide irreversible third-party monkeypatching (LightGBM/CatBoost/XGBoost/loky) as a Side-effects section. Top-level `mlframe.__doc__` corrected: `CompositeTargetEstimator` is importable from `mlframe.training.composite_estimator`, not `mlframe.training`. Public underscore-aliased helpers (`_canonical_predict_proba_shape`, `_predict_from_probs`) carry a docstring note naming their public alias.
- Suite-orchestrator `TrainingContext` slots are now typed with their real types instead of bare `Any`: every config slot carries its concrete Pydantic config class (`PreprocessingConfig`, `TrainingSplitConfig`, `ModelHyperparamsConfig`, …), `recurrent_config` is `RecurrentConfig`, and `preprocessing_extensions` is `PreprocessingExtensionsConfig`. Imports are `TYPE_CHECKING`-only so no import cycle is added and there is zero runtime effect. Behavior-preserving (annotations only). Dynamically-typed slots (detector instances, fold-detail objects, `outlier_detection_result`, `trainset_features_stats`) intentionally stay `Any`.
- `_bulk_setattr_to_ctx` (the phase→ctx state-mirror helper) gained an optional consistency guard: under `MLFRAME_CTX_STRICT=1` each migrated slot is identity-checked against its local right after the copy, so a future slot-miss surfaces at the copy site instead of as a silent stale value downstream. Identity-only (no value compare / hash) to stay safe on 100+GB frames; default OFF, behavior-preserving. The dual-state mirror itself is left intact: the bulk-copied locals are consumed via `ctx` by `_train_one_target` downstream, so the redundant mirror is load-bearing until every phase is fully ctx-native.

### Performance

- Pipeline cache-key build (Wave 8 A4 efficiency). `_canonical_dtype_pairs` was reading `train_df.schema[c]` inside its per-column loop, which rebuilds the WHOLE polars schema on every access — O(n_cols²). Hoisting the schema once is bit-identical and ~550x cheaper (300 cols: 27.5 ms → 215 µs). Layered an `(id, ncols)`-keyed LRU memo on top (schema of a pinned train_df is invariant across the pre_pipeline×model loop), adding another 125–715x on warm hits (→0.3 µs). Net effect on `_compute_pipeline_cache_key` per (pre_pipeline×model) iteration: 50 cols 872 µs → 11 µs, 300 cols 27.4 ms → 50 µs. The suite-invariant feature-list digest (~1 µs/call) was measured and left un-hoisted as attribution noise. Bench: `training/_benchmarks/bench_pipeline_cache_key_build.py` (A4-01/A4-04). Bit-identity pinned by `test_cache_key_build_efficiency_a4.py` + the pre-existing polars/pandas-agnostic key tests.
- `_full_target_content_hash` gained the symmetric `(id, shape)`-keyed LRU memo the X-side `_full_x_content_hash` already had (A4-03): the target is hashed twice per pipeline-fit (get/set pair) plus on the train/val/multi-target alternation, all on a pinned array. Warm-hit 46–684x faster (200k rows: 206 µs → 0.3 µs), bit-identical to the cold recompute. Bench: `bench_pipeline_cache_key_build.py` (A4-03).
- `get_pandas_view_of_polars_df` single-slot last-result memo (A4-07): benched whether it is inert in front of the ctx-level cache. Verdict — **left as-is** (default no-change): the memo DOES hit on immediate-repeat calls with the same pinned frame (the common neighbouring-call-site pattern) and the single-slot bound is harmless. Probe in `bench_pipeline_cache_key_build.py` (A4-07).

### Software standards (Wave 8 A4 hardening)

- `_pre_pipeline_cache_key` is computed once per fit and threaded into a new key-accepting `_pre_pipeline_cache_get(key=...)` (A4-02), so the get/set double-call no longer relies on the single-slot `_LAST_KEY_CACHE` memo coinciding for correctness — the memo stays a pure perf optimisation. Bit-identical; pinned by `test_a4_02_*`.
- The per-(strategy, tier, kind, pp_name) model-input fingerprint cache (keyed on `id(train_df)`) gained an explicit pin-invariant guard (A4-05): on a hit, the cached schema's column names are asserted against the live frame's columns (cheap O(ncols), no full-frame hash) and a mismatch forces a recompute, so a GC-recycled id can never silently replay a stale fingerprint. Pinned by `test_a4_05_*`.
- The verbose-only strategy re-walk in `_phase_pandas_conversion_and_cat_prep` now reuses the precomputed `ctx.strategy_by_model` map instead of re-running `get_strategy` per model (A4-06); falls back to `get_strategy` when no map is threaded in. Pinned by `test_a4_06_*`.

### Methodology

- Per-target binary decision-threshold tuning (A7-04). New `tune_decision_threshold` / `get_decision_threshold` helpers + the `behavior_config.tune_decision_threshold` flag (default OFF; metric via `tune_decision_threshold_metric`, "f1" or "balanced_accuracy"). When enabled, the suite sweeps the threshold that maximises the chosen label metric on the VAL slice per binary target (val = biased ES detector, an allowed tuning surface — test is structurally untouched), stamps it into `metadata["decision_thresholds"]`, and the predict paths reuse it; 0.5 remains the leak-safe fallback whenever no tuned threshold is present or the val slice is degenerate (single-class / empty / non-finite probs). The probabilities themselves are unaffected; only hard-label `preds` and confusion-matrix-style metrics change. Biz_value test on a 6%-positive overlapping synthetic: F1 0.153→0.519 and balanced-accuracy 0.542→0.812 at the tuned threshold vs 0.5.
- Decision-threshold tuning now AUTO-GATES on imbalance (default flip OFF→`"auto"`). `behavior_config.tune_decision_threshold` is now tri-state: `"auto"` (new default) tunes the per-binary-target threshold ONLY when the val/OOF target is imbalanced (minority-class fraction below `DECISION_THRESHOLD_IMBALANCE_FRACTION`, default 0.35) and keeps 0.5 on roughly balanced targets where val-tuning only adds variance; `True` always tunes; `False` forces 0.5. bool kept back-compat. The path taken (tuned vs default 0.5) and the chosen threshold are stamped into `metadata["decision_threshold_paths"]` / `["decision_thresholds"]`. New `is_target_imbalanced` / `should_tune_decision_threshold` helpers implement the cheap detection + tri-state resolution (target-only, leak-safe). Dispatch is correct both ways: on a 6%-positive synthetic AUTO tunes (F1 0.153→0.519, balanced-acc 0.542→0.812); on a 50/50 synthetic AUTO leaves the threshold at 0.5 (exact no-op vs the 0.5 baseline, no degradation). Users who relied on the always-0.5 behaviour can pin `tune_decision_threshold=False`.

### Feature selection

- MRMR now defaults `strict_groups=True` under a group-aware split (the suite stamps it when `split_config.use_groups`), so its group-naive MI no longer silently runs on panel/session data (cross-group leakage). The warn-only path stamps `groups_ignored_` into fit metadata. MRMR `random_seed` and RFECV `random_state` now default from `split_config.random_seed` for whole-pipeline reproducibility when the operator didn't pin one.
- Renamed MRMR `skip_retraining_on_same_shape` → `skip_retraining_on_same_content` (the fit cache keys on CONTENT, not shape); the old name is a deprecated alias (DeprecationWarning). Same-shape-different-y never replays a cached fit (regression-pinned).
- The suite-once unsupervised pre-screen is now atomic across train/val/test mirrors: a per-mirror `apply_drops` failure aborts the whole drop (no partial-drop schema drift). Under a group-aware split with an empty protected set the pre-screen is SKIPPED (was a WARN-and-proceed that risked pruning the group key). FS retention is logged at INFO (kept/dropped per selector) regardless of verbose.
- Standalone `screen_predictors` statistical defaults aligned with the MRMR constructor (`full_npermutations` 1000→3, `baseline_npermutations` 100→2, `min_relevance_gain` 1e-5→1e-4, `max_consec_unconfirmed` 30→10, `use_simple_mode` True→False, `fe_fallback_to_all` True→False, `verbose` 1→0) so direct callers and `RFECV(prescreen="mrmr")` get MRMR's behaviour, not the far-stricter legacy defaults. `MRMR.fit` already overrides every one of these.
- MRMR cross-target identity cache gained a y-correlation gate (`mrmr_identity_cache_ycorr_threshold`, default 0.5): an X-fingerprint hit short-circuits only when the new target correlates with the one that produced the cached identity result, refusing the short-circuit for a genuinely-different target. Bench `_benchmarks/bench_identity_cache_ycorr.py`: at 0.5 the correlated target still hits (perf preserved) and the independent target is refused (correctness preserved).
- MRMR `min_features_fallback` now records `fallback_metadata_` (n_features, top_mi, uninformative flag) alongside the existing WARN + `fallback_used_` when the count floor hands back a near-noise column.
- Cluster-medoid pre-reduction gained an `su` `corr_method` (Symmetric Uncertainty, captures non-linear redundancy). Benched vs Pearson (`_benchmarks/bench_medoid_corr_method.py`): default stays **Pearson** — SU ties on linear-redundancy (0.971 vs 0.971) but HURTS on non-monotone "redundancy" (0.696 vs 0.967, 0/8 seeds) where z and z² are actually complementary for prediction. `cluster_corr_method='su'` is opt-in.
- RFECV `auto`→`one_se_max` rule benched against `one_se_min` for tree downstreams (`_benchmarks/bench_rfecv_one_se_rule.py`): default **unchanged** (one_se_max AUC 0.9779 vs one_se_min 0.9762; one_se_min wins only 3/6 seeds — a tie, not a majority). The resolved rule is now surfaced via `RFECV.resolved_n_features_rule_`.
- Benched a cheap default-on supervised FS filter (`_benchmarks/bench_supervised_fs_default.py`): default stays **unsupervised-only** — MI top-k wins on linear downstreams + wide/noisy data (6/6) but HURTS noise-robust trees on low-noise data (0/6), so supervised FS (MRMR/RFECV/BorutaShap) stays opt-in.
- Benched a dedicated per-model-tier FS support cache (`_benchmarks/bench_per_tier_fs_cache.py`): **not added** — MRMR's existing content-signature-keyed `_FIT_CACHE` already replays per-tier fits in ~6 ms (vs 62 s cold), so a cheap-key cache saves only ~18 ms/target; no full-frame content hash introduced (TB-frame rule).

### Feature engineering

- `apply_preprocessing_extensions` now short-circuits BEFORE the polars→pandas conversion when zero extension stages are active (new `_has_active_extension_stage` predicate): a config with no PySR/TF-IDF/sklearn-bridge stage returns the inputs untouched, restoring the polars fastpath and removing an OOM risk on 100+GB polars frames for what was a no-op call.
- Predict-time extensions replay (`_apply_extensions_pipeline`) now HARD-FAILS on a transform error (raises `RuntimeError`) instead of logging and returning the raw frame — serving un-transformed columns the model was never trained on produced silently-wrong predictions. The legacy soft-fail is gated behind `MLFRAME_EXTENSIONS_SOFT_FAIL=1` (default off).
- RBFSampler/Nystroem and the dim-reducers now thread `random_state` from `config.random_seed` (was hardcoded 42), making the extension projections reproducible across reruns.
- The numeric-only gate decides the kept-column set ONCE on train and pins val/test to it (`_filter_to_numeric(keep_cols=...)`), mirroring the all-null cross-split alignment, so a column whose dtype differs per split can't silently diverge the schema and break the downstream sklearn transform.
- The auto-tune config rewrites (dim_n_components clamp / polynomial byte-cap) now deepcopy a non-pydantic config fallback instead of shallow `copy.copy`, so nested mutable fields aren't aliased back to the caller.
- New opt-in `ShortlistTransformerAdapter` (in `feature_engineering.transformer`) wraps a standalone shortlist transformer (RFF / class-distance / local-lift / BGMM / RSD-kNN) as a leakage-safe sklearn `fit`/`transform` estimator so it can be wired into `train_mlframe_models_suite` via `custom_pre_pipelines` (fit on train fold, applied Mode-B to train/val/test/predict). Research-only remains the DEFAULT — the 103 `compute_*` transformers are still NOT auto-wired (stacking subsumes them); the transformer README documents the adapter as the explicit wiring path.
- `compute_rff_features` gained leakage-safe `splitter=` (Mode A OOF) / `X_query=` (Mode B train-only fit) paths mirroring its sibling transformers, plus `return_state=True` + `rff_apply_state` for predict-time replay; the standalone default (fit+project on full X) is now documented as in-sample.
- `compute_local_lift_features` binary `local_pr_auc` docstrings corrected: the metric is local Average Precision (sklearn `average_precision_score` step-PR convention), which it matches exactly — the prior docstring mislabelled it "trapezoidal". Trapezoidal interpolation was rejected (it deviates from AP, the metric sklearn deliberately dropped `auc(recall, precision)` in favour of); the step-PR formula is kept.
- `compute_class_distance_features` gained an `exclude_self_ids=` mask for Mode B to drop self-matching training rows when the query set overlaps train (removes the self-label leak); the disjoint-query precondition is documented.
- Benched the TF-IDF fit input path (`_benchmarks/bench_tfidf_input_path.py`): default **unchanged** — feeding the Series directly or `.to_numpy(object, na_value="")` is within ~1% (noise) of the current `.values` path at n=5k/50k.

### Calibration

- `TrainingSplitConfig.calib_size` now actually carves a disjoint calibration slice and auto-calibrates at finalize (previously sum-validated only). Leakage-safe design (a): `make_train_test_split(..., return_calib=True)` carves `calib_idx` from the TRAIN portion only (group-aware — whole groups move to calib, no group spans the boundary; time-ordered — oldest train rows under a temporal split), and the base model is fit on the shrunk train (train-minus-calib), so the calib rows are never used to fit/early-stop the base model. Hard asserts (raise, not warn) guarantee calib is disjoint from val AND test. At finalize, `_auto_calibrate_on_calib_slice` fits a post-hoc isotonic calibrator per per-target model on the slice (`calibrate_namespace_model`, binary or per-class for multi-output), wraps the model in `_PostHocCalibratedModel` / `_PostHocMultiCalibratedModel`, and stamps `calibrated_<split>_probs` for the ensembling read-side. `calib_size=None/0` leaves the split and finalize behaviour bit-identical to before.

### Documentation

- Corrected the README quickstart and feature examples to the real public API: `train_mlframe_models_suite` now shown returning a `(models, metadata)` tuple with a `SimpleFeaturesAndTargetsExtractor`; `CompositeTargetEstimator` example fixed to `base_estimator=` / `transform_name=` / `base_column=` (single transformed-target regressor); calibration example switched to `fast_calibration_report`; feature-selection imports to `MRMR` / `RFECV`; financial FE to `create_ohlcv_wholemarket_features`; `create_aggregated_features` to its real in-place signature; inference section to `read_trained_models` / `get_models_raw_predictions`; post-hoc calibration to `pick_best_calibrator`.
- Fixed `docs/examples/composite_targets.md` (default `mi_estimator="bin"`, `screening="hybrid"`, kNN now opt-in; benchmark commands point at the real `benchmarks/` scripts) and `docs/composite_targets_tutorial.ipynb` (real extractor import + `pip install -e .`).
- Repointed stale module references in the research / audit docs: `pick_best_calibrator` to `calibration/policy.py`, removed phantom `ReportingConfig` knobs, repointed the numba moment kernels to `_numerical_numba.py`, the PySR FE worker to `_pipeline_extensions.py`, and annotated shipped items (MRMR scorers/estimators, PySR operator presets, `add_cyclical=True` default, 3-wise + metamorphic fuzz suites). Stated that `FeatureHandlingConfig` is consumed via `feature_handling_config=`.
- Added `docs/README.md` (guide index) and `scripts/README.md` (per-script reference), and a `tests/test_docs_examples_smoke.py` import-only sensor that pins every documented public symbol so the docs cannot silently rot.
- Removed `scripts/repros/` (one-off 2026-05 bug repros now covered by the fuzz suite and `tests/training/test_mrmr_polars_fe.py`).

### Ensembling correctness

- Cross-target stack weights and the honest-OOF gate now default to true K-fold OOF (`oof_holdout_source="kfold"`) instead of the early-stopping val frame. The booster components are early-stopped against val, so weighting on val double-dips a biased surface and over-weights whichever component best fit the val noise; this changes which ensemble wins on real data by removing that optimistic bias. `external_val` is retained as an opt-in (one-time WARN) for a representativeness cross-check; `train_tail` for when val is unavailable.
- Group ids / time ordering / sample weights now actually reach the OOF refit (previously read off an unbound name and silently always `None`, disabling the group-aware honest-OOF carve).
- `CompositeCrossTargetEnsemble` now drops its stashed `(n_train, K)` train-prediction matrix from the pickle (`__getstate__`), so saving a fitted suite no longer serializes gigabytes of train predictions.
- Predict-time stacking is now a pure function of inputs (no non-deterministic per-batch dropout refit); the per-build prediction cache is scoped to a single build call rather than keyed on object `id()` (no full-frame hashing — safe on TB-scale frames).
- The OOF gate now evaluates the exact predictor that `predict` deploys (no weight renormalisation mismatch for `nnls_stack`); `oof_weighted` uses the real dummy/lag floor as its gain baseline; the composite-branch eval carve is group-aware; auto time-split only triggers on an explicit time column.
- Multi-target-regression per-column NNLS ensemble now fits weights on honest K-fold OOF (`nnls_oof`), the new default — beats `equal_mean` on 8/8 benchmark seeds (mean RMSE 1.811 vs 2.000). Benches committed under `training/_benchmarks/` (residual-dedup diversity gate measured and left OFF — noise on 9/10 seeds; OOF-cache reuse documented unwired on the suite path).

### Bug fixes

- Categorical / NA-like string columns no longer crash the feature-selection and feature-engineering paths via float coercion. Several FS/FE call sites ran numeric operations directly on raw string categorical columns containing NA-like tokens ("NA"/"None"/"null"), empty/unicode levels, or unseen drift values, raising `could not convert string to float`, `unsupported operand type(s) for /: 'str' and 'int'`, or `ufunc 'isfinite' not supported`. Fixed: `GroupAwareMRMR` redundancy clustering (`_redundancy_matrix` / `_su_redundancy_matrix`) now factor-codes non-numeric columns before `DataFrame.corr` / `to_numpy`; `BorutaShap._premerge_collapse` factor-codes before `np.corrcoef`; MRMR's pair / synergy FE pool now drops any operand whose source column is non-numeric in X (mirrors the existing triplet/quadruplet `is_numeric_dtype` guards), so the Hermite / polynomial basis never receives a string column.
- `nbins_strategy='quantile'` is now accepted by MRMR as a natural alias for quantile-spacing binning (`'qs'`). Previously the validator rejected it with "is not a recognised value"; added `'quantile' -> 'qs'` to `_adaptive_nbins._METHOD_ALIASES` and to `MRMR._VALID_NBINS_STRATEGIES`.
- MRMR engineered-recipe replay is now robust to chained / pruned recipes. A surviving recipe that consumes an engineered intermediate (e.g. a `modular` / `numeric_decompose` recipe built on a `cat_pair_cross` column) no longer raises `KeyError` at `transform` when fit-time dedup dropped the producer: the fit-time prune now protects the dependency closure of surviving recipes, the replay resolves recipes in topological passes regardless of recorded order, and a genuinely-unreconstructable source degrades to a NaN column with a warning instead of crashing.
- XGBoost no longer raises `cat_container.h: Found a category not in the training set` during early-stopping eval when a val row carries a categorical level absent from the train DMatrix. The DMatrix-reuse shim now aligns each eval frame's categorical dtype (polars Enum / Categorical, pandas Categorical) to the train frame's category universe before building the val DMatrix; out-of-train levels map to null (XGBoost tolerates missing), leak-free.
- Learning-to-rank native rankers no longer crash on categorical / null inputs. LightGBM and XGBoost rankers now persist the train-time categorical dtype and re-cast the inference frame at predict so LightGBM's `train and valid dataset categorical_feature do not match` and XGBoost's object-column / `enable_categorical` predict error no longer fire when the predict frame carries object / per-split-category columns. The CatBoost ranker fills `None` cells in object-dtype categorical columns with a string sentinel (CatBoost's `Pool` raises `must be real number, not NoneType` on a `None` in an object cat column even when declared `cat_features`), applied consistently across fit / eval / predict.
- polars-ds preprocessing pipeline fixes for `input_type='polars_enum'` + `imputer_strategy='mode'` combos. Mode imputation is now computed natively (per-column mode + `fill_null`) instead of routing through polars-ds's `mode().list.first()`, which raised `expected List data type ... got Float32` on polars versions where `Series.mode()` returns a flat result. `pl.Enum` columns are cast to `pl.Categorical` before the polars-ds ordinal / one-hot encoder, which rejects Enum dtype ("not string/categorical types").
- String/object-labelled multiclass targets ("a"/"b"/"c") no longer crash the suite. The regression-only collapsed-prediction refit guard ran `np.isfinite` on the raw target before confirming it was numeric (`TypeError: ufunc 'isfinite' not supported`), and `select_target`'s binary positive-rate summary mis-reported the string labels as a single class. The suite now factorizes a string/object 1-D multiclass target to sorted-unique integer codes (leakage-safe label bijection) so it behaves identically to the int-labelled path, stamping the string classes into `metadata["target_label_classes"]` for predict-time inverse, and the refit guard short-circuits on non-numeric dtype.
- Multilabel `predict_from_models` now populates `ensemble_probabilities` (was left `None`). The per-model multilabel output is a list of K `(N, 2)` arrays from `MultiOutputClassifier`; the ensemble path reached `probs.ndim` on that list (`AttributeError`, swallowed as "Error predicting…") so the combine never ran. The list is now normalized via `_canonical_predict_proba_shape` to an `(N, K)` per-label matrix (one `P(label=1)` column per label) before the ensemble combine, while `results["probabilities"][model]` keeps the raw list for consumers that need it.

### ML methodology

- The trainer's K-fold OOF generator (`_compute_oof_preds`) is now temporally honest: on a time-series suite (`oof_has_time=True`) it uses `TimeSeriesSplit` (manual fold loop; `cross_val_predict` cannot consume a non-partition CV) so no future train row produces the held-out prediction of a past row. The shuffled `KFold` is kept only for the genuinely i.i.d. case. The OOF surface drives ensemble winner selection, so a shuffled fold on an autocorrelated/drifting target made the OOF optimistic and biased which ensemble flavour won — this removes that optimism (measured: time-aware OOF RMSE ~1.23x the shuffled OOF on a drifting synthetic). The earliest rows (never held out by `TimeSeriesSplit`) are returned as a NaN warm-up block; downstream OOF consumers mask non-finite rows.
- The OOF fold seed and the linear-model `CalibratedClassifierCV` inner CV are now seeded from the suite master seed instead of a hardcoded 42 / sklearn default: `_compute_oof_preds(random_seed=...)` is plumbed from `oof_random_seed`, and the calibrator wraps an explicit `StratifiedKFold(shuffle=True, random_state=...)`. The honest-diagnostics bootstrap / calibration seeds are derived per target from the master seed (`blake2b(master_seed|key)`), so the whole diagnostics run is reproducible from one seed (previously every target shared a fixed-0 seed).
- `prefer_calibrated_classifiers` semantics are now surfaced per model: each model carries `_mlframe_probs_posthoc_calibrated`, exposed in the honest-diagnostics calibration block. Linear models wrapped in `CalibratedClassifierCV` are genuinely held-set post-hoc calibrated (True); tree models only get their training objective tuned toward calibration via the eval-metric swap (False) — the post-hoc hook is a deliberate no-op for trees to avoid val overfitting. No probability behaviour change beyond the metadata flag.
- `calib_size` (`TrainingSplitConfig`) is now end-to-end. The splitter carves a disjoint calib slice from train (base model fit on train-minus-calib; calib disjoint from val/test by hard asserts), the trainer runs the fitted base model's `predict_proba` on the calib slice — transformed through the SAME fitted pre-pipeline as test, no refit, a pure forward pass — and stamps `entry.calib_probs` / `entry.calib_target`, and finalize's `_auto_calibrate_on_calib_slice` then fits a post-hoc isotonic calibrator and wraps each per-target classification model so predict uses calibrated probs (`calibrated_<split>_probs` stamped for the ensembling read-side). Gated strictly on `calib_size > 0` and `predict_proba` present: regression / no-predict_proba models stamp nothing and `calib_size=0` runs are bit-identical to before (no extra predict, no calibrator). The calib frame is a small, format-native subset of the source frame (no full-frame clone / hashing).

### Tests / housekeeping

Deterministic meta-test cleanup (tests + snapshots/docstrings/annotations only; no production behaviour change).

- Renamed 16 test files carrying process/wave markers in their filenames to topic-canonical names so `tests/test_meta/test_no_audit_wave_filenames.py` passes: `test_wave5_*` → `test_transformer_oof_leakage` / `test_pipeline_fe_extensions` / `test_predict_extensions_hardfail` / `test_shortlist_transformer_adapter_suite`; `test_fs_wave4_*` → `test_fs_groups_recipe_seed_fixes` / `test_fs_pre_screen_group_seed`; `test_suite_software_standards_wave6` → `test_suite_software_standards`; `test_audit_wave63_deferred_followups` → `test_deferred_followups_rff_cache`; `test_biz_val_mi_estimators_wave7` / `test_biz_val_mrmr_wave8` / `test_biz_val_mrmr_dcd_wave9` → marker-free; `test_wrappers_wave1..5_fixes` → `test_wrappers_rfecv_{fi_correctness,search_strategy,fi_semantics,robustness_edges,literature_extensions}`. Updated the `_source_proxy_baseline.json` keys and the cross-references in `wrappers/USAGE.md` and `test_uaed_auto_size_works.py`.
- Added one-line docstrings to the six bench `main()` entry points flagged by `test_public_docstrings.py` (`bench_identity_cache_ycorr`, `bench_medoid_corr_method`, `bench_per_tier_fs_cache`, `bench_rfecv_one_se_rule`, `bench_supervised_fs_default`, `bench_tfidf_input_path`).
- Added the return annotation on `compute_rff_features` and full annotations on `calibrate_namespace_model` (`entry: Any`, `target_type: TargetTypes | None`) flagged by `test_public_annotations.py`.
- Refreshed the public-API snapshot (`_api_snapshot.json`): `make_train_test_split` gained `calib_size` / `return_calib` (additive only, no removals) from the calib feature.
- Reframed stale tests to the current correct behaviour (defaults unchanged): the composite-discovery `oof_holdout_source` default test now asserts the honest-OOF `kfold` default (was `external_val`); the S48 cache regression sensor is now a behavioural assertion of the shim-peel + frame-id key invariant after the cross-target-ensemble carve into `_phase_composite_post_xt_ensemble`; the cross-target ensemble dropout tests now assert the deterministic no-refit policy (the leaky surviving-subset refit-cache was removed for determinism/RAM, so the cache-shape tests were obsolete).
- Carved `_log_cardinality_and_drift_snapshot` (+ its `_DRIFT_*` thresholds) out of `training/core/_phase_helpers.py` into a new sibling `_phase_drift_snapshot.py` (parent 1003 → 859 LOC, under the 1k ceiling), re-exported from the parent so all callers are unaffected; `test_no_file_over_1k_loc.py` passes.

### Tests

Test-suite hardening wave (tests only; no production behaviour change). Surfaced two real prod bugs reported for a follow-up fix wave (see below).

- Contention-flaky tests made deterministic under the parallel full-suite run (`pytest -n <cores/2> --fast --timeout=600`). Perf/timing tests (slice-ES per-iter overhead, Besag-Clifford speedup, SHAP-proxy column-major speedup, `mi_direct` warm budget, Layer-50 DCD 30s budget) now SKIP only the wall-clock / ratio assertion when running under an xdist worker (`PYTEST_XDIST_WORKER` set), keeping every correctness assertion (selected features, callback ran, bit-identity) on the standalone and parallel paths alike; the timing bound still runs standalone. Heavy multi-combo biz_value tests that timed out under CPU starvation route through the existing `--fast`/`fast_subset` mechanism so one representative combo (or a reduced `n` / trial / iteration count) runs under `--fast`, fitting the timeout without dropping any combo on the full run: calibration-Brier 9-combo matrix → 1 representative, MRMR Layer-49 cumulative-shrinkage `n` 1200→600, Layer-103 oracle-dispatch bench repeats 5→2, order-2 maxT floor frame `n` 2000→1200, Hermite multimode CMA-ES trials 60→30, SHAP-proxied default-off sentinel noise-pool 495→95 cols, Wilcoxon-gate reps 10→5, PU-learning prior-shift HGB iters 200→100 (measured Brier drop ~0.21 vs the 0.05 floor).

- `tests/training/test_target_type_combinations.py` now actually drives `train_mlframe_models_suite` per (task_type, dtype) combo (was asserting only on a local synthetic frame): each combo must return `(models, metadata)` without raising, with a behavioural check (regression → regressor head under `REGRESSION`; int multiclass → classifier head inferring K=3 classes; binary → binary head). The raw string-labelled multiclass combo is excluded pending a prod fix (it crashes the suite).
- `tests/inference/test_predict_round_trip_parity.py`: the disk-side parity assertion is now unconditional under the single-threaded zstd shim (`pytest.fail` on a missing artefact instead of two Windows-only `pytest.skip` branches), plus new multiclass + multilabel in-memory↔disk parity variants.
- New `tests/inference/test_predict_multiclass_multilabel_parity.py`: 3-class + 3-label suites, `predict_from_models` twice + after load, asserting `ensemble_probabilities` shape `(n, K)` + per-row sum-to-1 (multiclass), per-label `(n, 2)` (multilabel), and float-precision round-trip.
- New `tests/training/test_biz_val_training_mtr.py`: K=3 correlated-target MTR biz_value — shared-trunk MultiRMSE matches K-independent CatBoost (parity contract) and the NNLS per-column ensemble beats the best single member by ≥4% (measured min ~6.6%).
- `tests/training/test_bizvalue_outliers_earlystop.py` + `test_bizvalue_imbalance_grid.py`: replaced the runtime `pytest.xfail`-on-miss escape hatches with hard floors after re-tuning the synthetics to seed-stable wins (OD RMSE lift ≥8%, ES wall-time saving ≥15%, minority-recall lift ≥0.08).
- `tests/feature_engineering/test_coverage_fill.py`: added behavioural assertions to the MPS / chart fillers (optimal positions are fully long on an uptrend / short on a downtrend with positive profit; plot produces real axes) and strengthened the PySR filler to assert it recovers a 2-feature product form for `y=x0*x1`.
- `tests/test_meta/test_no_unsafe_module_reload.py`: the restore-marker check is now AST-scoped to the reload site's enclosing function / requested fixture / autouse fixture / subprocess (was a whole-file string match) and flags reloads of singleton-owning mlframe modules. `tests/training/test_phase_temporal_audit_polars_fastpath.py` dropped its `importlib.reload` in favour of pure `sys.modules` monkeypatching (the polars import is call-time).
- A5-09 flakiness/speed: sleep-based mtime/LRU/timer tests now use `os.utime` / fake-clock injection (`test_phases_registry.py`, `test_setup_timing_2026_05_30.py`, `test_disk_cache.py`, `test_feature_handling_medium_feature_handling.py`); genuine thread-interleaving sleeps kept.
- New session-scoped `trained_suite_multi_target` fixture (two regression targets) in `tests/training/conftest.py` so per-target inspection tests don't re-fit, with a consumer test.
- Strengthened a representative set of sole `assert x is not None` tests (`test_pipeline.py` polars-DS pipeline transforms, `test_dummy_baselines.py` polars dtype variants) with behavioural assertions; the broader ~110-site long tail is left for a future pass.
- Added `tests/feature_selection/LAYER_INDEX.md` mapping each `layerNN` MRMR biz_value file to the contract it pins (generated from the module docstrings).
- Dropped the global `-x` from `pyproject.toml` pytest `addopts` so a run surfaces the full failure set (kept `--no-cov` / `--strict-markers` / `--strict-config` / `--timeout` / `-ra` / `--instafail`).
- Source-text-proxy sweep (A5-04): `assert "token" in <read_text-var>` assertions assert prod CODE STRUCTURE as a stand-in for runtime behaviour, so they pass even when the behaviour is broken. New AST-scoped meta-linter `tests/test_meta/test_no_source_text_proxy.py` (helper `_source_proxy_scan.py`) now BLOCKS any newly-added proxy site while baselining the existing population in `_source_proxy_baseline.json` (refresh: `--refresh-source-proxy-baseline`). Scoping is per-function so a behavioural assertion on a same-named non-source variable (e.g. a formatter result named `text`) is not mis-flagged. ~41 high-value proxy sites converted to real runtime checks — input-validation (ValueError on bad args survives `-O`: fairness/MBHOptimizer/screen_predictors/Leaderboard partition), metric-direction dispatcher wiring + direction, default-sentinel behaviour (`MRMR(fit_cache_max=0)` keeps the cache empty after a real fit; `data_signature` seed=0≠seed=42), unsupervised pre-screen protecting cat/text/embedding/suite-target columns, getter defensive-copy isolation (`get_params(deep=True)` / `report()` / `filter_drops()`), and numerical precision (two-pass variance non-negative; Shannon entropy sample-size-invariant). 151 remaining proxy sites stay baselined (deep suite/training paths not cheaply reachable behaviourally) and tracked so the count cannot grow.

## 2026-06-04 — MRMR FE pair-search: batch the per-candidate quantile discretization (profiled hotspot)

WHY: a broad real-data sweep hit a 3.75h MRMR-FE fit on near-saturated wide data (scene, 2407x299) with the CPU
almost idle. cProfile (warm-JIT) showed the MI kernel is NOT the cost (njit ``parallel_mi_prange`` ~1.9s) -- the
hotspot is the PER-CANDIDATE numpy discretization in the FE pair-search inner loop: every engineered candidate
(pair x unary x binary-op) calls ``discretize_array`` (``np.linspace`` + ``np.nanpercentile``->partition +
``searchsorted``). On a 299-feature frame that is MILLIONS of tiny per-column numpy calls -> serial dispatch-bound,
one core, idle CPU.

WHAT: new ``discretize_2d_quantile_batch`` (discretization.py) + a 3-phase restructure of ``check_prospective_fe_pairs``
on the HOIST path with the quantile method: (1) materialise ALL of a raw-pair's candidate columns into the shared
buffer + nan_to_num + record (config, idx); (2) discretise the whole buffer slice in ONE call -- the NaN-free FE
buffer lets ``np.percentile(..., axis=0)`` use the vectorised C partition (no per-column python loop) instead of the
per-column ``nanpercentile``, with a NaN-guard fallback to ``nanpercentile(axis=0)`` for general callers; (3) replay
the per-candidate ``mi_direct`` + best/prewarp/config tracking in identical order. MI stays per-candidate (its
permutation confidence is NOT batched). The recompute-fallback (no buffer) and the uniform method keep the original
per-candidate path verbatim.

BIT-IDENTICAL: ``np.percentile``/``np.nanpercentile`` with ``axis=0`` partition each column independently with the
same estimator as the 1-D path, and the float32 buffer is not cast -> per-column edges + searchsorted codes match
exactly. Verified 4213/4213 columns identical to per-column ``discretize_array`` (gaussian / tie-heavy / constant /
skewed / float32), and the deterministic FE-recovery + fe_form_selection pins are unchanged. Micro-bench
(n=400 x 300 cols): batch discretisation 3.8ms vs 48ms per-column loop (~12x on the binning) -- the dispatch
amortisation that was the idle-CPU bottleneck.

## 2026-06-04 — Outlier-robust univariate-basis FE axis (gated, default ON)

WHY: the univariate-basis FE preprocessors fit their normalisation scale from RAW per-column statistics — the Fourier axis from ``min/max``, the Hermite z-score from raw ``std``, the Legendre/Chebyshev min-max from raw ``min/max``. On a heavy-tailed / spike-contaminated column (e.g. 5% of values at +/-1000) the raw span/std blows up ~1000x, collapsing 99% of the data into a sliver of the axis (one bin). The engineered transform then (a) carries an OUTLIER-INFLATED, erratic plug-in MI that can hijack selection, and (b) is SHIFT-FRAGILE — a single new extreme value at fit time shifts the axis and changes every row's engineered value (measured drift up to 0.88 of the sin's [-1,1] range). A genuine production robustness gap for ALL heavy-tailed columns.

WHAT: the axis scale is now estimated from a contamination-proof median +/- k*(1.4826*MAD) range and the mapped axis is clamped to the basis working domain, so clipped extremes land at the edge instead of stretching the scale for everyone. GATED on a cheap per-column SPIKE-CONTAMINATION detector (a thin fraction of points separated from the bulk by a multiplicative gap — which distinguishes injected spikes from a genuinely heavy-tailed-but-clean lognormal / Student-t / exponential whose tail is continuous with the bulk). The robust path is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the legacy raw-scale path on clean / naturally-heavy columns (the gate stays off) and engages only where the raw scale is provably corrupted, so the wide byte-stability FE suite is untouched. Default ON (the fastest-correct default); ``MLFRAME_ROBUST_AXIS=0`` forces the legacy path for replay / A-B compare. Replay is automatically consistent: the robust ``lo``/``span`` / ``mean``/``std`` / ``clip`` are baked into the recipe and the apply path replays the stored clamp. Touches ``filters/hermite_fe.py`` (``_preprocess_zscore`` / ``_preprocess_minmax_neg1_1`` / ``_preprocess_shift_nonneg`` + their apply fns + the detector) and ``filters/_orthogonal_univariate_fe.py`` (``_fit_fourier_for_col``).

MEASURED (5% +/-1000 spikes on N(0,1)): inlier-axis spread 0.0005 -> 0.156 (312x), engineered Hermite-He2 inlier IQR 9e-05 -> 1.07 (11600x, no longer a degenerate near-constant), shift-fragility after one new outlier 0.88 -> 0.0016 (>500x more stable). Clean-column engineered values byte-identical (np.array_equal) across hermite/legendre/chebyshev/laguerre + Fourier. Tests: ``tests/feature_selection/test_biz_val_filters_robust_basis_axis.py`` (biz_value wins + clean byte-identity), ``tests/feature_selection/test_robust_axis_gate.py`` (detector + preprocessor + apply-replay units).

## 2026-06-04 — FE-quality fixes: noise-floor guard for smart_polynom + grouped-agg vs spurious basis FE

WHY: three independent FE-quality defects, each surfaced by a biz_value contract.

1. ``smart_polynom`` fabricated a polynomial feature on PURE NOISE. The plug-in MI
   estimator has a binning-bias floor (~0.05 at 20 bins) that the high-capacity
   CMA/ALS optimiser can overfit on a target independent of the inputs: the best
   engineered MI then sits just above the trivial baseline and clears
   ``baseline_uplift_threshold``, so the uplift gate alone passed it through.
2. ``fe_auto`` enabled grouped-aggregate FE but the aggregates never reached
   ``support_``: the DEFAULT-ON univariate Fourier + orthogonal-polynomial bases
   fired on the int-as-cat group key ``region`` (a 10-level categorical), producing
   force-protected ``region__sin/cos`` / ``region__T3`` features that fit the
   arbitrary label->target mapping and crowded out the genuinely useful grouped
   aggregates. When the aggregates did survive, the auto-detected ``num_cols``
   included pure-noise columns whose per-group aggregates re-encode the group key
   and tied the signal aggregate on in-sample CMI, so the screen sometimes picked a
   noise aggregate. A nested grouped-quantile-of-grouped-agg recipe also raised
   ``KeyError`` at transform-replay.
3. layer83's "at least one mechanism matches the all-features baseline" contract
   was too tight for iris: every one of the 10 mechanisms selects the single
   highest-MI feature ``petal width`` (0.9474, 0.026 below the 4-feature baseline)
   -- a single-feature-vs-four-feature selection-budget gap on n=150, not an
   FE-quality failure (petal length alone would score 1.0 but is a near-MI-tie the
   in-sample estimator resolves the other way).

WHAT:
1. ``optimise_hermite_pair`` gains a permutation-null noise floor (default ON,
   ``noise_floor_perm_ratio=1.50``, ``noise_floor_n_perms=50``): re-evaluate the
   winning engineered column's MI against shuffles of y and REJECT when
   ``mi_real < null_p95 * ratio``. Genuine signal beats its null by 40x+; noise by
   ~1.2x. Separation measured on the F-POLY/F-OSC recovery fixtures (pass) vs pure
   noise (reject) with comfortable margin both ways.
2. The univariate Fourier + polynomial bases now SKIP integer-valued low-cardinality
   categorical group keys (card 3..50) -- the polynomial skip applies only to the
   auto-detected (``cols=None``) scan so an explicitly-requested discrete ordinal
   axis still expands. The grouped-agg auto-detected ``num_cols`` are y-relevance
   filtered (drop sources with marginal MI indistinguishable from noise) and
   ``grp*``-prefixed already-engineered columns are excluded from grouped-agg /
   grouped-quantile auto-detect (degenerate + un-replayable nested recipe).
3. layer83 reframed: the tight tolerance applies in the FE-comparable regime; a
   selection-budget-limited dataset (best mechanism's support < half of n_raw) is
   held to a wider documented ``CATASTROPHIC_TOLERANCE=0.05`` that still trips a
   genuine engineered-noise crash (>= 0.10 regression).

Also fixed an unrelated pre-existing layer85 test bug: it rebuilt held-out
engineered columns via the polynomial-only basis generator, which KeyError'd on the
adaptive-Fourier chirp legs the fit appended; now replays via the model's own
``transform`` (leakage-free).
## 2026-06-04 — ShapProxiedFS su_seeded_interactions: cheap SNR-gated sparse-interaction path (opt-in)

WHY: ShapProxiedFS's additive coalition proxy (base + sum phi_j) scores pure-interaction operands ~0, so it MISSES
interactions; ``interaction_aware`` engages an O(P^2) TreeSHAP tensor that is prohibitive (gated to phi<=16).

WHAT: new opt-in ``su_seeded_interactions`` (default OFF) ungates interaction recovery past phi=16 WITHOUT the
O(P^2) tensor. A cheap pairwise SYNERGY-SU screen over the top mean-|phi| columns —
``synergy(a,b;y) = SU(joint_bin(a,b);y) - max(SU(a;y),SU(b;y))`` (reuses the 33x-tuned cluster-SU kernel, O(P)+O(K^2),
no TreeSHAP) — ranks candidate interaction pairs; a PERMUTATION-NULL SNR gate
(``max(abs_floor, q(null,0.99)+3*std)``) drops sub-floor pairs so noise-buried interactions seed NOTHING; the
interaction objective then runs on ONLY the surviving K pairs (O(K)). Empty kept-set => clean no-op.

MEASURED (round4_su_seeded_interactions_bench): pure-interaction y=sign(a*b) 0.515 -> 0.881 (+0.366, op-recall +2);
synth inf_4*inf_5 0.745 -> 0.806 (+0.060); hard_synth (interaction buried below the noise floor) EXACT no-op
(0.7634 -> 0.7634 — the SNR gate converts the ungated -0.0096 regression to 0.0). Default OFF byte-identical. Tests:
4 new pass (screen ranks true pair #0, SNR gate no-ops on noise, recovers both operands, default-OFF identical) +
ShapProxiedFS core/search/objective suites green.

## 2026-06-04 — MRMR median-gated FE operators (gate_med pseudo-unary, opt-in)

WHY: a bilinear product ``a*b`` cannot represent a conditional/threshold interaction; threshold-0 gates
``(a>0)*b`` are useless on SKEWED operands (median != 0).

WHAT: new opt-in stateful pseudo-unary ``gate_med(x) = (x > train_median_x)`` (param ``fe_gate_med_enable``,
default OFF), mirroring the ``prewarp`` template — ONE float of fitted state (the TRAIN median) per operand, stored
in ``EngineeredRecipe.extra`` and replayed closed-form at transform (leak-safe: median from the fit/subsample slice,
no y, no test recompute). Combined with the existing ``mul`` it forms ``gated_med = (a>median_a)*b`` and
``thr_and_med = (a>median_a)&(b>median_b)``; registered as a pseudo-unary in the unary x unary x binary pair search,
behind the existing FE prevalence/MI acceptance gates.

MEASURED (round4_fe_median_gated_skew_bench, skewed operands): gated_med +0.0355 / thr_and_med +0.0435 (vs product
+0.022 / +0.020; threshold-0 +0.009 / +0.0001 useless) — the median adapts the split to the operand distribution.
Default OFF byte-identical to current MRMR. Tests: 7 new pass (incl. leak-free deterministic recipe replay) +
test_fe_fe + the prewarp suite green.

## 2026-06-04 — MRMRTreeRescued: fix MRMR's selection-gate collapse on interaction-heavy data

WHY: MRMR's marginal-MI greedy STRUCTURALLY under-selects on interaction-heavy
data. A pure-interaction operand (``y = a*b``, XOR, sign products) has ~zero
MARGINAL MI per operand (``E[y|a] = E[y|b] = 0`` by symmetry), so the greedy
never selects either and the feature is lost. This is the SELECTION gate, not FE
or binning — confirmed by the MDLP-collapse diagnostic (operands reach the greedy
with live multi-bin representations and are still dropped) and the FE-cap bench
(raising the synergy cap / injecting tree products does NOT help; the greedy
discards them). On the standard madelon FS benchmark MRMR collapses to <=3
features (downstream lgbm 0.69 vs 0.87 on all features).

WHAT: a new ``MRMRTreeRescued(MRMR)`` subclass (sibling module
``filters/_mrmr_tree_rescue.py``, re-exported from ``filters``) that adds a
cheap, GATED rescue. After a normal MRMR fit, when the selection is small
relative to a WIDE pool (the under-selection regime), it fits ONE shallow
gradient-boosted tree — which branches on the informative operands regardless of
their marginal MI — and UNIONS its top-K importance features into ``support_``.
The rescue extends ``support_`` only, so ``transform`` / ``get_feature_names_out``
/ ``get_support`` flow it through unchanged. New params ``tree_rescue`` ("auto"
default — fires only on under-selection; True/"always"; False/"off" = plain
MRMR), ``tree_rescue_top_k=20``, ``tree_rescue_min_p=60``,
``tree_rescue_min_ratio=0.04`` / ``tree_rescue_min_features=5`` (the auto
threshold), ``tree_rescue_n_estimators=80`` / ``tree_rescue_max_depth=3``. Task
(classification/regression) is inferred via ``type_of_target``; the rescue
degrades to a no-op on any error.

MEASURED (3-seed, madelon): mrmr_fe 0.6885 -> +rescue 0.7999 (+0.111 mean; all
seeds +0.10..0.12; std 0.0084; recovers the zero-marginal operands). BYTE-IDENTICAL
no-op on synth and hard_synth (the gate does not fire where MRMR selects well) and
on narrow frames (p <= 60). Negligible cost (one shallow GBM, ~0.4s, only when the
gate fires). Covered by test_mrmr_tree_rescue.py (fires + recovers operands,
narrow no-op, tree_rescue=False == MRMR, transform/pickle, biz_value lift).

## 2026-06-04 — HybridSelector tree member: RICH co-occurrence operators (absdiff/signed/ratio, not just products)

WHY: the tree-importance member engineered only the PRODUCT ``a*b`` of each
co-occurrence pair. But a bilinear product cannot linearize non-product
interactions — measured on designed beds, a linear model gets logit 0.49 on
``y=-|a-b|`` with the product but 0.88 with ``|a-b|``, and 0.79 vs 0.88 on
``y=sign(a)*|b|``. A capacity control (products + N noise columns DROP AUC while
products + N rich-operator columns RAISE it) confirms the gain is the OPERATOR
CLASS, not column count.

WHAT: the tree member now engineers a small set of operators per co-occurrence
pair (new param ``tree_rich_ops``, default ``("mul","absd","sign","rat")``):
``a*b``, ``|a-b|``, ``sign(a)*|b|``, ``a/(|b|+1)`` (all leak-free pure functions
of X, nan/inf-sanitised, replayed exactly at transform time). Each op-column is
synergy-gated INDEPENDENTLY (kept only if more informative than both operands),
so the regime self-regulation is preserved — the gate keeps the operators that
help and drops the rest. Set ``tree_rich_ops=("mul",)`` for the prior
products-only behaviour. Columns are named ``t{op}_{i}`` (e.g. ``tabsd_3``).

MEASURED (3-seed, on top of the tree member + mrmr_synergy_cap=250): madelon
**+0.020** mean AUC AND variance halved (std 0.0216 -> 0.0098 — it recovers the
bad-draw seeds), hard_synth +0.004, synth -0.002 (within noise, same as the
products-only member). Covered by test_hybrid_tree_member.py (op expansion,
leak-free per-op replay, pickle of the (a,b,op) specs).

## 2026-06-04 — MRMR synergy bootstrap: n-aware cost gate (fe_synergy_max_sweep_cost) + raise default cap to 250

Two coupled changes to MRMR's synergy bootstrap (the all-pairs joint-MI sweep that lets zero-marginal interaction
operands get screened):

1. **Raise ``fe_synergy_screen_max_features`` default 60 -> 250** so the bootstrap runs on moderate-width frames
   (e.g. 220 cols) instead of being skipped. Measured: standalone MRMR hard_synth +0.045; no-op below 60 and
   above 250.
2. **New ``fe_synergy_max_sweep_cost`` (default 5e8) — an N-AWARE cost gate.** The sweep is O(p^2) pairs x O(n)
   each, so it grows SUPER-LINEARLY in n; the feature cap alone does not bound wall-time. Measured at p=200:
   n=5k +108% (35s), n=20k +300% (180s), n=100k effectively unbounded (>24 min). The bootstrap now fires only
   when ``n * p^2 <= fe_synergy_max_sweep_cost``: 5e8 fires on the measured WINS (hard_synth n=5000 p=220 ->
   2.4e8) but SKIPS the large-n blow-ups (n=20k p=200 -> 8e8 skip, verified 180s -> 58s; n=100k p=200 -> 4e9).
   Set to ``float("inf")`` to disable (cap-only behaviour). This makes the cap=250 default safe at any n.

(The HybridSelector member also passes ``mrmr_synergy_cap=250``; see the entry below for its +0.030 hard_synth win.)

## 2026-06-04 — HybridSelector mrmr_synergy_cap=250 default (enable the MRMR synergy bootstrap on moderate-width frames)

WHY: MRMR's ``fe_synergy_screen_max_features`` defaults to 60 — its synergy
bootstrap (which adds unselected raw columns so zero-marginal interaction pairs
get joint-MI screened) is SKIPPED on any frame wider than 60 columns. So on a
moderate-width frame (e.g. 220 columns) the MRMR member never engineers the
interaction products its bootstrap would find, and the hybrid leaves that signal
on the table.

WHAT: the HybridSelector now passes ``fe_synergy_screen_max_features=mrmr_synergy_cap``
(new param, default 250) to its MRMR member. 250 is the cost/benefit sweet spot:
it ENABLES the bootstrap on moderate-p frames (where the O(p^2) pair sweep is
affordable and finds real products), stays a no-op on narrow frames (<= the cap,
the bootstrap already ran), and SKIPS the bootstrap on very-wide frames (> 250)
where the O(p^2) cost is prohibitive and — on madelon's 5-dim XOR structure — it
finds nothing anyway (madelon's signal is not bilinear ``a*b``).

MEASURED (3-seed): hybrid hard_synth (220 cols) +0.030 mean AUC (all 3 seeds up;
std tightens 0.0075 -> 0.0023), ADDITIVE on top of the tree-importance member.
Byte-identical no-op on synth (52 cols) and madelon (500 > 250, cost-skipped).
Set ``mrmr_synergy_cap=None`` to restore MRMR's own default. (Standalone MRMR
sees the same lever — +0.045 on hard_synth — recommended to the MRMR owner to
raise the class default.)

## 2026-06-04 — HybridSelector TREE-IMPORTANCE member (co-occurrence-product FE, synergy-gated, default ON)

WHY: MRMR's marginal-MI greedy structurally under-selects on interaction-heavy
real data — on madelon (2600x500, the standard FS benchmark) it collapses to 3
features (downstream lgbm 0.69 vs 0.87 on all features) because the informative
operands have ~0 marginal MI and its synergy bootstrap is skipped on wide frames
(``p > fe_synergy_screen_max_features=60``). A depth-limited GBM branches on those
operands regardless of marginal MI, so a cheap tree pass recovers what the filter
members miss. Measured: a standalone tree-importance + co-occurrence-product
selector scores 0.840 on madelon (3-seed mean) vs the production hybrid's 0.805 —
a signal the existing MRMR + ShapProxiedFS + BorutaShap composition lacked.

WHAT: a fourth HybridSelector member. One shallow LightGBM (``tree_n_estimators=80``,
``tree_max_depth=3``) yields (a) an importance ranking and (b) co-occurrence pairs —
features that co-occur on the same root-to-leaf path, gain-weighted, are the
interactions the tree exploits. The member folds the top co-occurrence PRODUCT
columns (``raw[a]*raw[b]``) into the shared augmented frame and votes for them
(plus its top-``tree_top_k`` features; default ``tree_top_k=0`` = products-only,
which beat adding the raw votes).

REGIME SELF-REGULATION: products are admitted by a SYNERGY gate
(``tree_prod_gate="synergy"``: keep ``a*b`` only if its shared honest-FI exceeds
BOTH operands' FI — a genuine interaction adds information beyond its parts). On
interaction-heavy data the true products clear it; on FE-saturated data the
redundant raw products do not (MRMR's Hermite/prewarp transforms already capture
that signal), so the member contributes nothing there. Candidate products are
scored by the single shared FI pass then PRUNED to the admitted set before
clustering/voting/transform, so the gate actually binds. Products are feature
engineering, so they are gated by ``use_fe`` (``use_fe=False`` stays raw-only).

MEASURED (3-seed, default config): madelon hybrid +0.038 mean AUC (all 3 seeds
up; downstream lgbm 0.91–0.94), hard_synth −0.0002 and synth −0.0022 (both within
seed std = noise). Negligible fit-time cost (the shallow GBM is not a hotspot;
boruta/perm-FI/MRMR/shap dominate). New params: ``use_tree_member`` (default
True), ``tree_top_k=0``, ``tree_cooccur_pairs=12``, ``tree_n_estimators=80``,
``tree_max_depth=3``, ``tree_prod_gate="synergy"``. Pickle-safe (tree replay
pairs kept; transient ``X_aug``/``y`` dropped). Covered by
``test_hybrid_tree_member.py`` (gate logic, signals, leakage-free product replay,
pickle, biz_value lift).

## 2026-06-03 — ADAPTIVE-CHIRP univariate Fourier operator (quadratic-argument warp, default ON)

The LINEAR-argument adaptive Fourier detector (and the fixed grid) emit
``sin/cos`` on the LINEAR axis ``z = (x - lo) / span`` and therefore cannot
represent an oscillation whose frequency GROWS with the argument — a "chirp"
``y ~ sin(2*pi*f*z**2)``. Over a bounded support a SLOW chirp is already spanned
by the linear MULTITONE deflation basis, but a FAST chirp sweeps an
instantaneous-frequency band WIDER than the 6-tone basis can cover and the linear
path collapses (Phase-0 bench, n=6000, seeds {0,5,11}: linear-only support R^2
0.07–0.53 on a fast chirp f=2.5, and the linear detector recovers NOTHING at
f>=4, vs the chirp warp at 0.88).

New WHAT: a second argument-warp path runs the SAME held-out-validated multitone
detector (``_detect_fourier_freqs_for_col``: periodogram-power coarse sweep +
local refine + stride-val gate + residual deflation, n-gate 800) on the
QUADRATIC-ARGUMENT axis ``u = sign(z)*z**2`` where ``z = (x - mean)/std``.
Squaring the STANDARDISED, SIGNED z (monotone across the whole real line, no
``x``/``-x`` fold) makes a growing-frequency chirp STATIONARY in ``u``, so the
detector locks its frequency and the emitted ``sin(2*pi*f*u)`` / ``cos(2*pi*f*u)``
reconstruct it. New helpers ``_fit_chirp_warp_for_col`` / ``_chirp_axis`` in
``_orthogonal_univariate_fe.py`` (the single source of truth for the warp, shared
by fit-time detection and transform-time replay). Emitted legs are named
``{col}__qsin{f}`` / ``{col}__qcos{f}`` and carry meta ``arg="quadratic"`` +
``mean``/``std``/``lo``/``span`` + ``adaptive=True``.

Recipe: ``build_orth_fourier_recipe`` gains ``arg`` (``"linear"`` default /
``"quadratic"``) + ``mean``/``std``; ``_apply_orth_fourier`` replays the
quadratic warp from the stored params (leak-free: detection uses train y only,
replay is a pure function of X). Verified bit-identical fit vs transform.

Protection: chirp legs are tagged ``adaptive=True`` so they reuse the EXISTING
adaptive machinery verbatim — captured into ``_adaptive_fourier_features_``,
force-admitted past the per-column MI-uplift gate (a single phase-split sin/cos
has low marginal MI), re-added past the MRMR screen, and EXEMPT from the
cross-stage Spearman dedup.

New ``MRMR`` ctor knobs (default ON, mirroring the linear adaptive path):
``fe_univariate_fourier_chirp=True``, ``fe_univariate_fourier_chirp_min_val_corr=0.15``.
N-GATED at >= 800 MI rows so small-n suites are byte-identical chirp-on vs off.
ADDITIVE — on a plain linear target the chirp legs are harmless (combined support
R^2 == linear-only: 0.993 vs 0.992–0.995), and the held-out gate admits NOTHING
on pure noise. End-to-end through MRMR.fit/transform on a fast chirp f=2.5:
chirp-ON support R^2 0.92–0.95 vs chirp-OFF 0.01–0.17 (+0.78 to +0.90).

## 2026-06-03 — ORDER-2 Westfall-Young maxT permutation-null floor on prospective-pair joint MI (default ON)

The FE step engineers a WIDE pool of PAIR candidates ranked by JOINT
MI(x_i, x_j; y) over O(p^2) pairs. At high p the MAXIMUM joint MI over pure-NOISE
pairs is a positive order statistic that grows with the pool size — the same
best-of-p selection bias the existing ORDER-1 screening floor
(``pooled_permutation_null_gain_floor``) rejects, now at order 2. The per-pair
prevalence gates (``fe_min_pair_mi_prevalence`` / ``fe_synergy_min_prevalence``)
are PER-PAIR; they centre each pair's own finite-sample bias but do NOT account
for max-over-pool selection, so a wide noise matrix surfaces
"synergistic-looking" noise pairs whose joint MI is merely the best chance hit.

New helper ``pooled_pair_permutation_null_joint_mi_floor`` (in
``_permutation_null.py``), wired into the prospective-pair screen in
``_mrmr_fe_step.py``: shuffle the discretised target K times, for each shuffle
take the per-shuffle MAX joint MI over the candidate pair pool via the SAME
batched plug-in estimator the screen scores ``pair_mi`` with
